(Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., fin. and municipal depts., Jan., 1894; transfed. to gen., &c., depts., April, 1895; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1896.

BONUS, ARTHUR RIVERS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 17th Jan., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; transfed. to Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1889; retransfed., March, 1892.

BONUS, JOSEPH, Major-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay engrs., June, 1855; arrived, March, 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); joined the dept., June, 1859, and was employed on the Bombay harbour defences, Oct., 1860; from Sept., 1861, served as dep. consulting engr. for railways in Sind; offg. dep. consulting engr., N. W. Prov., March, 1870; from Nov., 1871, served as suptg. engr. on Indus valley state railway; engr.-in-chief, Sindia state railway, Sept., 1876; transfed. to Punjab N. state railway, May, 1877; offg. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lahore, July-Oct., 1878; engr.-in-chief, Pindi-Peshawar section, Oct., 1879; medal for services in the Afghan war,

1879-80; acting consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec. to govt., Bombay, 1882 and 1885-86; senr. dep. consulting engr. and under sec. to govt., railway branch, from Nov., 1883; chief engr., Dec., 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1888.

BOOTH, WILLIAM, B.A., Bengal Educ. Dept.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; served from 27th Nov., 1876, as prof'r. in the presidency coll.; principal, Dacca coll., June, 1884; prof'r. of physics, presidency coll., Nov., 1890; principal, Hugli coll., Dec., 1892.

BOOTY, PERCY ABBEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Trin. Coll. Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 16th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BOSANQUET, OSWALD VIVIAN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 17th Sept., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to rest. at Haidarabad, April, 1892; first asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Oct., 1893; acting under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., May, 1895.

BOSE, BHOOBUN MOHUN, RAI BAHADUR. India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. from Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll., Sept., 1867, as apprentice engineer, and posted to Oudh; asst. engr., Sept., 1868; transf'd. to State Rlwys., Dec., 1869; exec. engr., Oct., 1886.

BOUGHEY, GEORGE FLETCHER OTTLEY, C.S.I., Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1862; arrived, 5th Jan., 1865; served in the Bhotan expedn., 1865-66 (medal and clasp); joined the dept., Nov., 1869, as asst. engr., railway branch, N.W. Prov.; exec. engr., Rajputana railway, Sept., 1870; dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, May, 1877; asst. sec., railway branch, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1879; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); manager, Indus valley and Kandahar railway, Jan., 1882; manager, E. Bengal state railway, Nov., 1885; manager, N.W. railway, Oct., 1892; C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

BOULNOIS, CHARLES, B.A., LL.B., B.L., late Punjab Chief Court.—Educ. at King's Coll., London, and London Univ.; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 30th April, 1855, and in the same year was admitted advocate of the Calcutta supreme court; prof'r. of law in the presidency coll., Calcutta, 1857-61 and 1882-83; judge of the court of small causes, Bengal, 1860; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, in the legisl. dept., 1862-64; offg. dep. sec. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept., 1864; apptd. judge of the chief court, Punjab, in Feb., 1866, and was senr. judge from 1867 until his retirement in 1877; from 1881 has acted as reporter of appeal cases; fellow of the Punjab univ., 1882; editor of law reports in the supreme court, 1856-60; joint author of 'Notes on Customary Law,' by Boulnois and Rattigan, 1876.

BOURDILLON, JAMES AUSTIN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870; asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, Dec., 1870; offg. under sec. and govt. of India, finl. dept., Sept., 1874, and in the home dept., March, 1876; inspr. of registrn. offices, Aug. 1876; dep. supt. of census, Dec., 1880; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Dec., 1881; mag. and collr., April, 1887; offg. sec., finl. and municipal depts., 1892 and 1893;

member of Bengal legisl. council, 1893 and 1895; acting commr., Bardwan, April, 1895.

BOUSTEAD, ROBINSON, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Major, late Bombay Med. Dept.—Civil surgn., Thana, 21st Jan., 1876; acted as supt. of vaccn., 1876; supt. of Matheran, Feb., 1882; acted as surgn., Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., 1883; civil surgn., Ahmadnagar, July, 1887; ret'd., Feb., 1889.

BOUTFLOWER, WILLIAM NOLAN, B.A., Educ. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the dept., 9th Aug., 1869, as prof'r. in the Agra coll.; prof'r. in Muir coll., Allahabad, Oct., 1874; offg. meteorological reporter to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April-June, 1881; inspr. of schools, Feb., 1892; offd. as dir. of public instrn. from April, 1896; author of 'Elementary Statics and Dynamics,' and 'The Indian Arithmetic.'

BOVILL, EDWARD, M.B., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1872, and served on gen., jail, and hosp. duty, and as civil surgn., at various stations, from Dec., 1885.

BOWDEN, WILLIAM JAMES, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893; clerk, 2nd class, 28th Aug., 1895.

BOWEN, CHARLES, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn., Dec., 1885; arrived, April, 1863; joined the dept., Nov., 1863, and, from June, 1864, served under the Mysore govt.; exec. engr., March, 1867; asst. to chief engr., and sec. to chief commr., Mysore, June, 1869; offg. engr.-in-chief and joint sec. to govt., 1880; served in Madras, April, 1883, to Sept., 1885, as dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt.; served again under the Mysore govt. from Sept., 1885, to July, 1892; suptg. engr., April, 1886; ret'd., Feb., 1894.

BOWER, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Caistor Gr. Sch., Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Dec., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and offg. under sec. to govt.; joint mag., March, 1890; dep. supt., family domains of the Maharaja of Benares, April, 1890, to April, 1893; dep. commr., April, 1896.

BOWIE, MATHEW MORTON, Col., I.S.C., Arrived, 20th June, 1858; from June, 1863, served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and dep. commr., and was placed on special duty in connection with the Delhi assemblage, Nov., 1876; commr. of excise, &c., June, 1880; dep. commr., Nagpur, Sept., 1880; inspr.-gen. of police and prisons, Central Prov., April, 1882; commr., Nerbudda divn., April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1896.

BOWRING, LEWIN BENTHAM, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Mount Radford Sch., Exeter, and at Leipzig; apptd. from Haileybury, 1843; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, 1845; asst. to treasurer, Calcutta, 1846; asst. to rest. at Lahore, 1847; dep. commr., Punjab, 1849-54; private sec. to Lord Canning, 1858; chief commr. of Mysore and Coorg, 1862; ret'd., 1870; has publ. 'Eastern Experiences,' 'Haidar Ali and Tipu Sultan,' &c.

BOYD, CHARLES CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BOYD, HENRY WALKER BUTLER, F.R.C.S.I.,

surgn.-lieut.-col., I.M.S., Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1876; served as civil surgeon at various stations; supt. of lunatic asylum, Colaba, June, 1890.

BOYD, JOHN EWART EDWARDS AGINCOURT, Madras Police Dept.—Joined the service, Nov., 1869, and served in the police dept. as inspr., asst. supt., and supt.

BOYS, HENRY SCOTT, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863; from Dec., 1864, served in Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer, and was apptd. supt. of the encumbered estates of Sitapur and Kheri, June, 1872; dep. commr., Dec., 1881; mag. and collr., Nov., 1884; acted as commr. in 1883, 1837, and 1888; ret'd., 1889.

BRACKENBURY, SIR HENRY, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Lieut.-Gen., R.A.—First commn., 7th 1856; served in the Mutiny, 1858, the Ashanti expedn., 1873, and the S. African war, 1879-80; asst. adjutant and quarter-master-gen., Cyprus, July, 1878; mil. sec., S. Africa, May, 1879; private sec. to viceroy of India, March to June, 1880; mil. attaché, Paris, Jan., 1881; dep. adjutant and quartermaster-gen., Egypt, Sept., 1884; brigadier-gen., Egypt, Jan., 1885; dep. quartermaster-gen. at head-quarters (intelligence branch), Jan., 1886; dep. adjutant-gen., and director of mil. intelligence, June, 1887, to March, 1891; mil. member of viceroy's council, April, 1891, to April, 1896; prest. ordnance commn., War Office, May, 1896; K.C.B., May, 1894; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

BRACKENBURY, MAULE CAMPBELL, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1865; arrived and joined the dept., June, 1875; exec. engr., March, 1877; served in the Perak expedn., 1875-76 (medal and clasp), and the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and two clasps and bronze star); offg. dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lahore, 1881; asst. manager, Punjab N. state railway, Feb., 1883; offg. manager and traffic supt., Mysore railway, April, 1883; acting dep. consulting engr., Bombay, Oct., 1884; dep. manager, N.W. state railway, Sind section, Dec., 1885; manager, Sind section, March, 1887; under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Nov., 1887, and dep. dir.-gen. of railways, April, 1888, to July, 1890; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, May, 1892.

BRADBURY, JAMES FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 16th Oct., 1868; served in rev. and jud. dept., Bengal, and was apptd. joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1878; also acted as pol. agent, Hill Tippera, and junr. sec., board of rev.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1885.

BRADTON, JOHN BLOOMFIELD, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 12th Sept., 1871, and was employed on various public works and railway accts.; examr., Feb., 1884; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, March, 1895; govt. examr. of accts., Bengal and N.W. railway, Nov., 1895.

BRADFORD, SIR EDWARD RIDLEY COLBORNE, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Col., I.S.C.—Cornet, 6th light cavalry, Madras, Feb., 1854; proceeded to Persia under Gen. Jacob in 1856; pol. asst. in W. Malwa, and in command of 1st regt.

Central India horse, Nov., 1860; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, 1871; pol. agent, Bhartpur, Jan., 1873; gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, May, 1874; agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Dec., 1878; also chief commr. of Ajmir; sec., pol. and secret dept., India office, Feb., 1887; accompanied H.R.H. Prince Albert Victor on his visit to India in 1889-90; K.C.B., May, 1890; resigned on his appt. as chief commr., metropolitan police, London, June, 1890.

BRADLEY, HERBERT, B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and sec. to the commr. of salt, abkari and separate rev., April, 1888; collr. and mag., July, 1894.

BRADLEY, JOSEPH ADNUM, Legal Adviser's Dept., India Office.—Clerk to the legal adviser to the sec. of state, 25th June, 1877.

BRADLEY, RICHARD, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., 1st Nov., 1868, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; ret'd., 1896.

BRANDIS, SIR DIETRICH, K.C.I.E., Ph.D.—Educ. at Univs. of Copenhagen, Gottingen, and Bonn; lecturer on botany at Univ. of Bonn, 1849; served in the India forest dept. from 16th Jan., 1856, and was apptd. inspr.-gen. of forests, April, 1864; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; ret'd., 1883; apptd. a member of the board of visitors of Cooper's Hill Coll., 1886; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; author of 'Forest Flora of N.W. and Central India,' 1874, of the article on 'Teak' in the Encyclopædia Britannica, &c., &c.; is dir. of the practical course of forestry on the Continent in connection with Cooper's Hill Coll.

BRANDRETH, ASHTON MILNES, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—First commn., Dec., 1855; arrived, 30th Dec., 1857; served in the public works dept. from 8th Jan., 1858, as asst. engr., asst. principal, Thomason coll., and exec. engr.; transfld. to the Punjab, Sept., 1861, and served on the W. Jumna canal; asst. sec., govt. of India, May to Dec., 1863; asst. sec., public works dept., Punjab, May, 1867; principal, Thomason coll., 1879-91.

BRANDT, FRANCIS, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., under sec. to govt., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, April, 1874; collr. and mag., S. Kanara, Oct., 1877, and in the Nilgiris, 1883; district and sessions judge, Trichinopoli, June, 1878; judge of the high court, Madras, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1887.

BRANFOOT, ARTHUR MUDGE, M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Brig.-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 30th March, 1872; arrived, 28th Dec., 1872, and served under the mil. dept. till March, 1873, and subsequently as civil surgn. at Coconada and Madras, rest. surgn. at the gen. hosp., Madras, and profr. in the med. coll.; asst. physician, gen. hosp., and profr. of hygiene, med. coll., July, 1878; supt. of the lying-in hosp., and profr. of midwifery, med. coll., July, 1881; fellow of Madras univ., 1886.

BRANSON, JAMES CHARLES EVERTON, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 16th Jan., 1882, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; transf. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Dec., 1885; offg. asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, March, 1886; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1889; acting acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1892; returned to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896.

BRENDON, BENJAMIN ADAMS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. on the Continent and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 6th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BRERETON, ALFRED, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., March, 1873, and served in the railway branch; exec. engr., April, 1880; asst. dir., N.W. railway, Nov., 1887; district traffic supt., Nov., 1888; dep. manager, July, 1891; offg. manager, E. Bengal railway, March, 1893; manager and engr.-in-chief, E. Coast railway, Nov., 1893.

BRERETON, ASHLEY FRANCIS, Rev. and Statistics Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Bedford Sch.; supernumerary writer, 13th Feb., 1860; temporary clerk, Dec., 1860; clerk in registry dept., Jan., 1869; junr. clerk, finl. dept., April, 1874; senr. clerk, Nov., 1883; transf. to rev. and statistics dept., 1886.

BRERETON, HERBERT JAMES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch.; joined the service, 7th April, 1873; from Feb., 1874, served in the office of the acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1877; N.W. Prov., Nov., 1877; Burma, April, 1878; Punjab, Aug., 1879; N.W. Prov., March, 1884; Central Prov., March, 1888; N.W. Prov., July, 1888; comptr., Assam, June, 1890; asst. compt.-gen., India (in charge of papercurrency), July, 1893; dept. acctnt.-gen., March, 1896.

BRERETON, REGINALD HUGH, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1889; asst. commr., Oudh, Nov., 1891; joint mag., 1st grade, June, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., April, 1896.

BRETT, ALFRED CORBYN, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Bengal rev. and jud. dept.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1874; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1878; ret'd., Aug., 1894.

BRETT, CECIL MICHAEL WILFORD, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1885; registrar, high court (appellate side), April, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1890; offg. jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, April-Sept., 1891.

BRIDGES, ALBERT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 29th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., &c.

BRIDGES, JAMES EMILE, B.L., Indian C.S.

(Burma).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Tournai, and Univ. of Bonn; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 16th Nov., 1876, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; offg. sec. to chief commr., land rev. and agricl. dept., and offg. dir. of agric., Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Aug., 1886, and Dec., 1888; offg. chief sec., March-July, 1890; commr., March, 1891.

BRIGGS, W. H., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Army Med. Staff.—First commn., 30th Sept., 1875; served in the Jowaki campaign, 1877-78 (medal and clasp), the Afghan campaigns, 1878-80 (medal), and the Nile campaign, 1884-85 (medal and clasps and Khedive's star); med. officer on personal staff of govr. of Madras, Dec., 1886 to March, 1889; ret'd. from the army, Dec., 1893.

BRIGHT, WILLIAM ROBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1890; dep. commr., Palamau, March, 1892; mag. of Midnapur, Dec., 1895.

BRIGSTOCKE, ARTHUR MONTAGUE, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Heidelberg Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Dec., 1892, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

BRIND, MICHAEL JOSEPH, India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 9th Nov., 1870; served in the construction of several lines in the Punjab, Assam, &c.; supt., April, 1891.

BROADBENT, JOHN EDWARD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Mil. Works Dept.—First commn., 17th July, 1866; arrived, Dec., 1868, and served as asst. engr.; exec. eng., June, 1879; asst. sec., mil. dept., Dec., 1882; second dep. sec., March, 1890; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works for fortifications, April, 1892; transf. to Bombay command as chief engr., April, 1894; transf. to Punjab, March, 1895.

BROADFOOT, RAMSAY DOUGLAS, B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Kensington Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1888; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1893; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1896.

BROADWAY, PHILIP NEVILLE, Police Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1861, as asst. district supt. of police; district supt., April, 1873.

BRODIE, NORMAN SOMERVILLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1834; arrived, 12th Dec., 1837, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting asst. sec. to govt.; head asst., Malabar, Feb., 1893; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., April, 1896.

BRODIE, VERNOR ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settl. officer; senr. asst., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1891; district and sessions judge, March, 1894; collr. and mag., March, 1895.

BRODIE, WILLIAM PATRICK, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1872, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., July, 1881.

BJOJENDRA COOMAR SEAL, B.L., Sta-

tutory C.S. (Bengal).—From 23rd Oct., 1865, served in the subord. jud. dept., Bengal, and was appointed subord. judge, 4th grade, May, 1873, and district judge, Bankura, and asst. sessions judge, Bardwan, April, 1881; district and sessions judge, June, 1887.

BROJENDRA NATH DE, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Hare Sch., Calcutta, Canning Coll., Lucknow, Calcutta Univ., and St. Mary's Hall, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 6th Sept., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1886; mag. and collr., Aug., 1891.

BROOK-FOX, FREDERICK GEORGE, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Sept., 1873, and employed on mil. works in the Punjab; transf. temporarily to Madras on famine relief work, 1877-78; employed in Burma, 1880-85; exec. engr., May, 1883; transf. temporarily to state railways, April, 1885; and permanently, Oct., 1892.

BROOKE, HERBERT OTTO WILDMAN GOODWYN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 6th Nov., 1892, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BROOKE, THOMAS HENRY BARSTOW, Coll., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., April, 1855; arrived, 13th May, 1855, and was employed under the mil. dept. up to 1864, including service in the China expdn., 1857-59; from Feb., 1864, served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Saugor, Nov., 1874; dep. commr., March, 1883; reverted to mil. duty, 1889.

BROOKE, SIR WILLIAM ROBERT, K.C.I.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 13th Sept., 1857; supt., Bombay, Jan., 1866; served in the Punjab, 1869-71; 1st grade supt., Aug., 1877, offg. as dir. of traffic; dir., construction branch, June, 1883; dep. dir.-gen., July, 1889; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, April, 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; ret'd., April, 1895; K.C.I.E., May, 1895.

BROOKE, WILLIAM SAURIN, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., Dec., 1858; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1859; served in the Central Prov., from Jan., 1863, as asst. commr., &c.; cantonmt. mag., Jabalpur, May, 1869; dep. commr., Feb., 1873; acting inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Aug.-Nov., 1883; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1893.

BROUN, JOHN ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at foreign schs., Univ. Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag.; joint mag., April, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1894; mag. and collr., Feb., 1896.

BROWN, CHARLES, Punjab Police Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Feb., 1868; asst. district supt. of police, Nov., 1868; district supt., April, 1876; served in Afghanistan, 1879; in charge of Gen. Daud Shah in 1880; rejoined the dept., Feb., 1882, and served as personal asst. and as asst. to the inspr.-gen.; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1894.

BROWN, CHARLES DALLAS, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived,

22nd Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; ret'd., June, 1895.

BROWN, FREDERICK LATHAM, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., 15th July, 1869; transf. to superior accts. establ., April, 1878; examr., state railway accts., Lower Burma, Nov., 1885; offg. engr.-in-chief and manager, Burma state railway, 1885 and 1886; examr., telegraph accts., March, 1889; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Nov., 1891; ret'd., April, 1893.

BROWN, JAMES SAMUEL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Feb., 1885; offg. dep. manager, N.W. railway, 1889-90.

BROWN, RICHARD CECIL, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Clergy Orphan Sch., Canterbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer.

BROWN, THOMAS EDWIN BURTON, C.I.E., M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the dept. in 1858; from Nov., 1860, served as prof. and principal, med. sch., Lahore; also offd. as civil surgn. of Lahore on several occasions, and as meteorological reporter, 1879; ret'd., Oct., 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; author of a work on the symptoms and treatment of the poisons used in the Punjab.

BROWN, WILLIAM BARCLAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Ayr Acad., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., June, 1889; returned to Bengal as offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1894.

BROWNE, LORD HENRY ULICK, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, Dec., 1850; arrived, 1st Sept., 1851; served in rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1866; also served as private sec. to the prest. of the govr.-gen.'s council, Feb., 1858, under sec. to the govt. of Bengal, July, 1859, offg. under sec. to the govt. of India, home and finl. dept., April, 1860, registrar of the Sadar Diwani and Nizamat Adalat, April, 1861, supt. of stamps and stationery, Oct., 1865, and offg. addl. judge, Jessur and Nuddea, Feb., 1867; commr., Dec., 1869; chairman of the justices of the peace, Calcutta, Feb., 1872, and offg. member, board of rev., June, 1878; ret'd., Oct., 1886.

BROWNE, Ven. JAMES FRANCIS, B.D., late Ecclesi. Dept., Madras.—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, Madras, 20th July, 1870; senr. chaplain, July, 1880; arch-deacon of the diocese of Madras and commissary to the lord bishop, Feb., 1884; senr. joint chaplain of St. George's Cathedral, Feb., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1886; ret'd., April, 1893.

BROWNE, SAMUEL HASLETT, C.I.E., M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Punjab Med. Dept.—Joined the service, March, 1874, and arrived, 14th Nov., 1874; served on hosp. duty and on mil. duty in Afghanistan in 1878; from Aug., 1879, served in the Central Prov. on gen. and jail duty; served under the govt. of India from Dec., 1886, in joint med. charge, Simla; principal of med. coll., Lahore, Oct., 1889; offg. surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, April-Nov., 1893; hon. surgn. to the viceroy, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1896.

BROWNE, WILLIS, F.I.A., India Office.—Writer, 15th Oct., 1867; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878; actuarial clerk, Feb., 1882; fellow of inst. of actuaries, 1887; actuary, funds dept., Dec., 1880; actuary to India office, Oct., 1892.

BROWNE, WILLIAM RICHARD, M.D., Ch.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, April, 1873; arrived, 1st Nov., 1873, and served on mil. and civil and jail duty in Madras; rest. surgn. in gen. hosp. and prof. of pathology, med. coll., Nov., 1883; surgn. in gen. hosp., and prof. of surgery, med. coll., May, 1890; fellow of Madras univ., 1892; field service in Rumpahill tracts and Jaipur territory in 1879-80; princpl. and prof. of medicine, med. coll., April, 1896.

BROWNING, COLIN ARROTT ROBERTSON, C.I.E., M.A., late Educ. Dept., Central Prov.—Senr. inspr. of schools, N.W. Prov., 26th June, 1862; acted as dir. of public instrn., Central Prov., 1864-65, and as inspr.-gen. of educn., Central Prov., 1868, and 1869-70; dir. of public instrn. in Oudh, July, 1870; inspr.-gen. of educn., Central Prov., Oct., 1873; C.I.E., Jan., 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1880; author of 'The Indian Official Thesaurus,' 'The Annals of the Indian Empire,' 'Mahomedan Education in Oudh,' 'A List of Sanscrit Manuscripts existing in Oudh,' &c.; has edited several school and text books, and was joint editor of the 1st edition of the Central Prov. Gazetteer.

BROWNING, HERBERT ARROTT, Capt., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., 21st Jan., 1881; employed in Burma as asst. commr., Sept., 1886.

BROWNING, WINTHROPP BENJAMIN, Surgn.-Major, Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; arrived, 18th Sept., 1880, and served in Madras under the mil. dept. and as acting civil surgn.; district surgn. and jail supt., Madura, Sept., 1890; surgn., 2nd district, Madras, May, 1895; surgn. to the govr., from May, 1892.

BROWNLOW, HENRY ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Bengal engrs., Dec., 1849; dep. supt., Ganges canal, Feb., 1852; and afterwards on the E. and W. Jumna canals; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); suptg. engr., N.W. Prov., irrign., March, 1865; offg. chief engr. and joint sec., irrign., N.W. Prov., Feb., 1872; suptg. engr., Bari Doab, Punjab, April, 1874; acted as chief engr. and joint sec., Punjab, Feb.-May, 1876, and as chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., irrign., June, 1876; offg. inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1877; chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1878; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, Feb., 1882; ret'd., Nov., 1886.

BROWNRIGG, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rossall, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 31st Dec., 1881, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlt. officer, Nov., 1888; joint mag., April, 1890; settlt. officer, Sultanpur, Nov., 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1895; mag. and collr., March, 1896.

BRUCE, ALEXANDER BANNERMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Chanowry Sch., Aberdeen, Milne's Instn., Fochabers, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 11th Nov.,

1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893.

BRUCE, RICHARD ISAAC, C.I.E., late Punjab Commn.—Served in the Punjab as extra asst. commr. from May, 1863; on special duty in connection with Major Sandeman's 2nd Kalat mission, 1876; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Feb., 1877; acted as agent, in 1878, 1882 and 1885; pol. agent at Thal-Chotiali from 1882; rejoined Punjab commn. and was apptd. dep. commr., May, 1888; commr. and supt., Deraajat, Feb., 1893; on special duty as British joint commr. for demarcation of Afghan-Waziri boundary, Oct., 1894; ret'd., April, 1896; author of a gazetteer of Dera Ghazi Khan district, a manual of the Baluch language, and a history of the Marri-Baluchi tribe.

BRUERE, ROBERT SALE ELPHINSTONE RICHARD, Punjab Police.—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., June, 1862; dist. supt., June, 1873.

BRUNYATE, JAMES BENNETT, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 9th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and asst. commr.

BRYANT, JOHN FORBES, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Weymouth Coll., and Clare Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 16th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BUCK, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, K.C.S.I., B.L., LL.D., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived 2nd Feb., 1862; from May, 1863, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, and offg. sec., board of rev.; dir. of agric. and commerce, N.W. Prov., Feb., 1875; commr. for the govt. of India at the Melbourne exhibn. of 1880; sec. to the govt. of India in the rev. and agric. dept., April, 1882; ret'd., Dec., 1896; Kt., Aug., 1886; C.S.I., May, 1892; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1897.

BUCKLAND, CHARLES EDWARD, C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 29th Nov., 1870; asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, Dec., 1870; attaché, foreign dept., Nov., 1871; on famine duty, March, 1874; private sec. to lieut.-govr., 1874-77; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, Aug., 1876; private sec. to Sir R. Temple during his mission to Madras and Bombay, 1877; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, May, 1877; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., May, 1878; private sec. to prest. of govr.-gen.'s council, Nov., 1878; offg. press commr. with govt. of India, Feb., 1879; supt. of stamps and stationery, Bengal, May, 1880; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1887; sec., May, 1890; sec. to govt. of Bengal, gen., rev. and statistical depts., Dec., 1891; member of Bengal legisl. council, Dec., 1893; C.I.E., May, 1895; commr. Burdwan divn., April, 1896; edited 'Excise Manual,' etc.

BUCKLE, HENRY, Jud. Dept., Burma.—Served in the Burma commn. from 7th Aug., 1867, as asst. commr., and was in charge of the town mag.'s office, Akyab, 1871-74, and of the hill tracts of N. Arakan, 1874-76; in command of an expdn. against the Shandus, Nov., 1876; from June, 1878, served as dep. commr. in various districts; prest., Henzada municipal committee

Dec., 1884; offg. commr., 1888, 1890, 1892; addl. sessions judge, Pegu and Irawadi, Jan., 1891.

BUCKLEY, LLEWELLYN EDDISON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and at New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 19th Sept., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; acted as rev. under sec., 1894; head asst., Dec., 1894; senr. asst. dist. mag., and agent to govr., Nov., 1895.

BUCKLEY, ROBERT BURTON, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., July, 1869; exec. engr., March, 1876; services lent to Calcutta corporation, June, 1881; offg. under sec., public works dept., Bengal, July, 1886, and again, Feb., 1887; under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1887, to Dec., 1889; suptg. engr., March, 1892; author of 'Irrigation Works in India,' 1893.

BULL, CHARLES ALBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept. as exec. engr., Nov., 1868, and served in the railway branch; engr.-in-chief, Madras railway surveys, Nov., 1884, and Cuddapah-Nellore railway, Sept., 1886; also in charge of Madras state railway surveys, July, 1887; supt. of way and works, Cuddapah-Nellore railway, Sept., 1887; supt. of works, Burma state railway, Sept., 1891; rank of suptg. engr., June, 1892.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK D'OLBERT, LL.B., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Kingstown Gr. Sch., St. Aubin's Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 11th Jan., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Delhi, Jan., 1873; jud. asst., Dec., 1877; district judge, Nov., 1884; divl. judge, Oct., 1885; offg. judge, chief court, Punjab, in 1892 and 1893; ret'd., June, 1894.

BULLOCK, FREDERICK SHORE, Indian C.S. (Berar).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 7th Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and small cause court judge; mag. and collr., April, 1889; jud. commr., Berar, April, 1892; commr., Hyderabad assigned dists., Oct., 1895.

BULLOCK, ROBERT, Col., I.S.C., late Berar Commn.—First commn., Madras infantry, 11th Dec., 1858; from July, 1862, served in the Berar commn. as asst. commr., offg. asst. rest., and dep. commr. for demarcation of the Dhangs frontier, 1866; jud. commr., Aug., 1888; civil and sessions judge, July, 1889; ret'd., April, 1891.

BULMAN, ALBERT ROBINSON, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Richmond Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; jud. asst., March, 1878; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; offg. commr. and supt., Lahore, Aug., 1884; and Ambala, Jan., 1887; ret'd., April, 1889.

BUNBURY, CECIL EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 21st Nov., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., &c.

BURGESS, GEORGE DOUGLAS, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 9th Nov., 1870, and served in British Burma as asst.

commr., asst. collr. of customs, and dep. commr.; offg. sec. to chief commr., and officer in charge of state prisoners, Jan., 1879; sec. to chief commr., July, 1881; commr., April, 1886; C.S.I., Jan., 1889; jud. commr., Upper Burma, Jan., 1892.

BURGESS, JAMES, C.I.E., LL.D., Hon. A.R.I.B.A., M. Soc. As. Paris, late Archaeological Survey of India. —Educ. at Dumfries, Glasgow, and Edinburgh; apptd. archaeological surveyor and reporter for W. India, 15th Jan., 1874; also archaeological surveyor for S. India, Nov., 1881; dir.-gen. of the archaeological survey of India, March, 1886; fellow of the univ. of Bombay; ret'd., 1889; in 1894, attended the Geneva oriental congress as representative of India; editor of 'The Indian Antiquary,' 1872-84; author of 'Views of Architecture and Scenery in Gujarat and Rajputana,' 'The Temples of Satrunjaya,' 'Somanath, Girnar, &c.,' 'Reports of the Archaeological Survey of W. and S. India,' 'Notes on the Rock Temples of Ajanta, their Paintings and Sculptures,' 'Elephanta,' 'Epigraphia Indica,' &c., and, conjointly with the late J. Ferguson, 'The Rock Temples of India.'

BURKE, CHARLES TOLER, B.E., M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1876; under sec., May, 1892; author of articles on various waterworks schemes.

BURKE, JOHN, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., and June, 1864; supt., Aug., 1883; offg. dir. of traffic, Feb.-June, 1886; has held charge of various divns.

BURKITT, WILLIAM JOHN DWYER, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

BURKITT, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 11th Oct., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, March, 1891; jud. commr. in Oudh, July, 1891; acting puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Nov., 1892.

BURLS, EDWIN GRANT, Store Dept., India Office.—Temporary clerk, India office, 20th Oct., 1863; junr. clerk, store dept., Dec., 1865; senr. clerk, Jan., 1875; asst. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1891; designation changed to dep. dir.-gen. of stores, June, 1895; dir.-gen. of stores, August, 1896.

BURLTON, CHARLES HENRY BEAUMONT, Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; exec. engr., March, 1887; dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., public works dept., April, 1894.

BURLTON, HENRY MONTGOMERY BARTON, Col., I. S. C. —Ensign, Bengal infantry, Aug., 1855; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp); served subsequently in the Bhutan campaign, 1862, and the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); asst. cantonmt. mag., Morar, 1870; cantonmt. mag., Mhow, 1881, Morar, Dec., 1884, and Ambala, Nov., 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1891.

BURN, JOHN GUNN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Inverness Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 24th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BURN, RICHARD, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891, and served in the W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

BURN, ROBERT NATHANIEL, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., Aug., 1870; dep. examr., Jan., 1880; examr., Jan., 1882; auditor, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, April, 1886; govt. examr. of accts., E. Indian railway, Feb., 1889; examr. of telegraph accts., April, 1890; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Jan., 1895; offg. under sec., public works dept., March, 1895.

BURN-MURDOCH, JOHN, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 14th Feb., 1876, and employed on state railways; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal with two clasps); employed in mil. works dept., 1880; offg. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Dec., 1881; on service with the Egyptian expedi., 1882 (medal with two clasps, star, and 5th class Medjidie); dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lahore, Nov., 1882; in same capacity at Bombay, March, 1890; exec. engr., Jan., 1887; in 1890-92 offd. as under sec. to govt. of Bombay, railway branch; services lent to Assam-Bengal railway co., Sept., 1892; and to S. Mahratta railway co., Dec., 1894.

BURNE, HENRY KNIGHTLEY, C.B., Gen., I.S.C.—Ensign, Dec., 1843; arrived, 25th May, 1844, and served throughout the Sutlej campaign, 1845-46 (medal and clasp), and during the Burmese war of 1852-53 (medal and clasp); acted as cantonmt. joint mag., Ambala, Nov., 1849; also as brigade major, Ambala, 1849-51, and as dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Sirhind divn., 1851-52; dep. judge advocate-gen., April, 1852; and asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., July, 1856; 1st asst. sec., Jan., 1858; dep. sec., March, 1862; sec., June, 1870, to Dec., 1881; C.B., May, 1875.

BURNE, KNIGHTLEY GREY, Burma Commn.—Served in the Bengal police as asst. supt., from 26th Oct., 1865 to May, 1872; from the latter date served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and acted as town mag. and supt. of jail at Maulmain, 1872-75; offg. dir. of public instrn., 1882; dep. commr., July, 1886; acting addl. sessions judge, March, 1895, and January, 1896.

BURNE, SIR OWEN TUDOR, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., Major-Gen., Council of India.—Ensign, 20th foot, 15th April, 1855; served in the Crimea; also in the Mutiny of 1858-59, and was present in fifteen actions and received two steps of rank for gallantry in the field; apptd. mil. sec. to the commander-in-chief in India (Lord Strathnairn), 1861-62, and afterwards A.D.C., and at times offg. mil. sec., to that gen. officer when commander of the forces in Ireland, 1865-68; private sec. to viceroy of India (Earl of Mayo), 1869-72; pol. A.D.C. to sec. of state for India, 1872; asst. sec. in pol. and secret dept., India office, 1873; sec. in that dept., 1874; on special duty in India as private sec. to the viceroy (Lord Lytton), 1876-78; member of the council of India, Jan., 1887 to Dec., 1896; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1897; was a royal commr. of the Health

and the Colonial and Indian exhibns. of 1882 and 1886; vice-prest. of the society of arts, 1888-94; apptd. by H. M. the Queen a govr. of the Imperial Institute, 1891; and a royal commr. for the World's Exposition in Chicago, 1891-93; vice-prest. of the council of India, 1891-92; ret'd. from the army, Jan., 1894; chairman of the society of arts, 1896-97; a knight of grace of the order of St. John of Jerusalem; author of 'Clyde and Strathnairn,' &c.

BURRARD, SIDNEY GERALD, Capt., R.E., Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 6th April, 1879; attached to mil. works dept., Baluchistan, March, 1883; asst. supt., trigonl. survey, Sept., 1884; dep. supt., June, 1889.

BURRIDGE, CHARLES EDWARD, Store Dept., India Office.—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 16th March, 1888.

BURROWS, LEONARD ROBERT, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Lincoln Diocesan Sch., St. Nicholas Coll., Lancing, and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1865; prest. of municipal commn., Madras, 1875-80; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1882; acting collr. of sea customs, Madras, and protector of emigrants, Sept., 1883; collr. and mag. and addl. sessions judge, Nilgiris, Sept., 1884; ret'd., Aug., 1889.

BURT, HENRY PARSELL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. from Royal Indian Engrg. Coll. as asst. engr., Sept., 1878; employed at Liverpool on works of L. and N. W. railway; posted to state railways, July, 1879; exec. engr., Nov., 1892; offd. as under sec. to govt. of India, March, 1895.

BURTON, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 26th July, 1857; was apptd. to the dept., May, 1858, and served in Oudh and Haidarabad; exec. engr., July, 1865; exchanged for temporary service out of India, and apptd. to Portsmouth district, and charge of Needles divn., Aug., 1867; proceeded to China, Nov., 1875, where he acted in 1876-77, as commanding royal engrs.; returned to India, Nov., 1877; member of defence committee on coast defences, Dec., 1877; exec. engr., Madras, Jan., 1878; suptg. engr., April, 1889; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

BUSHELL, WILLIAM DONE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 5th Dec., 1895, and served in Bombay.

BUSTEED, HENRY ELMSEY, C.I.E., M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Mint Dept., Calcutta.—First commn., Indian med. service, Aug., 1855; arrived, March, 1856, and served through the Mutiny campaign (medal with clasp); civil surgn., Cuddalore, Jan., 1861; asst. assay master, Madras mint, Feb., 1865; acted as assay master for various periods, and also as mint master and commr. of paper currency; dep. assay master, Bombay, April, 1868; dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, Nov., 1870; assay master, Sept., 1872; also acted as mint master in 1873 and 1875; ret'd., June, 1886; C.I.E., Jan., 1887; author of 'Echoes from Old Calcutta.'

BUTLER, JAMES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—Ensign, 19th foot, Feb., 1866; arrived in Madras, 24th May, 1866; served in the Burma commn. from Oct., 1868, as asst. commr. and town mag.; dep. commr., July, 1881; author of the 'Mergui Gazettee.'

BUTLER, SPENCER HARCOURT, Indian C.S.

(N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 29th Oct., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec., board of rev., March, 1892; asst. settl. officer, Dec., 1893; settl. officer, Dec., 1894.

BUTLER, WILLIAM GEORGE, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Bradfield and Eton; junr. clerk, store dept., India office, 31st Jan., 1867; asst. private sec. and précis writer to sec. of state, 1874-78; senr. clerk, Dec., 1889.

BUTTERWORTH, ALAN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 15th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1893.

BUTTS, HENRY HILL, B.L., late Oudh Commn.—Extra asst. commr., 3rd June, 1863; from Nov., 1865, served as asst. settl. officer and asst. commr.; subord. judge, Faizabad, Aug., 1879; district judge, Faizabad, Jan., 1882; dep. commr., Jan., 1882; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1893; author of the Lucknow settl. report.

BUYERS, JOHN WALKER, C.I.E., M.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. exec. engr., N.W. Prov., 18th Nov., 1869; engr.-in-chief, Garo hills survey, Dec., 1881; suptg. engr., March, 1887; C.I.E., May, 1889; engr.-in-chief, Mu valley railway, July, 1889; ret'd., 1891.

BYATT, JOHN, Store Dept., India Office.—Asst. clerk, educn. dept., May, 1869; clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, May, 1873; supervisor, 2nd grade, Nov., 1880; 1st grade, July, 1888.

CABELL, WILLIAM HENRY LAWSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Eton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

CADELL, ALAN, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., Guetersloh Gymnasium, Westphalia, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; from April, 1867, to March, 1881, served in the settl. dept.; mag. and collr., May, 1880; offg. commr. of excise and stamps and inspr.-gen. of registrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1883; offg. commr., May, 1889; junr. member, board of rev., April, 1893; senr. member, March, 1894; also member of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1893; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan. to Nov., 1895; C.S.I., May, 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Feb., 1896; member board of rev., May, 1896.

CADELL, ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—2nd lieut., Bengal engrs., 13th June, 1845; joined the public works dept., April, 1848; exec. engr., Rawal Pindi, 1850; Nagpur, 1856; Oudh road dept., Dec., 1858; suptg. engr., Punjab, 1864; from 1867 to 1878 served in Indore, as suptg. engr., and chief engr., and sec. to the govr.-gen.'s agent for Central India; also offd. as chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1874-75; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1879-80; dep. inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1881; inspr.-gen., mil. works, 1881-82; ret'd., March, 1882.

CADELL, PATRICK ROBERT, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and administrator of Sachin state.

CADELL, THOMAS, V.C., Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, April, 1854; arrived, July, 1854; served during the Mutiny (medal, clasp, and Victoria cross); second in command, Malwa Bhill corps, dep. Bhill agent, and pol. asst., April, 1861; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, April, 1870; pol. agent, Alwar, Nov., 1870; pol. agent, Marwar, Nov., 1877, and in Mewar, April, 1878; commandant, Erinpura force, and pol. agent, W. Rajputana, Oct., 1879; chief commr. and supt., Port Blair and the Nicobars, Dec., 1879; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892.

CALCRAFT-KENNEDY, BENNET CHRISTIAN HUNTINGDON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Queen's Coll., Oxford, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CALDECOTT, RANDOLPH, Bde.-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the Indian med. service, 9th May, 1870; acted for various periods as residency surgn., Indore, and civil med. officer in Central India; and was apptd. to that post, March, 1894.

CALLAGHAN, THOMAS, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 27th March, 1885.

CALNAN, DENIS, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at French Coll., Blackrock, Ireland, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag.; joint mag., Oct., 1891.

CAMERON, CHARLES, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 27th July, 1859; arrived, 28th Jan., 1860; was employed under the mil. dept., 1860-66, and served in the Bhutan campaign (medal); from April, 1866, served in Oudh as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; ret'd., Sept., 1892.

CAMERON, DUNCAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 30th Nov., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1887; in 1889 served in the Punjab as offg. divl. judge; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1894.

CAMERON, LEWIS, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1866; from Feb., 1873, served as civil asst. surgn. and surgn. in Bengal; ret'd., Aug., 1891.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER WILLIAM DENNIS-TOWN, Major, I.S.C., Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1871; served under the mil. dept. to April, 1882, when he was apptd. cantonmt. mag., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD COLIN, late Assam Commn.—Served in Assam from 27th April, 1861, as asst. commr. and personal asst. to chief commr.; was personal asst. to the chief civil officer, Bhutan field force, 1865 (medal and clasp); supt. of emigration and embarkation agent, Dhubri, Sept., 1875; dep. commr., Dec., 1878; acted as commr., Assam valley districts, in 1889; ret'd., 1890.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD EDWARDES, Major-

Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn., Jan., 1851; served in the Sonthal campaign, 1855-56, and in Central India, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); from May, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., Dec., 1866; inspr. of labourers, Sibsagar, Feb., 1882; reverted to mil. dept., 1890.

CAMPBELL, ARTHUR FREDERICK TRAVERS, India Audit Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 18th March, 1893.

CAMPBELL, COLIN GEORGE, Pol. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, mil. dept., 13th Jan., 1876; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, 1877-92; senr. clerk, Feb., 1889; reading clerk to council, Aug., 1890; private sec. to sec. of state (Right Hon. H. H. Fowler), March, 1894, to July, 1895; sec. to royal commn. on Indian expenditure, Aug., 1895.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN JOHN ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 24th Nov., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; transf. to Burma, Aug., 1878, and served as asst. commr. and boundary officer; cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, 1883; on special duty in Upper Burma, Feb., 1886; dep. commr., May, 1888; addl. sessions judge, June-Oct., 1894; transf. to Cent. Prov. as dep. commr., Nov., 1895; div. judge, May, 1896.

CAMPBELL, DUNCAN McNEIL, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 8th April, 1869, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; under sec. to govt., public works dept., 1885-86; suptg. engr., July, 1890; chief engr. and sec. to govt., public works dept., Mysore, July, 1892, to July, 1894; joint sec. to govt. of Madras, gen. buildings and roads branch, Jan., 1896; chief engr., Jan., 1896.

CAMPBELL, FRANCIS JOHNSTONE GRAHAM, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 20th Jan., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and district and sessions judge; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

CAMPBELL, GEORGE ALLEN, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 5th Aug., 1873, as asst. engr., and was employed in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh on provl. railways and other duties; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Indian Midland railway co., 1887-88; transf. to state railways, March, 1891; supt. of works, Cawnpore-Gogra connection, Dec., 1894.

CAMPBELL, GERALD EDWARD LYON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 11th Dec., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; sub. collr. and joint mag., Mar., 1896.

CAMPBELL, JAMES McNABB, C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, April-Aug., 1877; acting municipal commr., Bombay, April, 1880; acting under sec. to govt. in the rev., finl., and gen. depts., Oct., 1880; acted as collr. of Bombay and supt. of stamps and stationery in 1881; C.I.E., Jan., 1885; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1890; collr. of land rev., customs and opium, and supt. of stamps

and stationery, Nov., 1891; senr. collr., Aug., 1893; compiler of 'Bombay Gazetteer.'

CAMPBELL, JOHN SCARLETT, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Grange, Sunderland; apptd. from Haileybury, 1847; arrived, Jan., 1848, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in the N.W. Prov.; joint mag., 1856; during the Mutiny was present at the siege of Delhi, and subsequently took charge of the Rohtak district as dep. commr.; commr., Saugor divn., Central Prov., Jan., 1862; jud. commr., Central Prov., 1864; offg. chief commr., Central Prov., 1865; judge of the chief court, Punjab, 1871; on assassination of Lord Mayo in 1872, was depd. to enquire into the administration of the convict settl. in the Andaman Islands; ret'd., 1873; author of a 'Memo. on proposed changes in the mode of selection and training of candidates for the Indian Civil Service.'

CAMPBELL, JOHN STRATHEDEN, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 14th Dec., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1890; dep. commr., Feb., 1896.

CAMPBELL, RICHARD HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.; acting under sec., rev. dept., in 1892 and 1893.

CAMPBELL-WALKER, INCHES, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 27th Dec., 1859; from May, 1865, served in the Madras forest dept. as asst. and dep. conservator; apptd. a commr. for the Vienna exhibn., 1873; knight commander of the order of Francis Joseph of Austria for services there; offg. inspr. of forests, Feb., 1874, to Sept., 1875; on duty at the forest conference, Simla, Sept., 1875; on special duty to New Zealand, 1876-77, and in the Nilgiris, S. Coimbatore, and Tinnevell, Dec., 1877; conservator of forests, 1st grade, Dec., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1892; author of reports on forest management in Germany, Austria, and Great Britain (1872), and on the forests of New Zealand.

CAMPION, JOHN MONTRIOU, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 1st July, 1869, as asst. engr., and employed on state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf. to Punjab provl. establ., Aug., 1881; served temporarily in mil. works dept., 1881, and on state railways, 1884-85.

CANDY, CHARLES HARRISON, B.A., LL.M.; Bombay Educl. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1875, as acting vice-principal, Elphinstone high sch.; head master, Maharaja's coll., Mysore, June, 1876; principal and profir. of English literature, Rajaram coll., Kolhapur, June, 1880; principal, Jan., 1886.

CANDY, EDWARD TOWNSHEND, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1878; acting jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, May, 1883; judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; acting rememberance of legal affairs, July-Nov., 1885; judge of high court, Bombay, April, 1892.

CANDY, RICHARD EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting dir. of public instrn., Berar, June, 1872, to Dec., 1873; first asst. collr. and mag., Oct., 1877; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1883; senr. collr., Sept., 1888.

CANTOPHER, BERNARD WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 29th Aug., 1873, and posted to state railways; transf. to Bengal, Aug., 1881; again employed on railways, from Nov., 1883; exec. engr., May, 1884; services lent to Mahratra railway co., 1888-89; offg. engr. in-chief, Burma state railway, Sept.-Dec., 1893.

CAPPEL, SIR ALBERT JAMES LEPPOC, K.C.I.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 10th Feb., 1857; 1st grade supt. and personal asst. to dir.-gen., Dec., 1866; dir. of traffic, May, 1869; dep. dir.-gen., Feb., 1879; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, June, 1883; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; retd., 1889.

CAPPEL, EDWARD LOUIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 28th Oct., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; dir. of land records and agric., June, 1891, to April, 1893; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1896.

CARD, HENRY CHARLES, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 22nd Jan., 1875.

CARDEW, ALEXANDER GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to the Malabar land tenures committee, Dec., 1885; asst. sec., jud. and legisl. dept., Nov., 1888; inspr.-gen. of jails, Aug., 1892.

CARDEW, CORNELIUS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the superior rev. establt., state railways, locomotive dept., March, 1876; locomotive supt., April, 1881; served on the Rajputana, Nagpur-Chhattisgarh, Rajputana-Malwa, and other railways.

CARDOZO, HENRY O'CONNELL, Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Served in the dept. from 21st March, 1859, and was promoted to dep. supt., 1st class, March, 1883, and to supt., July, 1887.

CAREY, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. collr. of salt rev.; collr. of salt rev., April, 1881; from Feb., 1882, served under the govt. of India as offg. commr., inland customs; commr., salt rev., N. India, Dec., 1882; commr. of customs, salt, opium, and abkari, and reporter-gen. of external commerce for Bombay, May, 1891; on special duty in Lisbon for Goa treaty negotiations, 1891-92; retd., March, 1893.

CAREY, HORACE RICHARD LE MARCHANT, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 15th Jan., 1861; served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp); apptd. to the dept., 24th Dec., 1870, as asst. engr., and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; transf. to superior accts. dept., Sept., 1874; examr., Jan., 1882; employed on public works

accts. in Burma, April, 1883; and in Bengal, June, 1888.

CAREY, HIRZEL DENIS DE MUSSSENDEN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CAREY, LIONEL SLADE, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, June to Dec., 1885; settl. officer, Oct., 1886; dep. commr. and rev. sec., Dec., 1891; commr. of settlts. and agric., Jan., 1895.

CARGILL, JAMES DUDLEY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the Brighton Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 13th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CARLESS, GEORGE PROCTOR, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., February, 1896; exec. engr., October, 1888.

CARLYLE, ROBERT WARRAND, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; undersec. to govt., Dec., 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1890; has offd. also as under sec. to the govt. of India, home dept.; mag. and collr., June, 1894.

CARMICHAEL, CHARLES PAGET, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Harrow and Haileybury; apptd., 1849; arrived, March, 1849, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. sec. to govt.; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, Jan., 1856; depd. as civil officer with the relieving force to Shahjahanpur, April, 1858; mag. and collr., Budaun, Feb., 1862; inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Prov., Feb., 1870; commr. and agent to govr.-gen., Benares, March, 1877; also held the substantive appt. of district and sessions judge, Jan., 1877; member of the board of rev., 1882; retd., March, 1884.

CARMICHAEL, DAVID FREMANTLE, late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1851; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1851; head asst. registrar, court of Sadar and Foujdari Adalat, Feb., 1855; dep. sec. to govt., Oct., 1856; sec. to board of examrs., Oct., 1856; private sec. to govr. of Madras, March, 1859; fellow of Madras univ., Oct., 1859; collr. and mag. and agent, July, 1863; acting judge, high court, Madras, April to July, 1869; employed in preparation of Vizagapatam district manual; acting member, board of rev., Aug., 1871; inam commr., Aug., 1871; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1872; member of legisl. council, Feb., 1874; on special duty at imperial assemblage, Delhi, Dec., 1876; chief sec. to govt., June, 1877; ordinary member of council, Madras, Dec., 1878; on tour as special commr. through hill tracts of Ganjam, Vizagapatam, and Godavari, Feb., 1881; retd., 1886.

CARMICHAEL, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; arrived, 4th Dec., 1836, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1889; returned to Bombay as 2nd asst., Oct., 1894.

CARNDUFF, HERBERT WILLIAM CAMERON, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Coll.

Sch., Univ. of Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 30th Nov., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, May, 1888; registrar, appellate side, high court, Calcutta, Oct., 1890; acted as dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., from Jan., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, December, 1895.

CARR, REGINALD CHILDERS CULLING, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Cheam Sch., the Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; addl. special asst. to district mag. and collr., March, 1889; asst. sec., jud. and legis. depts., Sept., 1891; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1891, 1893, and 1895; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1892; under sec., in depts. under the chief sec., May, 1894.

CARR, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Lancaster Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 14th Nov., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

CARSTAIRS, ROBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and Edinburgh Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 16th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1888; author of 'British Work in India.'

CARTER, ALBERT MICHAEL, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; temporary clerk, India office, 4th Nov., 1861; junr. clerk, March, 1866; senr. clerk, Aug., 1882; assessor of income tax, 1892.

CARTER, GEORGE MARKHAM STRANGE, Burma Commn.—Apptd. chief inspr. of police, June, 1875; asst. district supt., Feb., 1880, and district supt., Oct., 1882; apptd. asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., Dec., 1890; acted as prest., Rangoon municipality, Dec., 1893, and Jan., 1894.

CARTER, HENRY VANDYKE, M.D. (Lon.), Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 27th Jan., 1858; arrived, 29th March, 1858, and served on mil. duty and as prof. at the Grant med. coll., and asst. surgn., Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp.; also as civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Satara; surgn. to the Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., Bombay, Nov., 1879; principal and prof. of medicine, Grant med. coll., and first physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., 1886; ret'd., July, 1888.

CARTER, PHILIP JOHN, Burma Forest Dept.—Arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1875, and was apptd. to Burma as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, April, 1881; on duty at Port Blair, Nov., 1886; rejoined his Burma appt., June, 1889; conservator, Feb., 1892.

CARTER-CAMPBELL, THOMAS TUPPER, Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—First commn., Dec., 1857; arrived, Dec., 1859; served with the Sikkim field force, 1860-61, as survey officer; from May, 1861, served as asst. engr. in the public works dept., Punjab; apptd. to the great trigon. survey of India, March, 1862, and served as asst. in the Kashmir and Kumaon and Garhwal surveys; served in the Ambela campaign, 1863 (medal and clasp); surveyor, April, 1866; in charge of the survey party with the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-69; dep. supt., 1st grade, March, 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1887.

CASEY, ARTHUR EDWIN COMERFORD, late Bengal C.S. (N. W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., and Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Kumaon, May, 1882; ret'd., Oct., 1889.

CASPERSZ, CHARLES PETER, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 21st Dec., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1894.

CASSON, HERBERT ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Newton Abbot Proprietary Coll., Marlborough Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 15th Nov., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., 1894-95.

CAVE-BROWNE, EDWARD RABAN, C.S.I., Acctnt.-Gen., India Office.—Educ. at the Coll. Sch., Taunton; junr. clerk in the treasury, East India house, 12th April, 1854, and from 1858 in the India office; acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 1860; senr. clerk, June, 1867; asst. in the acctts. branch of the finl. dept., Jan., 1872; assessor of income tax, 1872; dep. acctnt.-gen., Feb., 1879; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; acctnt.-gen., June, 1893.

CAWLEY, GEORGE JOHN, late Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1862, as asst. supt. of police, Assam; district supt., Dec., 1876; transf. to Bengal, Dec., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

CAYLEY, HENRY, Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1857; from April, 1872, served as civil surgn. and offg. supt. of vaccn., supt. of royal botanical gardens, ophthalmic surgn. and prof. of ophthalmic surgery, med. coll., Calcutta, supt. of the eye infirmary, and marine surgn.; med. board, India office, 1885; ret'd., April, 1887.

CENTER, WILLIAM, M.B., M.A., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined Indian med. dept. in 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866; from March, 1871, served in the Punjab as chemical examr. and prof. in the Lahore med. sch.; offg. meteorological reporter in addn., May, 1872; served under the govt. of India, 1882, as statistical officer in sanitary dept.; civil surgn., Lahore, and supt., lunatic asylum, 1884; prof. Lahore med. coll., April, 1888; ret'd., April, 1893.

CHADWICK, WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Public Works Dept.—Apptd. from Royal Indian Engrg. Coll. as asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to State Rlys.; exec. engr., Oct., 1892.

CHAMBERLAIN, SIR NEVILLE BOWLES, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., Gen.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, 1837; served through the Afghan campaigns of 1839-42 (medal); present with the govr.-gen.'s body-guard at the battle of Maharajpur, Dec., 1843 (bronze star); dep. asst. quartermaster-gen. to the army, 1843; hon. A.D.C. to Lord Dalhousie, 1848; served in the Punjab campaign of 1848-49, including the actions of Chilianwala and Gujrat (medal and two clasps); mil. sec. to the chief commr. for affairs of the Punjab, March, 1853; commandant, Punjab irregular force, Dec., 1854; adjutant-gen. of the Bengal army, July, 1857;

C.B., 1857; A.D.C. to the Queen for services in the Mutiny; commanded the force on the N.W. frontier of India in various expeditions against the hill tribes (medal and clasp); commander-in-chief, Madras, and member of the govt.'s council, Feb., 1876, to 1881; was apptd. head of the special mission to Kabul, Aug., 1878, which, however, was not allowed to enter the Amir's territory.

CHAMBERLAIN, WILLIAM JOHN, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt. Dhond-Mamdad railway, 29th Oct., 1878; served upon Berar, Punjab N., Rajputana-Malwa, and other railways; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, April, 1892.

CHAMIER, EDWARD MAYNARD DES CHAMPS, (barr.-at-law).—Entered the service as public prosecutor in the High Court, N.W.P., and standing counsel to govt., Jan., 1896.

CHAMIER, FRANCIS EDWARD ARCHIBALD, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, 7th April, 1850; A.D.C. to Gen. Outram throughout the Oudh campaign; commanded the Kapurthala contingent during its service in Oudh, in 1858 (medal and two clasps); from March, 1858, served in Oudh as asst. commr., dep. commr., and offg. commr.; district judge, Rae Bareli, Feb., 1882, and afterwards at Faizabad; returned to Europe, 1888.

CHANNING, FRANCIS CHORLEY, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 25th Nov., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settlt. officer; settlt. sec. to finl. commr., Jan., 1878; senr. sec. to the finl. commr., March, 1882; dep. commr., March, 1886; district judge, Jan., 1888, but to continue to act as divl. judge; divl. judge, May, 1890; acting judge, chief court, May-Dec., 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CHAPMAN, CHARLES, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th Aug., 1884.

CHAPMAN, EDMUND PELLY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 12th Nov., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and collr., Dec., 1895.

CHAPMAN, REGINALD CORRIE, Fin. and Commerce Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1874, and was employed at Nagpur, and afterwards at Rangoon; asst. comptr.-gen. at Calcutta, Feb., 1879; transfed. to Madras as acting asst. acctnt.-gen., March, 1879; confd., July, 1880; in addn., inspr. of local fund accts., April, 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1891.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT ALEXANDER BOSWELL, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 15th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., May, 1896.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT BARCLAY, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1850; arrived, 6th March, 1850; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; under sec. to govt. of India, home, finl., and foreign depts., April, 1856, to April, 1860; called to the bar (Inner Temple), Jan., 1863; junr. sec., board of rev., Lower Prov., June,

1863; acting commr., Nuddea divn., Nov., 1866, to Jan., 1869; served subsequently as sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., and govt. dir. of Bank of Bengal; ret'd., 1881.

CHARLES, FENDALL LOWIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Repton Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, demarcation of forests in Nasik, July, 1875; forest settlt. officer, Dharwar, Nov., 1881; acting municipal commr., Bombay, Oct., 1886; junr. collr. and mag., April, 1890; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892.

CHARLES, GEORGE GWYTHER, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 9th May, 1857; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1857, and held charge of various offices in Bengal and the N.W. Prov.; inspr., April, 1862; asst supt., Feb., 1863; supt., April, 1880.

CHARLES, JOHN GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 15th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec.; member of cattle plague commn., June, 1870; offg. dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, July, 1871; offg. dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, March, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1876; acted as presidency and as chief presidency mag., Calcutta; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1885; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

CHARLES, RICHARD HAVELOCK, M.D., M.Ch., F.R.C.S.I., Surgn.-Major, Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1882, and served as prof. of anatomy, Lahore med. coll.; apptd. in similar capacity to Calcutta med. coll., April, 1894.

CHARLTON, ALFRED, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th April, 1882.

CHATFIELD, KYRLE MITFORD, M.A., Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; served from 29th March, 1866, as principal and prof. of logic and moral philosophy at the Elphinstone coll., Bombay; dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1874.

CHATTERTON, FRANK WILLIAM, Col., Bengal Infantry.—Arrived in India as ensign, 4th Nov., 1859; employed under the mil. dept. till Feb., 1869, from which date he served in Jhansi and Kumaon as offg. asst. commr.; from May, 1873, served as cantonmt. mag. of Rurki; also small cause court judge, Rurki, March, 1879; cantonmt. mag., Agra, Dec., 1880, to Nov., 1886, when he was apptd. commandant of administrative battalion Bengal presidency volunteers; hon. A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1888.

CHENEVIX-TRENCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1879; served in the Burma expedn., 1886; offg. asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Rajputana, June, 1888; on depn. in Kotah and Jhallowar, 1888-89; acting asst. district supt. of police, Quetta, Dec., 1889; also asst. commr., April, 1890; offg. first asst. to pol. rest., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1890; acting pol. agent, Basra, April, 1891; British joint commr. at Leh, Dec., 1891; asst. rest. for Leh, Kashmir, Dec., 1894.

CHERRY, JOHN WILLIAM, Forest Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1869, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, Jan., 1872; conservator, Oct., 1891.

CHEVIS, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford;

apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, April, 1893.

CHICHELE- PLOWDEN, TREVOR JOHN, C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 31st Oct., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec.; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1873; inspr.-gen. of police, Assam, Nov., 1876; sec. to the prison conference, Jan., 1877; sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1878; rest., and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, March, 1880; consul-gen., Baghdad, June, 1880; commr. of Ajmir-Merwara, June, 1885; rest. in Mewar, Nov., 1885; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1887; commr., Hyderabad assigned districts, Nov., 1888; rest., Hyderabad, March, 1892; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; author of several reports on journeys in Kurdistan, &c.

CHIPPER, ERNEST, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd Dec., 1875.

CHISHOLM, JAMES WRIGHT, late Central Prov. Commn.—Served in the Central Prov. from 24th Oct., 1856, as dep. collr., asst. commr., dep. commr., and settl. officer; inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, and commr. of excise, Oct., 1871; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Oct., 1876; sec. to the chief commr., June, 1880; commr. of the Chhattisgarh divn., March, 1883; ret'd., 1889.

CHISHOLME, JOHN JAMES SCOTT, Lieut.-Col., 5th Lancers.—First commn., 9th lancers, 10th Jan., 1872; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80; mil. sec. to govr. of Madras (Lord Connemara, G.C.I.E.), 10th Aug., 1888; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1890.

CHITPAL SINGH, Raja, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. under native civil service rules, 12th Dec., 1881, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893, offd. as dep. commr., March, 1896.

CHITTY, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., B.L., Bombay Jud. Dept.—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 17th Nov., 1884; acting chief judge, Bombay small cause court, Feb.-May, 1891, and again from Oct., 1891; confd., July, 1892.

CHOLMELEY, NORMAN GOODFORD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1884, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1890.

CHRISTIE, ARCHIBALD, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 7th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty, Kuram field force, Oct., 1878; offg. under sec. to govt., Aug. to Dec., 1884; district judge, Sept., 1887; divl. judge, April, 1893; acting judge, chief court, June, 1895; ret'd. Aug., 1896.
