Prov. as asst. under the commr. of Meerut, asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, asst. settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1866; mag. and collr., Moradabad, July, 1869; civil and sessions judge, Gorakhpur, April, 1877; commr. and agent, Benares divn., March, 1881; member, board of rev., March, 1884; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

DANIELL, CLARMONT JOHN, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1855; arrived, 7th Dec., 1855; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., under sec. to govt., dep. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Cawnpore, Aug., 1872; civil and sessions judge, Gorakhpur, Aug., 1878; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1883; offg. commr., 1881 and 1883; 1st grade judge, April, 1885; ret'd., Oct., 1886.

DANIELS, SIDNEY REGINALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wycliffe Coll., Stonehouse (Glos.), Balliol Coll., Oxford, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

DANKS, ERIC, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Ripon Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 15th Dec., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

DANSEY, EDWARD PRINCE, India Forest Dept.—Joined the service, 20th Dec., 1872, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, Sept., 1882; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests, June, 1888; conservator of forests, Bengal, May, 1891; transf. to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1894.

DANVERS, FREDERICK CHARLES, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and King's Coll., London, and, after a special training preparatory for Addiscombe, studied for two years for a civil and mechanical engr.; East India house writer, old establ., 26th Jan., 1853; junr. clerk, public and ecclesi. dept., India office, Sept., 1858; public works dept., 1861; senr. clerk, June, 1867; asst. sec. in the public works dept., Feb., 1875; asst. sec. in the rev. dept., 1877; registrar and supt. of records, Jan., 1884; in 1855 designed a salt-weighing machine, of which a number were manufactured in this country for the use of the Madras govt.; in 1859 was depd. to Liverpool and Manchester to report on traction engines, with a view to their being used in India; depd. to Lisbon to examine the Portuguese records relating to India, 1891-92; on similar duty to the Hague, 1893-95; author of articles publ. in 'Engineering' relative to public works in India, 1866-75; of a design for carrying the East India Railway under the Hugli from Howrah to Calcutta, transmitted to India by the sec. of state in 1868; of 'Statistical Papers relating to

India' (parly. paper, 1869); of memoranda on Indian coal, coal-washing and artificial fuel, 1867-69; of 'Coal Economy,' 1872; of 'A Century of Famines, 1770-1870,' 1877; of 'Spon's Information for Colonial Engineers—India,' 1877; of papers read before the Society of Arts, on 'Agriculture in India,' 1878 (Society's silver medal), 'Famines in India,' 1886, and 'The India Office Records,' 1889 (Society's silver medal); of a 'Report on the Records of the India Office,' 1887; of 'Chiefs, Agents, and Governors of Bengal,' 1888; of a 'Report on the Portuguese Records relating to India,' 1892; and of a 'History of the Portuguese in India,' 1894; elected a corresponding member of the royal geographical society of Lisbon, 1894, and of the international colonial institute, Brussels, 1895.

DANVERS, SIR JULAND, K.C.S.I., late Public Works Dept., India Office.—Educ. at King's Coll., London; East India house writer, 22nd March, 1842; junr. clerk, Feb., 1847; minuting branch, sec.'s dept., 1847; private sec. to chairman and dep., 1848-53; senr. clerk, Feb., 1852; asst., minuting branch, sec.'s dept., 1857; asst. sec. in the railway and electric telegraph dept., India office, and asst. dir. of Indian railway cos., Feb., 1858; sec. in the railway and telegraph dept., and dep. dir. of Indian railway cos., Sept., 1858, to July, 1861; govt. dir. of Indian railway cos., 1861; visited India in his official capacity in 1875-76; sec. in the public works, railway and telegraph depts., Aug., 1880, continuing to be dir. of Indian railway cos.; K.C.S.I., 1886; ret'd., March, 1892.

DARLINGTON, EDWIN, Customs Dept., Burma.—Joined the service as supt. of the Burma secretariat, June, 1868; from March, 1872, served as collr. of customs, Akyab; asst. sec. to the chief commr., April, 1875; collr. of customs, Rangoon, July, 1877; chief collr., Sept., 1880; also vice-chairman of the Rangoon port commrs., March, 1882.

DARRAH, HENRY ZOUCH, B.A., LL.B., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rathmines Sch., Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 24th Oct., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. dir. of agric. and commerce; acting sec. to chief commr. of Assam, March-April, 1886; dir. of land records and agric., Assam, Jan., 1887; on special duty in the Central Prov. in 1887, and in Burma, 1890; returned to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as offg. dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1893; mag. and collr., July, 1894; author of a 'Monograph on Cotton in Assam,' 'Burma and Assam,' 'The Eri Silk of Assam,' 'The Cattle of Assam,' 'An Experiment in Survey and Settlement,' a 'Note on the People of Assam,' 'Eleven Years' Work of a Land Records Department,' &c.

DAUKES, FREDERICK CLENDON, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Uppingham Gr. Sch., Blackheath Proprietary Sch., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. sec. to govt.; asst. settlt. officer, Banda, Nov., 1873; attaché, foreign dept., govt. of India, 1873; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., 1876-77; sec., public service commn., Sept., 1886; under sec., home dept., Dec., 1877; sec. to the chief commr. of Assam, Nov., 1890;

C.I.E., Jan., 1892; retd., Oct., 1894; transl. Terentyef's 'Russia and England in Central Asia.'

DAVIDSON, ALFRED, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1877.

DAVIDSON, JAMES, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and cantonmt. mag.; on famine duty in Mysore, 1877-78; second asst. collr., Nov., 1886; forest settl. officer, Nasik, Aug., 1887; first asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1888; acting collr. of salt rev., Aug., 1892; junr. collr. and mag., March, 1893; retd., Oct., 1896.

DAVIDSON, JAMES YOUNG, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., 9th Dec., 1869, and served in the Central Prov.; exec. engr., Oct., 1875; from Jan., 1882, served as manager, on the Wardha coal, and Nagpur-Chhattisgarh railways; in charge of Umaria colliery, Jan., 1886; acting engr.-in-chief, Nagpur-Bengal railway, Feb., 1886; retd., 1889.

DAVIDSON, JOHN PATON, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884; asst. to chief engr. for irrig., and under sec., irrig. branch, July, 1886, to Nov., 1888.

DAVIDSON, LIONEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting asst. and under sec.; spl. asst. collr. and mag., Oct., 1895.

DAVIDSON, ROBERT, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1852; served as asst. collr. and mag., sub-judge, small cause court judge, civil and sessions judge, Trichinopoli, and district and sessions judge; acted as collr. and mag. of Karnul during the famine, 1877-78; third member, board of rev., March, 1878; chief sec. to govt., Dec., 1878; legisl. council, 1880-83; provisional member of the exec. council, May-July, 1881; 1st member, board of rev., March, 1882; retd., June, 1884.

DAVIES, ARTHUR MATCHAM, Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 23rd Jan., 1849; served in the dept. from Sept., 1862, as asst. supt. and supt.; acting personal asst. to commr. of police, Madras, Sept., 1866; dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1881, to 1886.

DAVIES, CHARLES ALLAN WILLIAM, late Hyderabad Comm.—Joined the service, 22nd March, 1855, as attaché to dep. commr., N. Berar; extra asst. commr., July, 1858; also asst. commr., paper currency, July, 1868; special asst. commr., July, 1891; retd., June, 1893.

DAVIES, CHARLES MERSON, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. locomotive supt., 15th Sept., 1875; supt., March, 1880; retd., Sept., 1893.

DAVIES, HENRY SAMUEL PRICE, Capt., Punjab Comm.—Arrived, 28th June, 1878, and from April, 1883, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty at Rawal Pindi durbar, March, 1885; acted as private sec. to the lieut.-govr. of the Punjab in 1887 and 1888; asst. commr.

and settl. officer, Oct., 1888; dep. commr., March, 1895.

DAVIES, JAMES ACWORTH, B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Denmark Hill Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1869, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. sec. to govt. in the jud. and legis. depts., April, 1880; member of the board of examrs., Jan., 1882; under sec. in the rev. dept., Oct., 1882; acting sec. to the board of rev., June, 1883; from Oct., 1883, acted as registrar, high court, Madras, and was apptd. sub-collr. and joint mag., Kistna, July, 1884; district and sessions judge, March, 1888; judge, high court, Dec., 1895.

DAVIES, SIR ROBERT HENRY, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. to the Bengal civil service from Haileybury in 1844; served in the N.W. Prov. and cis-Sutlej territory as asst. commr.; settl. officer, Bari Doab, 1851; joint mag. and dep. collr., Azimgarh, 1858; sec. to govt. of Punjab, 1859; finl. commr., Oudh, 1864; chief commr., Oudh, 1869; lieut.-govr., Punjab, 1871; retd., 1878; member of council of India, March, 1885, to March, 1895.

DAVIES, SIR WILLIAM GEORGE, K.C.S.I., Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Punjab Comm.—Arrived, 5th Jan., 1849; from April, 1854, served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer; dep. commr., May, 1862; commr. and supt., Delhi, Nov., 1876, and subsequently in the Jalandar divn.; finl. commr., Punjab, Dec., 1883; member of the govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, March, 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; retd., April, 1887; author of a report on the settl. of the Shahpur district.

DAVIS, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Rossall, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., March, 1891; on special duty with Manipur field force, 1891; on special duty as pol. officer, N. Lushai, Oct., 1892; confd., April, 1893.

DAVIS, JOHN SAMUEL CHAMPION, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Taunton Coll. Sch., Rossall, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 1st Dec., 1880, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1882; junr. sec., board of rev., Oct., 1886, and Feb., 1890; joint mag., Oct., 1889; settl. officer, Feb., 1893.

DAWSON, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Wadham and Ariel Colls., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 12th Dec., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

DAWSON, WALTER HILL, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Kensington Gr. Sch., St. Peter's, Weston-super-Mare, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 21st Nov., 1888, and served as asst. commr. in Burma; under sec. to chief commr., April, 1892; offg. dep. commr., June, 1895.

DAY, ARTHUR SOMERSET, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Brighton Coll., Bedford Gr. Sch., and Clifton Coll.; writer, India office, 26th Oct., 1874; clerk, 2nd class, Nov., 1878.

DAYARAM GIDUMAL, B.A., LL.B. Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under

the native civil service rules, and asst. collr., 3rd Jan., 1885; employed from May, 1885, to April, 1886, in compiling a jaghir record for Sind; confd. as member of the civil service, 3rd March, 1887; and has since acted as asst. judge and sessions judge, and joint judge and sessions judge; 1st asst., May, 1896.

DE, KIRAN CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DEAN, EDWARD JOSEPH.—Supt. of Bengal secretariat press, 1st May, 1868, and of the public works dept. press and dep. supt. of govt. printing, April, 1869; supt. of govt. printing, Calcutta, April, 1873; ret'd., May, 1891.

DEANE, HAROLD ARTHUR, C.S.I., Major, I.S.C., Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1874, and from July, 1885, served in the Punjab, as asst. commr.; placed on special duty with Chitral relief force, March, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; dep. commr., March, 1896.

DEANE, THOMAS, Col., I.S.C.—First commn., 21st hussars, March, 1862; lieut., Bengal staff corps, March, 1863; adjutant, govt.-gen.'s body guard, Jan., 1871; 3rd asst. sec. to the govt. of India, mil. dept., Jan., 1877; 2nd asst. sec., April, 1884; dep. sec., July, 1886; dir., army remount dept., Nov., 1889.

DEAS, JOSEPH, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 1st Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. settl. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1887; legal remembrancer, Dec., 1890; sec. to legisl. council, Dec., 1890; also member of the council, Jan., 1891; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1893; jud. commr. in Oudh, April, 1896.

DEASE, PAGET PATRICK, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st April, 1871, and employed on state railways in the Punjab and Baluchistan; exec. engr., Sept., 1881; engr.-in-chief, Wazirabad-Multan railway survey, Nov., 1892; dep. to Kashmir in 1893, in connection with railway survey; engr.-in-chief, Umarkot-Pachpadra railway survey, Oct., 1893; offg. engr.-in-chief, Kotri-Rohri railway, May, 1895; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Feb., 1896.

DE BEAUREPAIRE, ERNEST LUCIEN, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 31st March, 1885.

DE BOINVILLE, CHARLES ALFRED CHASTEL, A.R.I.B.A., Surveyor and Clerk of Works, India Office.—Architect and surveyor-in-chief to the govt. of Japan, 1872-82; draughtsman, H.M.'s office of works, 1886-93; surveyor and clerk of the works, India office, 22nd Aug., 1893.

DE BOURBEL, RAOUL, Maj.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., as lieut., Bengal engrs., 1850; joined the dept., Nov., 1852; exec. engr., Lower Prov., Dec., 1853; asst. to chief engr., Lower Prov., Jan., 1857; dep. consulting engr., railway dept., and asst. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1860; supt. engr., Gauhati, June, 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Oudh, Feb., 1870; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow,

1871; in temporary charge of public works dept. in Baluchistan, 1879-80; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, 1880; chief engr., 1st class, Jan., 1884; retired, 1885.

DE BRETT, EDWARD ADOLPHUS, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settl. officer.

DE BROE, VICTOR EDWARD, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 22nd Jan., 1874, and posted to State Railways; exec. engr., June, 1892.

DE FABECK, WILLIAM FREDERICK, M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Entered the army med. dept., June, 1855; joined the Madras med. dept., June, 1857, and served under the mil. dept. to May, 1871, also holding various civil surgeoncies; from Nov., 1873, served in British Burma as civil surgn. and supt. of lock hospital, Maulmain; also health officer, Maulmain, Feb., 1878; dep. surgn.-gen., Nagpur force, and Central India regts., March, 1886; surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras, May, 1890; ret'd., May, 1894; served in the Crimean campaign, 1855-56 (medal and clasp), and in the Mutiny, 1859 (medal).

DEIGHTON, KENNETH, B.A., late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as prof'r., Agra coll., 19th Feb., 1862; principal, Agra coll., March, 1864; inspr. of schs., Rohilkhand divn., April, 1885; ret'd., 1890.

DE LA COURNEUVE, STEWARD HOWARD THOMAS, Burma Commn.—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1873; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1880; asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., March, 1891.

DE LAESOE, ALBERT FREDERICK, C.M.G., C.I.E., Capt., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Served in the Danish army from April, 1867; attached to foreign legion of French army, 1870-71; chevalier of legion of honour, 1871; from 1873 to 1880 served as instructor in Persian army and chief of staff coll. in Persia; apptd. translr. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Feb., 1881; attached to Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; on special duty with Sir West Ridge-way in St. Petersburg and London; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Nov., 1887; asst. commr., Merwara, July, 1889; Ajmir, May, 1892; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1893.

DE LAWAR HOSAIN AHMED, Khan Bahadur, B.A., Bengal Registrn. Dept.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; joined the service, 27th Feb., 1861, as dep. mag., and dep. collr.; apptd. inspr.-gen. of registrn., Bengal, April, 1895.

DELEVINGNE, WALTER NORMAN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DE MARSAC, FREDERICK ROOME, late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 8th March, 1864; asst. supt., Jan., 1866; supt., April, 1880; in charge, store dept., May, 1891; ret'd., March, 1892.

DE MORGAN, WALTER CAMPBELL, M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1885.

DEMPSTER, JAMES INNES ROTHNEY, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 2nd Nov., 1870, and employed on irrign. works in

the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; services lent to Egyptian govt., 1885-89; supt. engr., April, 1893.

DENMÄN, JOHN, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 4th Dec., 1880, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1891; small cause court judge, Aug., 1893; district and sessions judge, March, 1894.

DENNYS, CHARLES JOHN, Major, I.S.C., Jud. Dept., Punjab.—Arrived, 1st May, 1873; services placed at disposal of Punjab govt., July, 1881; offg. asst. cantonmt. mag., Aug., 1881; confd., Oct., 1882; cantonmt. mag., March, 1886.

DE PALEZIEUX-FALCONNET, GRENVILLE PULTENEY, Major-Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras engrs., 1852; joined the Madras public works dept., Aug., 1854, as asst. engr.; from Dec., 1854, served in the Bengal public works dept. as exec. engr., Pegu; Mhow divn., Rajputana, Nov., 1864; offg. supt. engr., mil. works, Allahabad, 1872; supt. engr., Meerut, April, 1874, and afterwards at Moradabad and Rawalpindi; offd. as inspr.-gen. of mil. works, 1883-84; ret'd., 1886.

DE RENZY, ANNESLEY CHARLES CASTRIOT, C.B., B.A., Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; entered the Bengal med. service, 1851; served through the Burmese war, 1852-53 (medal and clasp), and the Mutiny, 1857-58 (medal and clasp); apptd. first sanitary commr., Punjab, 1868; sanitary commr., Assam, 1878; served in the expdn. against the Nagas, 1879 (clasp and C.B.); dep. surgn.-gen. Punjab frontier force, 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1882; author of sanitary reports to govt. on the Punjab and Assam, and of a paper on the sanitary state of British troops in N. India.

DE ROZARIO, VICTOR PHILIP, Madras Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1858, and served in various subord. posts; district munsif, Nov., 1864; sub-judge, Oct., 1875; judge, Madras small causes court, Dec., 1892.

DE VILLE, LOUIS, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, and class, 22nd July, 1892.

DEVONSHIRE (DUKE OF), HIS GRACE SPENCER COMPTON CAVENDISH, K.G., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; attached to Earl Granville's special mission to Russia in 1856; M.P. for N. Lancashire from 1857 to 1868, when he was returned for the Radnor boroughs; LL.D., Cambridge, 1862; apptd. a lord of the admiralty, March, 1862; under sec. for war, April, 1863; sec. of state for war, Feb.-July, 1866; postmr.-gen., 1868-71; chief sec. for Ireland, 1871-74; lord rector of the univ. of Edinburgh, 1879; was returned for N.E. Lancashire in April, 1880, and became sec. of state for India; sec. of state for war, 1882-85; a royal commr. for the exhibn. of 1886; chairman of the royal commn. on the labour question, 1891; chancellor of Cambridge univ., 1892; K.G., 1892; lord prest. of the council in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895.

DEWHURST, ROBERT PAGET, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clitheroe Gr. Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 28th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.

DE WINTON, WALTER BERNARD, Madras

Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1883; in 1885-86 served temporarily on Madras state railway surveys, and in 1890-91 was employed under the inspr.-gen. of mil. works.

DEY, GEORGE GOODAIR, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at North London Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1880; also served in Assam, in 1880, as offg. judge of Sylhet and Cachar; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1887; ret'd., April, 1895.

DHANJISHA NAVROJI PARAKH, L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bombay Med. Service.—Joined the service, Sept., 1874; arrived, 5th May, 1875; employed under the mil. dept. till 1881, including service in the Persian expdn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp); served subsequently as asst. civil surgn., Poonah, and in various acting appts.; surgn. to the Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., Jan., 1887.

DHARMA RAO NAYUDU, REDNAM, Dewan Bahadur, late Madras Salt Dept.—Joined the service, 15th May, 1861, and was employed in the police dept. until April, 1869, when he was transf. to the rev. dept. as dep. collr.; personal asst. to salt commr., July, 1878; asst. commr. of salt rev., Feb., 1880; dep. commr., Dec., 1892; retired April, 1896.

DIACK, ALEXANDER HENDERSON, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen, and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1882, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settl. officer; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., Nov., 1891, to July, 1892; dep. commr. and settl. officer, Dec., 1894.

DICKENS, CRAVEN HILDESLEY, C.S.I., Lieut.-Gen., late R.A.—2nd lieut., Bengal artillery, June, 1838; asst. sec., govt. of India, public works dept., June, 1855; chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., April, 1864; sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1865, to Feb., 1877; ret'd., 31st Dec., 1878; is a visitor of Cooper's Hill College.

DICKINSON, FRANCIS BRADLEY, Forest Dept., Burma.—Joined the dept., 22nd Dec., 1872, and served in Coorg, Madras, Central Prov. and Burma as asst. and dep. conservator; conservator, Burma, April, 1894.

DIMMOCK, HENRY PEERS, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Surgn.-Major, Bombay Educl. Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; civil surgn., Sept., 1887; supt., Mahabaleshwar, Sept., 1889; obstetric physician and profir. of midwifery, Grant med. coll., June, 1891; also presidency surgn., April, 1893.

DIXON, FREDERIC PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DIXON, FREDERIC THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at London Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DIXON, JAMES, Burma Police Dept.—Apptd. inspr. of police, Sept., 1870; asst. district supt., Oct., 1877, and district supt., Jan., 1883; dep. inspr.-gen. of civil police, July, 1889.

DOBIE, WILLIAM HERBERT, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at the Merchant Taylors' Sch.; joined the dept., 23rd May, 1872; on famine duty, 1874-75; asst. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1875; from Jan. 1876, served in Bengal, as acting dep. acctnt.-gen., and as asst. comptr.-gen.; served in Madras, 1878-83, and in the Punjab, 1883-84; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1884; Punjab, April, 1885; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., March, 1886; comptr., Assam, April, 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1890; offg. comptr., Haidarabad, July, 1891.

DOBBS, HENRY ROBERT CONWAY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Winchester and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 6th Dec., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, March, 1895; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., June, 1896.

DOBIE, STANLEY LOCKER, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; supt. of lunatic asylum, Madras, Jan., 1882; served in Afghanistan and Egypt; med. officer, Utakamand, April, 1888, to Nov., 1889; supt. of lunatic asylum, Madras, Jan., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1892.

DODD, CHARLES, late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as offg. profir. Agra coll., 4th Aug., 1868; head master, Bareilly coll., Dec., 1868; profir. of English literature, Agra and Benares colls., Dec., 1874; inspr. of schools, Sept., 1884; retd., March, 1894.

DODD, CHARLES ALBERT, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived as ensign, 12th Nov., 1857, and served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); from Feb., 1860, served in the police dept.; personal asst. to inspr.-gen., N.W. Prov., March, 1861; district supt., Aug., 1861; from April, 1870, served as supt., govt. press, and curator of govt. books, N.W. Prov.; also supt. of emigration, Jan., 1883; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1893.

DODERET, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Nov., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, May, 1888; 2nd asst., April, 1890; special under sec. to govt. in the dept. of famine relief, Feb.-May, 1892; 1st asst., May, 1893.

DODGSON, CYRIL GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1883, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., forest settlt. officer, and W. Bhil agent; in 1892 acted as undersec., rev., &c., depts.; 1st asst., March, 1894.

DOIG, SCROPE BERDMORE, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 16th July, 1867, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1872; on special duty, Ahmadnagar water-supply scheme, in 1891, and again as member of Sind irrig. commn.; sanitary engr., and member of sanitary board, Jan., 1892; returned to public works dept., as suptg. engr., Oct., 1893.

DONALD, JOHN STUART, C.I.E., Punjab Commr.—Joined the service, April, 1882; extra asst. commr., May, 1884; asst. commr., Nov., 1890; accompanied mission to Kabul under Sir H. M. Durand, 1893; on special duty on the N.W. Frontier, 1894; was British commr. for demarcation of Kurram-Afghan boundary, May-Dec., 1894.

DONALD, WILLIAM, late Finl. Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 26th Oct., 1856; from 23rd May, 1859, served in the central public works office, and the civil paymaster's office, Madras, and was apptd. to finl. dept., Aug., 1862; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Aug., 1869; dir. of incorporated bank of Madras, March, 1871; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1874, and at Madras, June, 1875; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1884; retd., 1889; compiled the manual of rules and regulations for the use of junior members of Madras civil service.

DONOVAN, CHARLES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Jan., 1863; from Feb., 1864, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; served in Assam, Sept., 1879, to June, 1882, as dep. commr.; judge, Benares, Jan., 1885; retd., June, 1889.

DOSABHAIFRAMJI, C.S.I., late Presidency Mag., Bombay.—Served from 6th Oct., 1860, as income tax assessor; police mag., July, 1866; special officer for the imperial licence tax, May, 1867; collr. of income tax, and certificate tax, May, 1869; sheriff of Bombay, Dec., 1871; from June, 1874, served as second mag. of police, Bombay, and also held the posts of chairman of the municipal corporation, chairman of the justices of the peace, member of legisl. council, joint commr. of income tax, &c.; retd., 1888; author of 'Moral and Miscellaneous Essays,' 'The British Raj,' 'History of the Parsees,' and 'Travels in Great Britain.'

DOUGLAS, EDWARD WILLIAM SPENCER, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 4th Nov., 1871, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Sept., 1872; transf. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrign. branch, Nov., 1873; exec. engr., May, 1883; served on state railways in 1885-86 and 1886-87.

DOUGLAS, SHOLTO JAMES, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and inspr. of labourers; from April, 1885, served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1889; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1894.

DOUIE, JAMES McCrone, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the High Sch., Edinburgh, Univ. of Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 7th Dec., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., sec. to the finl. commr. and settlt. officer; senr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1889; dep. commr., Nov., 1891; offg. rev. sec., April, 1893, to Oct., 1894, and from Nov., 1895, to April, 1896; has publ. a transl. of the Biluchinama, with a Biloch grammar.

DOVETON, JOHN CHADWICK, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Central Prov.—First commn., Oct., 1858; asst. conservator of forests, Central Prov., 8th April, 1864; dep. conservator, May, 1865; conservator, Sept., 1868; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1894.

DOWDEN, THOMAS FREEMAN, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1856; joined the Bombay public works dept., Aug., 1859; under sec. to govt., 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, 1872; manager, Dhond and Manmad state rail-

way, Dec., 1878; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); offg. sec. to agent, Baluchistan, April, 1880; acting senr. dep. consulting engr., and under sec. to govt., Bombay, Nov., 1881; asst. to dir.-gen. of railways, 1884; suptg. engr., July, 1886; dep. consulting engr., Calcutta, April, 1886; consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Lucknow, Feb., 1887; reverted to mil. duty, June, 1892; ret'd. from the army, May, 1893.

DOWN, JAMES ERSKINE, Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service, 24th May, 1872; asst. supt., Aug., 1875; supt., April, 1876.

DOYLE, BERNARD, B.A., M.B., M.Ch., Surgn.—Major, Med. Dept., Punjab.—Apptd. to the Indian med. dept., and arrived, 21st Nov., 1876; served in mil. dept. to April, 1881, and afterwards in the Punjab as civil surgn. and dep. sanitary commr.; served in Burma, 1886-87; med. adviser to Maharaja of Jhind, Sept., 1887; civil surgn., Ambala, March, 1890; civil surgn. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Delhi, June, 1890; civil surgn., Firozpur, Oct., 1892; Peshawar, July, 1893.

D'OYLY, WARREN HASTINGS, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 28th May, 1858, and served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts.; inspr.-gen. of jails, May, 1876; mag. and collr., 1st grade, July, 1876; offg. opium agent, Behar, 1878, 1885-87, and 1889-90; offg. commr., Bhagalpur, 1884-85, and 1889; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

DRAKE, FRANCIS COURTNEY, B.A., India Office.—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, record office, 23rd Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India office, 22nd Aug., 1892; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, Feb., 1894.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, EDWARD FORSTER, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 1st Oct., 1866; arrived, 23rd April, 1867, and served under the mil. dept., Madras, and also in acting civil appts.; rest. asst. surgn., gen. hosp., and prof'r., med. coll., Madras, Oct., 1870; ophthalmic surgn., gen. hosp., and prof'r. of ophthalmic surgery and physiology, med. coll., March, 1875; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1885; ret'd., April, 1894.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, ELLIOT GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1895.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, HENRY VERNON, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 10th Dec., 1886, and served in the Central Prov. as acting under sec. and asst. commr.; commr. of excise, Aug., 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1894.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, RALPH RHENIUS EVANS, A.I.C.E., Lieut.-Col., R.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 1st March, 1864, and joined sappers and miners; from Dec., 1864, served in the public works dept.; exec. engr., March, 1870; suptg. engr., Nov., 1888; acted in 1889 and 1892-93 as joint sec. to govt. and chief engr., gen. buildings and roads branch; chief engr. for irrign., and joint sec. to govt., irrign. branch, April, 1893; chief engr., 1st class, Jan., 1896.

DRAKE - BROCKMAN, WILLIAM DRAKE, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Served in the Punjab as sub. asst. rev. surveyor, 1852-55; joined Thomason Coll., and thence was apptd. to the public works dept., April, 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov., in the buildings and roads branch; transf'd. temporarily to railway dept., 1858-60; employed on famine relief works in 1861, and 1877-78; exec. engr., March, 1874; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1892.

DREW, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 14th Nov., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag., and was permanently transf'd., June, 1885; inspr. of factories, town of Bombay, May, 1887; 2nd asst. collr., Nov., 1888; provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890; 1st asst. collr. and mag., May, 1891; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1896.

DRIBERG, JOHN JAMES STREET, late Assam Commn.—Educ. at St. Nicholas Coll., Lancing; joined the service as acting dep. mag. and dep. collr. in Bengal, Nov., 1861; confd., 16th April, 1862; from Dec., 1863, served in Assam as extra asst. commr.; asst. civil and pol. officer with the Bhutan Duars field force, 1864-65 (medal and clasp); dep. supt. of census in Assam, Oct., 1880; supt. of emigration, June, 1882; dep. commr., May, 1886; inspr.-gen. of jails, &c., Nov., 1891; in 1893 acted as commr., Assam valley districts; ret'd., April, 1896.

DRUMMOND, JAMES RAMSAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., Univ. of Glasgow, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 27th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, March, 1890; dep. commr., Aug., 1890.

DRURY, GEORGE MARSTON, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt., 30th Jan., 1878, and served chiefly on the E. Bengal state railway; traffic supt., E. Coast railway, Nov., 1891; ret'd., Dec., 1892.

DRYSDALE, ALEXANDER TOVEY, late Forest Dept., Hyderabad Administration.—Arrived, Dec., 1864; from April, 1865, served in the Central Prov. as head master of govt. sch.; from June, 1868, served in the forest dept., Berar, as asst. conservator, dep. conservator, and conservator; ret'd., Jan., 1887.

DRYSDALE, THEODORE, Central Prov. Commn.—Asst. master, govt. school, Saugor, April, 1861; served in the Central Prov. commn. from 19th Jan., 1863; apptd. extra asst. commr., April, 1864; supt. of the jud. commr.'s office, June, 1864; registrar, May, 1869; dep. supt. of census operations, Central Prov., April, 1880, and wrote the provl. report; judge, small cause court, Nagpur, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., April, 1885; cantonmt. mag., Kampti, 1885-89; divl. judge, June, 1895.

D'SOUZA, FRANCIS XAVIER, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 20th Jan., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

DU BOULAY, JAMES HOUSSEMYNE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of

1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd assist., May, 1896.

DUCAT, WALTER MARDEN, Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as second lieut., 13th June, 1856; arrived, May, 1858; asst. engr., Aug., 1859; exec. engr., Aug., 1863; suptg. engr., Nov., 1886; ret'd., April, 1890.

DUCKETT, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 31st July, 1893.

DUFF, ALAN COLQUHOUN, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.)—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 28th Dec., 1881, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., acting under sec., &c.; judge, small cause court, Oct., 1891; commr. of excise, March, 1892; dep. commr., March, 1892.

DUFFERIN AND AVA (MARQUIS OF), RIGHT HON. FREDERICK TEMPLE HAMILTON BLACKWOOD, K.P., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D.—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; specially attached to Lord John Russell's mission to Vienna in 1855; in 1860 was apptd. British commr. in Syria to enquire into the massacre of Christians, and for his services was nominated a K.C.B.; under sec. of state for India, 1864-66; under sec. for war, 1866; chancellor of the duchy of Lancaster, 1868-72; govr.-gen. of Canada, 1872-78; ambassador at St. Petersburg, Feb., 1879; ambassador at Constantinople, May, 1881; from Nov., 1882, to April, 1883, was engaged in Egypt in the settlement of the various questions arising out of Arabi's rebellion; viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 13th Dec., 1884; left India to take up post of ambassador at Rome, Dec., 1888; lord warden of the cinque ports, 1891-95; ambassador at Paris, Dec., 1891 to Oct., 1896; K.P. in 1863; privy councillor, Dec., 1868; earl of the United Kingdom, 1871, and marquis, 1888, taking the style of Ava in addn. to Dufferin by special command of Her Majesty; prest. of the royal geographical society, May, 1878; LL.D., Harvard, 1878; LL.D., Dublin, 1879; D.C.L., Oxford, 1879; G.C.B., 1883; chancellor of the royal univ., Ireland, 1886; author of 'Letters from High Latitudes,' and other works.

DUKE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal)—Educ. at Arbroath High Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892; mag. and collr., Dec., 1895.

DUKE, JOSHUA, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was employed on mil. duty and as civil surgn. at Amritsar (1881-82) and in Kashmir (1884); served in the Afghan war, Marri expedn., and with the Gilgit mission; med. officer, Malwa bhil corps and Bhopawar pol. agency, May, 1891; acted as residency surgn., Kashmir, 1889-91 and 1895; author of 'Recollections of the Kabul campaign,' and 'Banting in India.'

DUMERGUE, JOHN WILLOUGHBY FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Madras)—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. rest. in Travancore; Malayalam translr. to govt., March, 1884; head asst.,

Nilgiris, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1889; district and sessions judge, Madura, Aug., 1893.

DUNCAN, DAVID, M.A., D.Sc., LL.D. (Edinburgh), Educ. Dept., Madras.—Served in the dept. from 20th May, 1870, as prof. of logic and moral philosophy at the presidency coll., and was registrar of the Madras univ., 1875-79 and 1881-86; fellow of the Madras univ., March, 1877; principal and prof., presidency coll., from March, 1884; dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1892; member of legisl. council, Oct., 1894.

DUNCAN, HARVEY TUCKETT, C.S.I., Major-Gen.—Ensign, 46th regt., Madras infantry, June, 1848; employed in the Mofussil police, 1860; inspr.-gen. of police, British Burma, March, 1862; offg. sec. to chief commr., April, 1873; pol. rest., Mandalay, 1875-78; ret'd. from the army, Feb., 1880.

DUNCAN, PATRICK, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 3rd March, 1873, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transf. to state railways, Dec., 1873; exec. engr., May, 1883; employed on Madura-Pamban railway survey, Nov., 1894.

DUNCAN, WILLIAM ALEXANDER MACKENZIE, Indian C.S. (Assam)—Educ. at Milne's Instn., Fochabers, Aberdeen Univ., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 28th Nov., 1890, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; acted as personal asst. to chief commr. in 1893 and 1895.

DUNDAS, CHARLES LAWRENCE, Indian C.S. (Punjab)—Educ. at Eton, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 30th Dec., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

DUNLOP, ALEXANDER JOHNSTON, Berar Commn.—Apptd. asst. to cotton commr., Central Prov. and Berar, 1st May, 1869, and asst. commr., Feb., 1876; from 1869 to 1874 held charge in addn. to other duties of a govt. experimental farm; inspr.-gen., rev. dept., Nizam's govt., April, 1885, and survey and settlt. commr., Nizam's govt., April, 1887; rank of dep. commr., Berar, July, 1889.

DUNLOP-SMITH, JAMES ROBERT, M.A., Capt., Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 24th Oct., 1879; from March, 1883, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Dec., 1883; asst. commr. and settlt. collr., April, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1895.

DUNN, GEORGE OWEN WILLIAM, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1886; under sec. to govt., public works dept., May, 1894.

DUPERNEX, HUBERT EMILE LEONCE PAUL, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh)—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 31st Oct., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; author of 'The Enhanced Excise Duties' and 'Tenancies in Oudh.'

DURAND, ALGERNON GEORGE ARNOLD, C.B., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C.—First commn., 21st Dec., 1872; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal with clasp, and bronze star); A.D.C. to the viceroy, 1881-82; on special duty on N.W. frontier, 1888; British agent, Gilgit, July, 1889; in 1891 commanded Hunza-Nagar field force (medal with clasp; C.B.); mil. sec. to viceroy, Jan., 1894.

DURAND, SIR EDWARD LAW, Bart., C.B., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th Oct., 1865; arrived, June, 1866; served with the 96th regt., and was transf. to Bengal staff corps, Oct., 1868; A.D.C. and private sec. to lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, June, 1870; from Feb., 1871, served in the foreign dept. in Rajputana and Central India; acting pol. agent, Manipur, Feb.-Oct., 1877; acting asst. rest., Bushire, April, 1878; in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul, March, 1881; asst. commr., Afghan boundary commn., Oct., 1885; rest., Nepal, May, 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1893; author of 'Report on Fars and Sketch Map of part of the Country,' and 'Report on Pearl Fisheries.'

DURAND, SIR HENRY MORTIMER, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.L., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 3rd March, 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1873; from June, 1874, served in the foreign dept. of the govt. of India, and was apptd. offg. 1st asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, July, 1876; offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., Nov., 1877; pol. sec. to major-gen. Sir F. Roberts with the Kabul field force, Sept., 1879 (medal and two clasps); under sec., foreign dept., March, 1880; C.S.I., Feb., 1881; offg. private sec. to the viceroy, June, 1880; sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Feb., 1885; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1889; sent on a special mission to Afghanistan, Aug., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1894; envoy and minister plenipotentiary in Persia, May, 1894; has publ. a work on the first Afghan war, 1871, and a life of Sir H. M. Durand, 1883.

DURRANT, WILLIAM SCOTT, M.A., Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Whitgift, and Lycée St. Omer; B.A., London (with honours in French), 1888; M.A. (Classics), 1891; junr. clerk, India office, 18th Sept., 1879.

DUTHOIT, WILLIAM, D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 17th Dec., 1857; served from Sept., 1858, in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and dep. supt., family domains, Maharaja of Benares; offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn. and commr. of excise and stamps, March, 1875; mag. and collr., Shahjahanpur, April, 1877; offg. puisne judge, high court, June, 1881; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1881; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, May, 1882; employed on the opium commn., under govt. of Bengal, 1883; jud. commr. of Oudh, March, 1884; ret'd., Aug., 1886.

DUTT, RUSSICK LALL, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the civil dept. from 30th Nov., 1872, in acting appts., and as civil surgn. at various stations.

DYMOTT, DONALD FREDERICK, M.B., Surgn.-Major, Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; asst. physician, gen. hosp., July, 1884; rest. physician, March, 1885; inspr. of vaccn., and dep. sanitary commr., June, 1888; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1893; acting med. and sanitary officer, Salem, Oct., 1894; offg. jail supt., Feb., 1895; acting surgn., 2nd district, May, 1895.

DYSON, HEREERT JEKYL, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Capt., Bengal Med. Service.—First commr., 1st April, 1885; acted as civil surgn. in various stations from Jan., 1889; acting dep. sanitary

commr., Panjab, Nov., 1889; confid., Oct., 1889, sanitary commr., Bengal, Aug., 1895.

DYSON, JOHN, late Oudh Commn.—Joined the service, 8th June, 1858, as dep. mag. and dep. collr. in Bengal; from Dec., 1861, served in Oudh as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; small cause court judge, Lucknow, 1869; district judge, Faizabad, Aug., 1886; offg. commr., Sept., 1886; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, Aug., 1889; addl. jud. commr., July, 1890; ret'd., March, 1891.

EADES, FREDERICK TALBOT, Store Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Jan., 1875; staff clerk, July, 1896.

EALES, CHARLES LINDSTEDT MODELLIER, B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 4th Dec., 1878, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1887; small cause court judge, March, 1893; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1893.

EALES, HERBERT LOVELY, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 30th Oct., 1879, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; dep. commr., May, 1888; offg. sec. to chief commr., April-May, 1890; supt. of provl. census, 1890-92; offd. as prest. Rangoon municipality, April, 1896.

EARDLEY-WILMOT, SAINT-HILL, India Forest Dept.—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1873, as asst. conservator in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; dep. conservator, Nov., 1883; conservator, Oct., 1891.

EARLE, ARCHDALE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. private sec. to the lieut.-govr., Feb., 1886; offg. under sec. to govt., jud., &c., depts., Feb., 1889; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1891; mag. and collr., Jan., 1895.

EAST, WILLIAM ALBERT, M.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 26th Nov., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. registrar, dep. registrar and sealer of the high court, and asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of Panchganj, Nov., 1876; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Dharwar, June, 1881; senr. collr., June, 1885; ret'd., May, 1891.

EBDEN, EDWARD JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Sch., Ipswich, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 6th Dec., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Sept., 1872, served in the forest dept. as acting dep. conservator; on famine duty, Dharwar, 1877, and in Mysore, 1877-78; from July, 1878, served as asst. collr., railway mag., and forest settl. officer; first asst., Aug., 1881; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1888; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1890.

EDGAR, SIR JOHN WARE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; served in Cachar from Dec., 1863, as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; on special duty with the Lushai expedn., 1871; mag. and collr., Shahabad, Feb., 1878; commr. of Chittagong, Dec., 1885; chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, Aug., 1887; K.C.I.E., May, 1889;

member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Jan., 1892; ret'd., July, 1892.

EDGE, SIR JOHN, Kt., Q.C., B.A., LL.B., LL.D. (hon.), B.L., Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1866; Q.C., 1886; went the northern and north-eastern circuit, and was apptd. chief justice, N.W. Prov., Aug., 1886; vice-chancellor, Allahabad univ., 1887-93.

EDGELL, WILLIAM, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Radley; writer, India office, 31st July, 1869; temporarily employed as shipping inspr. of railway stores, Jan., 1872; receiver of wharf stores, Jan., 1873; supervisor, 2nd grade, March, 1877; 1st grade, June, 1886.

EDGERLEY, STEYNING WILLIAM, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 29th Nov., 1879, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1881, served in acting appts. in Sind; 2nd asst. collr. in Sind, 1885 and 1886; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct.-Dec., 1888; private sec. to the govr. of Bombay, March, 1889, to July, 1895; C.I.E., May, 1895; jun. collr. and mag., July, 1896; joint compiler of a manual of village accts. for Sind, 1887, which he also transld. into Sindhi.

EDGINGTON, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 21st Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

EDMONSTONE-MONTGOMERIE, CHARLES WATERTON, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Copenhagen and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 7th Dec., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; offd. as dep. commr. from June, 1896.

EDWARDES, STEPHEN MEREDYTH.—Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1895, and served in Bombay.

EDWARDS, ALFRED CLARKE, M.A., B.L., Bengal Educ. Service.—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd., 24th April, 1878; arrived, 28th Sept., 1878, and served as profr. in the presidency coll. and principal, Rajshahye coll.; principal, Dacca coll., Nov., 1887.

EDWARDS, THOMAS CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 29th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

EGERTON, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Major, I.S.C., Punjab Commn.—Joined the service, Aug., 1873; was in mil. employ till April, 1879; served in Punjab as asst. commr. from April, 1879; dep. commr., June, 1894.

EGERTON, SIR ROBERT EYLES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Edinburgh Acad., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. from Haileybury, in 1850; served in the Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer, and was in charge of the Lahore settlt., 1854-58; dep. commr., 1856; addl. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1863; commr., Nagpur, Central Prov., March, 1864; offd. as jud. commr., Central Prov., at various times from 1865 to 1867; finl. commr. of the Punjab, March, 1868; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, 1871, and again in 1872 and 1877;

lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, April, 1877, to April, 1882, when he retired from the service.

EGERTON, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 5th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl., &c., depts., April, 1895.

ELGIN AND KINCARDINE (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. VICTOR ALEXANDER BRUCE, P.C., LL.D., G.M.S.I., G.M.I.E.—Born, 16th May, 1849; succeeded his father (viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 1862-63) in the earldom in 1863; educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A., 1877); received the hon. degree of LL.D. from the Univ. of St. Andrews in 1886; was treasurer of Her Majesty's household and first commr. of works in Mr. Gladstone's third administration (1886); sworn of the privy council, Feb., 1886; apptd. lord lieut. of Fifeshire, Aug., 1886; and is also a univ. commr. for Scotland; assumed office as viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 27th Jan., 1894.

ELIAS, NEY, C.I.E., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Extra attaché, foreign dept., March, 1874; second in command, Yunan expdn., and asst. to rest., Mandalay, Sept., 1874; offg. pol. agent, Bhamo, April-Aug., 1875, in addn. to other duties; extra attaché, foreign dept., Oct., 1875; from Oct., 1877, served as joint commr. of Ladakh; on special duty to Yarkand, 1879; and to Kashgar, 1880; pol. agent with the Kashgar mission, May, 1885; C.I.E., Jan., 1888; boundary settlt. officer, Jaipur, 1887-88; asst. agent, Rajputana, March, 1888; on special duty in connection with Sikkim expdn., Nov., 1888; British commr., Anglo-Siamese boundary commn., Oct., 1889; agent to the govr.-gen. in Khorasan and Seistan, Dec., 1891; has publ'd. the 'Tarikh-i-Rashidi,' 1895.

ELIOT, JOHN, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.S., Meteorological Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, June, 1869; from 27th Dec., 1869, to Feb., 1876, served in the N.W. Prov. as profr. at Thomson and Muir colls., supt. of the meteorological observatory, Allahabad, and meteorological reporter to govt.; from Feb., 1876, served in Bengal, as profr., presidency coll., and meteorological reporter; meteorological reporter to govt. of India, May, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1897.

ELIOTT, LAWRENCE EDWARD, Capt., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., Aug., 1880; employed in Burma as asst. commr., July, 1886; offg. supt., N. Shan states, July-Nov., 1890; and from May, 1894; dep. commr., Jan., 1895.

ELLENOR, THOMAS RALPH, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872.

ELLERCAMP, HERBERT McDONALD, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, Oct., 10th 1879.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS HUGH, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 21st Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from June, 1873, to Nov., 1875, served in Berar as educ. inspr., and acting dir. of public instrn.; principal of the Raja's sch., and tutor and govr. of the Gaekwar of Baroda, Dec., 1875; C.I.E., May, 1883; on duty to England with the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1884; survey and settlt. officer, Baroda, March, 1890; ret'd., June, 1896.

ELLIOT, FREDERICK EDEN, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1858; arrived, 16th March, 1858; served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., April, 1877; district and sessions judge, March, 1881; ret'd., March, 1894.

ELLIOTT, ALGERNON, Berar Commn.—Educ. at Repton Sch.; joined the service, 16th March, 1869, as acting asst. district supt. of police, Central Prov., transf. to Berar as acting asst. commr., June, 1869; confd., April, 1874; dep. commr., July, 1889; civil and sess. judge, Oct., 1895.

ELLIOTT, SIR CHARLES ALFRED, K.C.S.I., LL.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 30th Nov., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag., and in Oudh as asst. commr.; from Feb., 1863, to Oct., 1866, served in the Central Prov. as settl. officer, Hoshangabad; served subsequently as settl. officer, Farakhabad, and as sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1870-77; commr., Meerut, Oct., 1877; famine commr., Mysore, Sept., 1877; addl. sec. to govt. of India, famine branch, May, 1878; sec. to famine commn., June, 1878; census commr., Oct., 1880; chief commr. of Assam, March, 1881; prest. of the public expenditure committee, Feb., 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1887; ordinary member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1887; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Dec., 1890, to Dec., 1895; author of 'Chronicles of Unao' and of the reports of the famine commn., 1879, and finance commn., 1887.

ELLIS, LOVEYS EDWARD, India Audit Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1894.

ELLISON, THOMAS EDWARD, late Bengal C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Coll., Bradford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 19th Oct., 1864; asst. commr., Delhi, Aug., 1865; transf. to Central Prov., Oct., 1868; judge, small cause court, Nagpur, Sept., 1883; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

ELPHINSTONE, GRAHAM WARBURTON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived in Madras, 19th Nov., 1887, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1894.

ELSMIE, GEORGE ROBERT, C.S.I., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, Oct., 1868; dep. commr., May, 1870; from 1871 to 1877 offd. as addl. commr.; civil and sessions judge, March, 1877; commr. and supt., Lahore, 1880-82, and 1885-87; judge, chief court, Punjab, 1882-85; 2nd finl. commr., May, 1887; also member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1888; 1st finl. commr., March, 1889; on special duty at Rawal Pindi durbar, March, 1885; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1893; author of 'Notes on Peshawur Crime.'

ELSTON, JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 16th Oct., 1871, and posted to state railways; asst. engr., Sept., 1872; exec. engr., May, 1883.

ELWES, VEN. WILLIAM WESTON, M.A., Ecclesi. Dept., Madras.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junr. chaplain, 20th Oct., 1870; domestic chaplain to bishop, June, 1874;

senr. chaplain, Oct., 1880; archdeacon of Madras, April, 1893.

ELWIN, EDGAR ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 7th Nov., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Jaipur estate, July-Oct., 1890; senr. asst. collr., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1891; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1896.

ELWIN, ERNEST BARNES, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 5th Jan., 1891, and served as asst. collr. and mag.

EMERSON, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ENGLISH, ALEXANDER EMANUEL, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ENTHOVEN, REGINALD EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and inspr. of factories; 2nd asst., June, 1896.

ERSKINE, GEORGE ELPHINSTONE, Col., late Kumaon Commn.—Arrived, as cornet, Bombay light cavalry, 9th Dec., 1857; employed under the mil. dept., including service with the Rajputana field force, till Feb., 1863, when he joined the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; asst. settl. officer, May, 1865; personal asst. to chief commr., Oudh, July, 1871, to April, 1878; offg. sec. to chief commr., May to Aug., 1876; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. in forest branch, and Oudh rev. dept., April, 1878; commr., Jan., 1882; on special duty to inquire into condition of tenantry in Oudh, 1882-83; wrote a report on that subject, and a digest of Oudh rev. circulars; reverted to mil. dept., Nov. 1895.

ESTCOURT, ERNEST ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 29th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ETESON, ALFRED, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Assam.—Joined the Indian med. dept. as asst. surgn., May, 1854; arrived, 30th June, 1854; held various mil. med. charges (medal for services during the Mutiny); sec. to surgn.-gen., Indian med. dept., March, 1879; dep. surgn.-gen., eastern frontier district, and sanitary commr., Assam, Jan., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1889.

EUAN-SMITH, SIR CHARLES BEAN, K.C.B., C.S.I., D.C.L., Col.—Ensign, Madras infantry, March., 1859; served in Abyssinian expdn., 1867-68 (medal); sec. to Sir F. Goldsmid's mission to Persia, 1870-72; C.S.I., Nov., 1872; private sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, on special mission to Zanzibar and Muscat, 1872-73; on special duty in India office, 1873-74; pol. agent and consul-gen., Zanzibar, May, 1875; on special duty, foreign dept., on occasion of Prince of Wales's visit to Calcutta, 1875; first asst. rest., Haidarabad, March, 1876; pol. agent, and consul-gen., Muscat, July, 1879; during the Afghan war, 1879-80, was on special pol. duty on the staff of Lieut.-Gen. Sir D. Stewart, and subsequently

with Gen. Hill's divn., and with the Kabul-Kandahar force under Gen. Roberts (medal with two clasps, and bronze star); acted as rest., Mewar, in 1882 and 1884; asst. pol. agent, Banswara, Dec., 1882; from May, 1883, served as addl. pol. agent, Bhartpur and Karauli; apptd. consul-gen. at Zanzibar, 1888; ret'd. from the army, March, 1889; K.C.B., Nov., 1890; envoy extraordinary and minister plenipotentiary at Tangiers, 1891-93; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1893.

EVANS, CHARLES THOMAS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; from Aug., 1860, served in subord. offices; apptd. to the dept., Sept., 1869, as asst. engr., and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., June, 1876.

EVANS, ERNEST SYDNEY, India Audit Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 11th March, 1895.

EVANS, HENRY FARRINGTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1880; special judge, Jhansi, July, 1882; joint mag., 1st grade, Oct., 1882; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1887; on special duty in Madras and Bombay, 1890-91, and as member of Deccan agriculturists relief commn., 1891-92; offg. jud. commr., Oudh, May-July, 1893; commr. and agent, Benares, Dec., 1893; commr., Agra, Nov., 1895; offd. as sec. to govt. from April, 1896; author of Farukhabad and Agra settl. reports, &c.

EVANS, LOFTUS GEORGE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Sept., 1886; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, March, 1891; district and sessions judge, April, 1891.

EVANS-GORDON, WILLIAM EDEN, Maj., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 15th July, 1876; from Feb., 1883, served under the foreign dept. with the Central India horse; boundary settl. officer, Central India, 1883-84; attaché, foreign dept., Oct., 1885; also translr. of foreign European languages, Aug., 1887; asst. sec., Nov., 1887; pol. officer in charge of Sardar Ayub Khan, March, 1888; asst. rest., Kashmir, Nov., 1890; joint commr., Ladakh, March, 1889; pol. officer in attendance on the Gaekwar in Europe, 1894; offg. pol. agent, Jhalawar, March, 1895.

EVERETT, STEPHEN HENRY, India Audit Office.—Educ. at Dulwich Coll.; writer, India office, May, 1872; junr. examr., India audit office, Dec., 1873; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

EVERS, BENJAMIN, M.D., C.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service, April, 1868; arrived, 27th Oct., 1868; served as civil surgn. and supt. of jail at various stations from April, 1876; ret'd., April, 1896.

EWART, CHARLES HENRY, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Punjab.—First commn., 20th Feb., 1857; arrived, 2nd April, 1857; from Feb., 1861, served in the police dept., Punjab, as district supt., and dep. inspr.-gen.; inspr.-gen., and under sec. to govt., police dept., Aug., 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1894.

EWBANK, ALFRED LLOYD VANDYKE, M.A., Educ. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 16th Jan., 1868; arrived, 28th Feb., 1868, and served as profir., Patna coll., and principal, Dacca coll.; profir., Patna coll., Oct., 1879; principal, Oct., 1881.

EYRE, GERVAS SELWYN, B.L., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., 12th June, 1869; served in mil. dept. until July, 1879, when he was apptd. asst. commr. in Burma; on duty with the Burma field force, 1887; and with the force operating against the Chins, 1888; dep. commr., May, 1888; offg. inspr.-gen. of police, 1893, and from March, 1894; royal humane society's medal for saving lives of two brother officers.

EZECHIEL, JOSEPH ALOYSIUS, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

FAGAN, PATRICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; settl. officer, Bikanir, 1892-93.

FAICHNIE, ALEXANDER GEORGE, late Postal Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1858; served for some time as supt. of the Rawal Pindi mil. horse van and bullock train; dep. postmr.-gen., Central Prov. and Berar, Nov., 1879; ret'd., 1894.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Arrived, 20th March, 1856; in civil charge of Rawal Pindi and its jail, April, 1869; civil surgn. at Gurdaspur, April, 1874, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; offd. as sanitary commr., surgn.-gen., Punjab, and inspr.-gen. of prisons; also acted for various periods as med. adviser to the Maharaja of Patiala; ret'd., 1886; author of several papers on professional subjects.

FANSHAW, ARTHUR UPTON, C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, Nov., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and inspr.-gen. of registrn.; asst. sec. to the chief commr., July, 1880; in charge of model farm, Nagpur, 1880-81; from April, 1881, served in the postal dept.; postmr.-gen., Bombay, March, 1882; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Dec., 1888; on special duty under home dept., Jan., 1889; dir.-gen. of the post office of India, May, 1889; member of the royal commn. on opium, 1893-95; C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

FANSHAW, HERBERT CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Bedford, Tonbridge, and Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 6th Nov., 1873, and was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; offg. settl. officer, Rohtak, 1875-76 and 1877; asst. to the officer on special duty in Kashmir, June to Sept., 1879; junr. sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1888; rev. sec., March, 1890; offd. for various periods as chief sec.; dep. commr., April, 1892; chief sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1895; author of the Rohtak settl. report, and 'Punjab Famine Code.'

FARDUNJI KUVARJI TARAPURVALLA, B.A., L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service 28th March, 1876, and served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1888.

FARMER, HENRY RICHARD, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Loughborough, Broms-

grove, and Uppingham Gr. Schs.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 4th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1887; ret'd., June, 1896.

FARQUHAR, WILLIAM, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 28th May, 1858; arrived, 31st Dec., 1858, and served under the mil. dept. in Madras, Burma, and the Straits Settlements; also held civil med. charge, Tounghoo, in 1859; med. officer, Utakamand, Nov., 1882; dep. surgn.-gen., Belgaum and W. districts, July, 1885; ret'd., July, 1890.

FARRAN, SIR CHARLES FREDERICK, Kt., B.L., Bombay, High Court.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1864; served as reporter, high court, Bombay, 1868-74; clerk of the crown, May, 1880; acting advocate gen., Bombay, May-Dec., 1884; acted as addl. judge of the high court, Bombay, Nov., 1886, to Nov., 1887; apptd. judge of the high court, April, 1890; chief justice, July, 1895; Kt., Jan., 1896.

FARRAN, G. H., M.A., Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Served from 17th May, 1866, as head asst. in the prothonotary's office, high court, Bombay; dep. registrar, commr. for taking affidavits, and judge's clerk, Nov., 1873; asst. commr. for taking accts. and asst. taxing master, July, 1876; master and registrar in equity, commr. for taking accts., and taxing officer, Sept., 1893.

FARRELL, GEORGE, C.B., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Punjab Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 4th Aug., 1856; dep. surgn.-gen., Sept., 1886; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Punjab, March, 1889; hon. surgn. to the viceroy, 1888-89; ret'd., Sept., 1891.

FARRER, HENRY, Indian C.S.—Educ. at Heversham Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 15th Nov., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., Madras, June, 1886.

FAULDER, CECIL JOSEPH SALKELD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 9th Dec., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1887; mag. and collr., March, 1892.

FAUNTHORPE, JOHN CHAMPION, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 26th Oct., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. under sec.

FAWCETT, CHARLES GORDON HILL, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 9th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

FAYRER, SIR JOSEPH, Bart., K.C.S.I., LL.D., M.D., F.R.S., F.R.C.P., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen.—Acting asst. surgn., R.N., Aug., 1847; asst. surgn., royal artillery, 1849; joined the East India Company's service, April, 1850, and served under the mil. dept., also holding civil surgency of Cherra Punji, 1850; served in the Burma campaign, and offd. as civil surgn., Rangoon, 1853; apptd. residency surgn., Lucknow, by Lord Dalhousie, for services during war in Burma; hon. asst. rest. at Lucknow, Sept., 1854; civil surgn., Lucknow, March, 1856; brevet rank of surgn. for services in defence of Lucknow residency during the siege, Sept., 1858;

prof. of surgery and surgn. of med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1860; med. charge of Mysore princes, June, 1860; fellow of univ. of Calcutta, March, 1861, and served for two years as prest. of the med. faculty; prest. of the Asiatic Society of Bengal, 1867; offg. surgn. to viceroy, 1869; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Edinburgh in his tour through India, 1870; hon. physician to the Queen, July, 1871; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to India, 1875-76, in med. charge of his party; ret'd., 1874; prest. of med. board, India office, 1874-95; also member of army sanitary comm., and of senate of army med. sch. at Netley; bart., Jan., 1896; author of 'Clinical Surgery in India'; 'Clinical and Pathological Observations in India'; 'Thanatophidia of India,' and other works.

FELL, GEORGE BUTLER HUNTER, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

FENNER, HENRY ALGERNON SHRAPNEL, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 20th June, 1861; exec. engr., Sept., 1868; suptg. engr., March, 1887; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt. of the Punjab, Jan.-Feb., 1892; ret'd., May, 1893.

FENTON, LAYARD LIVINGSTONE, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Bombay Pol. Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1870, and after serving under the mil. dept. was transf. to the rev. survey dept. as asst. supt., Nov., 1873; asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, March, 1889; supt. of surveys under the rajasthanik court, Dec., 1890; prest. rajasthanik court, April, 1896.

FENTON, MICHAEL WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 18th Nov., 1883, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1887, to Nov., 1891; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., July, 1892; author of 'Punjab Municipal Manual.'

FENTON, THOMAS CHARLES, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd May, 1873; attached to the store dept. till Aug., 1879, and subsequently to the registry dept.

FERARD, HENRY CECIL, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Eton, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 17th Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., April, 1893.

FERARD, JOHN EDWARD, India Office.—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; upper divn. clerk, local govt. board, 2nd Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India office, 7th April, 1893; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Feb., 1894.

FERET, CHARLES JAMES, Store Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Jan., 1875.

FERGUSON, HENRY TANNER, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the public works dept. as locomotive supt., Rangoon and Irawadi valley railway, Oct., 1879; Punjab N. state railway, Aug., 1881; dep. locomotive supt., N.W. railway, Jan., 1886; E. Bengal railway, Oct., 1887; ret'd., 1888.

FERGUSSON, RT. HON. SIR JAMES, Bart., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., C.I.E., M.P.—Born in 1832; educ. at Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxford;

entered the grenadier guards, and served in the Crimean campaign, 1854-55 (medal and three clasps); ret'd. from the army in 1855; M.P. for Ayrshire, 1854-57, and 1859-68, and for N.E. Manchester, from 1885; under sec. of state for India, June, 1866; under sec. for the home dept., July, 1867; govr. of S. Australia, 1868-73; govr. of New Zealand, 1873-74; govr. of Bombay, April, 1880, to March, 1885; parly. under sec. of state for foreign affairs, Aug., 1886; royal commr. for the Adelaide exhibn. of 1887; postmr.-gen., Sept., 1891, to Aug., 1892.

FERRAR, MICHAEL LLOYD, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Belfast Acad. Instn., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Oudh, Sept., 1865, as asst. commr.; from July, 1868, to April, 1875, served in the settl. dept.; dep. commr., April, 1883; mag. and collr., March, 1884; commr. in Oudh, Aug., 1889; commr., Gorakhpur, July, 1891; author of Sitapur settl. report, 1873; ret'd., April, 1896.

FERRIS, WILLIAM BUTLER, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Bombay Pol. Dept.—First commn., 18th Oct., 1869; in pol. employ from May, 1874; rest. and pol. agent, Somali coast, March, 1896.

FINCH, BENJAMIN TRAILL, Dir.-in-Chief, Indo-European Telegraph Dept., India Office.—Apptd. to the Indian telegraph dept., 5th Nov., 1857; transf'd. to Indo-European telegraph dept. as dep. dir. of the Persian Gulf section, April, 1875; officially designated dir., March, 1884; in pol. charge, Mekran coast, Aug., 1879; justice of the peace for Mekran, Aug., 1880; dir.-in-chief, Indo-European telegraph dept., 1st April, 1893.

FFORDE, ARTHUR BROWNLOW, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 31st Oct., 1892, and served in the N. W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.

FIDDIAN, WILLIAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Huddersfield Coll., Shrewsbury Gr. Sch., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, May, 1874; offg. commr., Sundarbans, Jan., 1879; jointmag. and dep. collr., April, 1880; mag. and collr., April, 1886; ret'd., June, 1894.

FIELD, CHARLES DICKENSON, M.A., LL.D., B.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 11th Oct., 1860; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and registrar, high court; from Dec., 1870, served in the jud. dept.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1879; member of provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1879; judge, high court, Oct., 1880; ret'd., Sept., 1886; author of 'Essays on Indian Topics,' 'Chronological Table of, and Index to, the Indian Statute Book,' and other legal works.

FIELD, GEORGE ALFRED, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; supernumerary writer, 30th May, 1859; clerk in registry dept., Sept., 1860; employed in mil. dept., 1867; asst. to registrar, March, 1882.

FIELD, GEORGE MOYLE ROBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason

Coll.; apptd., 16th Oct., 1872, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; services lent to Patiala state, 1887-93.

FILGATE, ALEXANDER JEROME, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 1857; served in the China war, 1860 (medal and clasps); joined the Madras public works dept., Oct., 1863; dep. comptr., public works accts., July, 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, and examr. of accts., Feb., 1866; from March, 1867, served in the India public works dept. as asst. acctnt.-gen.; dep. acctnt.-gen., and under sec. to govt., March, 1872; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., Sept., 1881; member of fin. committee, 1886-87; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

FILGATE, TOWNLEY MACARTNEY, Registrn. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 1st March, 1867, and served as mil. sec. to govr. of Bombay, and registrar-gen. of assurances; from July, 1871, served as inspr.-gen. of registrn.; inspr.-gen. of prisons, in addn., April, 1887; also registrar-gen. of births, deaths, and marriages, Sept., 1888.

FINCH, CHARLES THOMAS, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872.

FINK, WILLIAM ROBERT, High Court, Calcutta.—Joined the service, 12th April, 1869, as clerk to the chief justice; asst. registrar, and asst. to the clerk of the crown, June, 1875; chief clerk, Feb., 1885; 1st asst. or dep. registrar, June, 1896.

FINLAY, HERBERT BLAKEY, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, Dec., 1865, and served in the N.W.
