Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; dep. commr., July, 1885; mag. and collr., Aug., 1885; offd. as commr. in 1887, 1891, and 1893; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1893; ret'd., March, 1896.

FINLAY, JAMES FAIRBAIRN, M.A., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 17th Nov., 1875, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transf'd. to fin. dept. of govt. of India, May, 1881, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, as offg. asst. and dep. acctnt.-gen.; asst. comptr.-gen., July, 1882; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, June, 1886; dep. auditor-gen., Nov., 1887; under sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., Nov., 1887; offg. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Dec., 1887; dep. sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., April, 1889; sec., May, 1892.

FINNEY, STEPHEN, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Oct., 1874; asst. manager, N. Bengal state railway, May, 1881; and dep. manager, E. Bengal state railway, July, 1889; manager, Oct., 1892.

FINUCANE, MICHAEL, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Munchin's Coll., Limerick, Limerick Acad. and Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., manager, Raj Darbhanga, settl. officer, and offg. under sec., &c.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1884; dir., agriclt. dept., Dec., 1884; dir. of the dept. of land records and agric., March, 1888; in 1893 offd. as sec. to govt., and as sec., board of rev.; in 1894 acted as sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric.; resumed his post as offg. sec. to rev. board, April, 1894; sec.

board of rev., April, 1896; edited the Bengal tenancy act of 1885.

FIREBRACE, FREDERICK, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1861; joined the dept. as asst. engr., N.W. Prov., May, 1865; asst. principal, Rurki Coll., 1865-68; manager, Calcutta and S.E. railway, Feb., 1870; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; asst. dir. of state railways, March, 1875; manager, Rajputana state railway, Nov., 1876; on duty at Cooper's Hill Coll., 1878-79; under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Jan., 1880; suptg. engr., Feb., 1882; consulting engr. and joint sec. to govt. (railway branch), Bombay, Feb., 1887; chief engr., July, 1891; ret'd., Oct., 1894; author of a 'Manual of Surveying.'

FISCHER, FRANCIS NORTON, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 20th Dec., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

FISHER, CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 15th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

FISHER, FREDERICK HENRY, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Boston Gr. Sch., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 4th Dec., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt. and small cause judge of Dehra Dun, March, 1879; on special duty in connection with the preparation of the 'N.W. Prov. Gazetteer,' Dec., 1881; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1885.

FISHER, JOHN HADDEN, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 19th Nov., 1861; from June, 1873, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., from Jan., 1872, served in the Central Prov. as dep. commr.; and from April, 1879, in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., April, 1880; ret'd., July, 1890.

FISHER, WILLIAM ROGERS, B.A., late Indian Forest Service.—Apptd. asst. conservator, 28th Sept., 1872; arrived, 16th Dec., 1872; dep. dir. of forest sch., Dehra Dun, April, 1881, and Feb., 1884; conservator and dir. of the sch., March, 1885; ret'd., Jan., 1893; asst. prof. of forestry, Cooper's Hill Coll., from Nov., 1890; editor of 'The Indian Forester,' and author of a transln. of the 'Aménagement des Forêts.'

FITZGERALD, EDWARD AMBROSE, Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 27th July, 1859; arrived, 27th Jan., 1860, and was employed under the mil. dept.; from Feb., 1868, served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as civil asst. surgn., civil surgn., and supt. of jail; ret'd., March, 1890.

FITZGERALD, SIR GERALD, K.C.M.G.—Educ. at St. Mary's Coll., Galway, and in France; apptd. to the late ordnance office, Aug., 1855; junr. clerk, war office, Jan., 1856, and estimate clerk, 1861-63; asst. commr. for the investigation of public works accts., India, Sept., 1863; offg. dep. comptr.-gen. of mil. expenditure, March, 1864; transf. to the finl. dept., India, Feb., 1868; personal asst. to finl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, July, 1869; asst. comptr.-gen., Aug., 1869; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, April, 1871; in charge of the office of

acctnt.-gen., British Burma, Dec., 1871; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Aug., 1872; and dep. comptr.-gen., Nov., 1876; served under the Egyptian govt. from Jan., 1877, and was apptd. dir.-gen. of accts. in Egypt, Dec., 1878, and member of fin. committee, March, 1884; attached to the intelligence dept. for the mil. operations at Alexandria in 1882 (medal and bronze star); C.M.G. in 1880; 3rd class Osmanieh, 1880; 2nd class Medjidie, 1884; grand cordon Medjidie, 1885; acctnt.-gen. of the navy, June, 1885, and dep. and asst. to the parly. and finl. sec., Nov., 1885; K.C.M.G., 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

FITZGERALD, JAMES, Col., I.S.C., late Berar Comm.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, June, 1856; arrived, Sept., 1856; was employed under the mil. dept. up to 1862, including service in the Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal and clasp); and in the China war, 1860 (medal and two clasps); from Aug., 1862, served in the Hyderabad assigned districts as asst. commr., railway mag., and dep. commr.; ret'd., April, 1880.

FITZGERALD, JAMES RICHARD, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 5th Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Aug., 1875, served under the foreign dept., govt. of India, as asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., and asst. to rest., Hyderabad; held charge of the office of rest., May, 1882; from July, 1882, served in Ajmir, as asst. commr. and district mag.; first asst., govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, May, 1883; pol. agent, Oct., 1883; held charge of the agent's office in 1883-84, and of the Kalat pol. agency, 1884; from April, 1885, acted as 1st asst. and sec. for Berar, Hyderabad; ret'd., 1888.

FITZGERALD, PERCY SEYMOUR VESEY, Bombay Pol. Dept.—Joined the service, 17th June, 1871, and was employed in the Bombay rev. survey; apptd. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, June, 1878; asst. to the pol. agent, Kolhapur, Aug., 1884; resumed his appt. in Kathiawar, Oct., 1886; acting pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, in 1894, and from March, 1895.

FITZGERALD, SIR WILLIAM GERALD SEYMOUR VESEY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., India Office.—Educ. at Harrow, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1865; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, 1867-72; pol. A.D.C. to the sec. of state for India, 14th Aug., 1874; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; K.C.I.E., Aug., 1887.

FITZMAURICE, JOHN DAY STOKES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, Jan., 1884, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; under-sec., pol., &c., depts., and sec. to council, July-Nov., 1892; 1st asst. collr., May, 1894; joint judge and sessions judge, July, 1895.

FITZPATRICK, SIR DENNIS, K.C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1858; served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dep. commr., and small cause court judge, and was placed on special duty in England in connection with Begam Samru's case, March, 1869, and at Calcutta in the legisl. dept. of govt. of India, May, 1873; dep. sec. in that dept., 1874; sec. from 1877 to 1885; was also sec. to the govr.-gen.'s council; acted as judge of chief court, Punjab, in 1876-77, and as sec. to govt. of India, home

dept., Jan.-April, 1885; acting chief commr., Central Prov., Dec., 1885; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; member of the public service commn., 1887, and acting rest. in Mysore; chief commr. of Assam, Sept., 1887; rest. at Hyderabad, Aug., 1889; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, 6th March, 1892; K.C.S.I., May, 1890.

FLEET, JOHN FAITHFULL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 6th Dec., 1867, and served as asst. collr. and mag. and educt. inspr.; epigraphist to the govt. of India, Jan., 1883; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Sholapur, June, 1886; senr. collr., Nov., 1889; acting commr., Dec., 1891; commr. of customs, &c., Bombay, March, 1893.

FLEMING, ALLAN STOPFORD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Harrow, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 13th Dec., 1882, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; dep. commr., April, 1890.

FLETCHER, WILLIAM MORRIS, late Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 2nd June, 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. supt. of rev. survey; on famine duty, Nov., 1876; supt. of survey, Sept., 1879; acted as survey and settlt. commr. in 1891 and 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1893.

FOLEY, BLANCHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 14th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. finl. dept., May, 1896.

FOORD, EDWARD ARCHIBALD, Major-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Second lieut., Madras engrs., 9th Dec., 1842; arrived, July, 1844; asst. civil engr., Oct., 1846; adjutant of engrs., 1850-52; 1st class district engr. in S. Arcot and Tanjore, Dec., 1856; suptg. engr., Tanjore and Trichinopoly, May, 1863; inspg. engr. for civil and mill. buildings, Madras, Dec., 1872; suptg. engr., Kistna and Nellore, June, 1874; dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., Dec., 1875; chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., buildings and roads branch, Feb., 1877; chief engr. and sec. to govt., public works dept., April, 1877; ret'd., Dec., 1878.

FOOTE, ROBERT BRUCE, F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India.—Apptd. asst. in the geological survey, and joined, 28th Sept., 1858; served in various districts of Madras, the Nizam's territory, and Bombay; joint charge of the survey collections, Vienna exhibn., 1873; dep. supt., June, 1881; fellow of Madras univ., 1884; designation changed to supt. of survey, Sept., 1885; offd. as dir. in 1887; ret'd., Oct., 1891; state geologist, Baroda, Oct., 1891; dir. geological dept., Mysore, Oct., 1894; author of contributions to the survey publs.

FORBES, ARTHUR, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Sedbergh Sch., Yorkshire, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; asst. mag. and collr., Bardwan, Dec., 1867; offg. dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Sept., 1873; also sec. to the central relief committee, Jan., 1874; asst. commr. in Assam, Nov., 1874, to May, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1878; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1882; mag. and collr., May, 1887; offg. commr. of excise, March, 1890; 1887; offg. commr. of excise, March, 1890; commr., Patna, June, 1892; C.S.I., May, 1895.

FORBES, ARTHUR TREVOR, Indian C.S.

(Madras).—Educ. at Thame Gr. Sch., Oxford, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 18th Oct., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. registrar, high court, Feb., 1893; head asst. collr. and mag., July, 1895.

FORBES, GEORGE FORREST GREENLAW, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 28th Nov., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1889; dep. commr., Dec., 1895.

FORBES, GEORGE STUART, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch. and Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 16th Oct., 1872, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; from Sept., 1880, was employed as acting under sec. in the rev. dept., asst. sec., jud. and legisl. dept., and under sec. in the depts. under the chief sec.; from Jan., 1883, served under the govt. of India in the home dept. as offg. under sec., also in the foreign dept., on special duty, Dec., 1884, and as junr. under sec., Jan., 1885; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., March, 1887; acting 1st asst. and sec. for Berar to the rest. at Hyderabad, Dec., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1890; collr. and mag., Jan., 1891; acted as rev. sec. in 1895.

FORBES, JOHN GREENLAW, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn. as lieut., 9th Dec., 1854; arrived, 1st Feb., 1857, and served during the Mutiny, 1857-58 (medal and three clasps); served in the public works dept., Punjab, from 21st June, 1858, as asst. engr. and exec. engr., and from July, 1863, in the N.W. Prov.; on special duty in Madras, Feb., 1867, reporting on the Kaveri, Krishna, and Godavari canals; supt. of irrig. works, Oudh, Dec., 1867; offg. chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Oudh, 1872; from Nov., 1872, served as suptg. engr. in the irrig. branch in the N.W. Prov., the Punjab, and Bengal, and offd. as chief engr. and joint sec., irrig. branch, Bengal, 1877; suptg. engr. and sec. to rest., Hyderabad, Dec., 1879; chief engr. and joint sec., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, buildings and roads branch, April, 1881, and in the irrig. branch from Nov., 1881; sec. to govt., Nov., 1887; inspr.-gen. of irrig. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Dec., 1889; sec. in the same dept., Nov., 1890; ret'd., Aug., 1892.

FORBES, L. R., late Bengal Non-Regulation Commn.—Served in the jud. dept., Bengal, from March, 1864, and was apptd. asst. commr., May, 1875; dep. commr., Aug., 1887; ret'd., March, 1891.

FORBES, WILLIAM A., C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby and Haileybury Coll., from which he was apptd. in 1847; served in the N.W. Prov., Punjab, and Oudh, and as pol. officer throughout the Mutiny (medal with clasp for Lucknow); mag. and collr., Jaunpur, 1862; commr., Allahabad divn., 1872; ret'd., 1875.

FORD, ERNEST, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 6th Dec., 1890, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

FORD, WILLIAM, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1842; arrived, 7th July,

1842; attached to the N.W. Prov., and served in the grades of asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on special duty in the Sikh states, April, 1846; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, N.W. Frontier, May, 1846; served subsequently in the Punjab as asst. commr. and supt., cis-Sutlej territory; mag. and collr., Gurgaon, 1856; dep. commr., 1858; on special duty to Kashmir, 1860; commr. and supt., Hissar, Oct., 1864; Multan, March, 1865; in charge of the state of Bhawalpur, 1866; ret'd., 1869; author of a report on native govt. as contrasted with the English system.

FORDHAM, JOHN WILLIAM, late India Public Works.—Joined the dept. as asst. acctnt., July, 1859; asst. compt., July, 1869; dep. compt., Holkar and Nimach state railways, Dec., 1871; examr., Jan., 1880; ret'd., 1889.

FORREST, GEORGE WILLIAM, B.A., Dir. of Records, Govt. of India.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Dec., 1872, as head master, Surat high sch.; prof'r. of mathematics, Deccan coll., July, 1879; acting census commr. for Bombay, 1882; on special duty in connection with the Bombay records, 1884-86; prof'r. of English history, Elphinstone coll., Aug., 1887; in addn., dir. of records, Bombay, April, 1888; prof'r. of English literature, Elphinstone coll., March, 1889; officer in charge of records of the govt. of India, and of the imperial library, March, 1891; in addn., officer in charge of patent office, Jan., 1894; author of 'Selections from Bombay State Papers,' 'Selections from the State Papers preserved in the Foreign Office of the Government of India, 1772-1785,' 'The Administration of Warren Hastings,' 'Selections from the State papers relating to the Indian Mutiny,' and 'The Administration of the Marquess of Lansdowne'; has also edited 'Selections from the Writings of Mountstuart Elphinstone.'

FORREST, HENRY Telford STONOR, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 26th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

FORRESTER, EDWARD ELLIS, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

FORSYTH, WILLIAM, F.R.C.S., Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 9th May, 1876, and served at various stations as civil med. officer; health officer, Calcutta, March, 1892.

FOSTER, HENRY FREDERICK EDWARD BERTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital, and Peterhouse, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

FOSTER, WILLIAM, B.A., Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Sept., 1882; B.A., London univ., 1888; editor, India Office List, 1891-95; in 1892 assisted in editing 'The Register of Letters, &c., of the E. India Co., 1600-19,' and in 1893 prepared a catalogue of the paintings, &c., in the India office.

FOSTER, WILLIAM SHRUBSOLE, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1859; served in Madras from Nov., 1861, as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-collr.; fellow of univ. of Madras, April,

1873; collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Godavari, April, 1875; ret'd., 1885.

FOWLER, RIGHT HON. SIR HENRY HARTLEY, G.C.S.I., M.P.—Born, 16th May, 1830; educ. at Woodhouse Grove Sch., and St. Saviour's Gr. Sch.; mayor of Wolverhampton, 1863, and first chairman of the Wolverhampton sch. board; dep. lieut. for Staffordshire; has represented Wolverhampton in parliament since 1880; under sec. of state, home dept., 1884-85; finl. sec. to the treasury, 1886; prest., local govt. board, Aug., 1892; sec. of state for India, March, 1894, to July, 1895; G.C.S.I., July, 1895.

FOWLER, JAMES TOWNSEND, late Educ. Dept., Madras.—Principal, govt. normal sch., Madras, 2nd Dec., 1855; fellow of Madras univ., Sept., 1857; on special duty suptg. formation of normal schools, May, 1858; from Jan., 1859, served as inspr. of schools; on special duty to Calcutta as member of the educt. committee, 1882-83; acting dir. of public instrn., Madras, April-June, 1883; ret'd., Dec., 1888.

FOWLER, WILFRID, Madras Salt Dept.—Entered the service, March, 1879, and served in the salt dept. as asst. collr. and asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1896.

FOX, CHARLES EDMUND, B.L., Jud. Dept., Burma.—Offd. as govt. advocate, British Burma, 1879-80, and 1884-85, and as addl. recorder, Rangoon, 1885-86, and 1888; apptd. govt. advocate, March, 1886.

FOX, CHARLES EDWARD, M.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Served from 9th Jan., 1866, as master and registrar in equity, commr. for taking accts. and local investigations, and taxing officer, high court, Bombay; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

FOX, FRANCIS WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Independent Coll., Taunton, and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in the Central Prov., and from March, 1877, in Burma, as asst. commr.; supt. of central jail, Maulmain, in 1877 and 1878; transfed. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Dec., 1883, and served as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; judge of the small cause court, Allahabad, Jan., 1889; district and sessions judge, April, 1890.

FOX, GEORGE JAMES BUSCALL, Store Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th July, 1877.

FOX, HARRY CROKER, Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 12th July, 1872, and posted to Haidarabad; exec. engr., Oct., 1875; employed temporarily in mil. works dept., 1890-91, and in Burma, 1892-93; public works sec. to rest., Haidarabad, May, 1893; rank of suptg. engr., June, 1895.

FOX-STRANGWAYS, MAURICE WALTER, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 26th Nov., 1882, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settl. officer; judge, small cause court, Jan., 1892; commr. of excise, March, 1892; dep. commr., April, 1892.

FOYSTER, GERALD MOWBRAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 18th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

FRANCIS, EDWARD BELCHAM, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Repton Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 9th Nov., 1872,

and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; dep. commr., May, 1889; offg. dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1892, to Dec., 1893.

FRANCIS, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Haidarabad Administration.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1873, as asst. supt., Bombay rev. survey; divl. inspr., land records and agric., Dharwar, May, 1891; dir. of land records and agric., Haidarabad, Aug., 1891.

FRANCIS, WALTER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Repton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 11th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

FRANKLIN, BENJAMIN, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.—Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—First commn., April, 1869; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869; civil surgn. in Oudh, Dec., 1869; supt., central prison, Lucknow, July, 1876; on special duty with the viceroy, Dec., 1880; civil surgn. of Simla, Dec., 1881, to Nov., 1886; hon. surgn. to the viceroy, Jan., 1886; on special duty with the lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, Nov., 1886, to March, 1887; civil surgn. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Jabalpur, Oct., 1887; surgn. to the viceroy, Jan., 1894.

FRANKS, CHARLES NASH BAYLEY, Public Works Dept., India Office.—East India house writer, old establ., 19th Nov., 1856; junr. clerk, railway dept., India office, 1860; senr. clerk, Feb., 1875.

FRANKS, EDWARD, Mil. Dept., India Office.—East India house writer, old establ., 1st Sept., 1858; junr. clerk, finl. dept., India office, Dec., 1866; funds dept., 1867; senr. clerk, mil. dept., Aug., 1881.

FRASER, ALEXANDER, C.B., Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—and lieut., Bengal engrs., 8th Dec., 1843; joined the dept., Sept., 1850, as exec. engr., Lower Prov.; exec. engr., Arakan, 1853, and subsequently at Rangoon; garrison engr., presidency, Feb., 1856; under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., 1859; supt. of lighthouses, 1860; chief engr., Feb., 1862; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1862; chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., British Burma, Aug., 1867; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov., May, 1871; sec. to govt. of India in the public works dept., Jan., 1879, to Feb., 1882.

FRASER, ALEXANDER THOMAS, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 2nd March, 1860, and served from that date in the dept.; exec. engr., Oct., 1866; transfld. to Central Prov., Dec., 1866; and to Rajputana, June, 1867; posted to N.W. Prov., mil. works branch, April, 1872; served also in Jhansi and Mysore; returned to Madras, as exec. engr., March, 1881; acted as dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt., public works dept.; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1894; ret'd., June, 1895.

FRASER, ANDREW HENDERSON LEITH, C.S.I., M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at the Acad., Instn., and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1871, and served in Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to the chief commr., April, 1877; commr. of excise, &c., Aug., 1881; junr. sec. to the chief commr., and dir. of agric., Feb., 1882; sec. to the chief commr., April, 1890; commr., Jan., 1891; member of hemp drugs commn., 1893-94; C.S.I., Jan., 1897.

FRASER, EDWARD ALEXANDER, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 13th Sept., 1864; served under the foreign dept. from July, 1874, as asst. rest. in the Persian Gulf, asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti, and asst. to rest., Haidarabad; 2nd asst. agent in Central India, May, 1878, to April, 1879; British joint commr., Ladakh, April, 1884; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1884; asst. rest. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, April, 1886; addl. pol. agent, and guardian to the heir apparent of Jodhpur, Dec., 1886; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, and to the chief commr., Ajmir-Merwara, June, 1887; pol. agent, Alwar, Oct., 1891; acting pol. agent in Jhalawar, Jan., 1895; and in E. states of Rajputana, Feb., 1895; ret'd., Sept., 1896; has transld. the 'Tanszimat-I-Hasanah.'

FRASER, HENRY, Col., late Police Dept., Central Prov.—First commn., Madras cavalry 1854; arrived, 14th March, 1854; asst. to gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., Nagpur, July, 1860; joint mag., Nagpur and Berars, Aug., 1861; from Nov., 1861, served in the police dept., Central Prov.; acted as inspr.-gen., July-Aug., 1868; 1st class district supt., Nov., 1885; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

FRASER, HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 15th Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; registrar of high court, N.W. Prov., April, 1889; dep. commr., March, 1892; mag. and collr., April, 1892.

FRASER, JAMES DENHOLM, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 21st Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

FRASER, STUART MITFORD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 25th Nov., 1884, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; tutor and guardian to the Raja of Kholapur, the Chief of Kagal, and Kunvar Saheb of Bhaunagar, May, 1889, to April, 1894; 1st asst., Sept., 1895; tutor to Maharaja of Mysore, May, 1896.

FRASER, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 6th Dec., 1894, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

FRAZER, ROBERT WATSON, B.A., LL.B., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Rathmines Sch., Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served as asst. collr. and mag., engr. and manager of the Parla Kimeedi estate, and acting dep. dir. of rev. settl.; ret'd., 1886; author of 'Silent Gods and Sun-steeped Lands.'

FREMANTLE, SELWYN HOWE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 27th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, and asst. commr., joint mag., Jan., 1896.

FRENCH, FRANCIS COOPE, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Marlborough and Eton, and at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 20th Dec., 1889, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and personal asst. to chief commr.; joint mag., March, 1896.

FRIZELLE, JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Dunganon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 20th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov., as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Punjab, May, 1864, and served as asst. commr.; jud. asst., July, 1876; dep. commr., April, 1877; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; judge, chief court, Punjab, May, 1889.

FROST, CHARLES EVITT, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Kensington Gr. Sch., and abroad; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst., Dec., 1884; first asst. collr., Aug., 1887; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; senr., June, 1894.

FRY, HENRY JAMES WAKELY, B.A., Store Dept., India Office.—Junr. clerk, 18th Aug., 1873; B.A. (honours in logic and moral phil.), London univ., 1875; senr. clerk, July, 1896.

FRY, THOMAS DUNCAN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., July, 1896.

FRYER, SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM RICHARDS, K.C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at foreign schs., and at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; jud. asst., April, 1877; on special duty with the Quetta field force, 1878, and 1879; dep. commr., Jan., 1882; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, May, 1883; commr. in Burma, Aug., 1886; finl. commr., Burma, June, 1888; C.S.I., May, 1890; retransfcd. to Punjab, and apptd. commr. and supt., March, 1891; offg. chief commr., Burma, May, 1892, to May, 1894, when he returned to the Punjab as offg. finl. commr.; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Dec., 1864; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895; chief commr. of Burma, April, 1895; author of the Dera Ghazi Khan settlt. report.

FRYER, WILLIAM HERBERT, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892.

FULLER, JOSEPH BAMPFYLDE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (N.W.Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 13th Nov., 1875; and served in the N.W. Prov., as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. dir. of agric. and commerce, Feb., 1878; transfed. to Central Prov., July, 1882; asst. sec. to chief commr., Aug., 1882; commr. of settlts. and agric., Oct., 1885; also junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1890, to Dec., 1891; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; offg. commr., July-Oct., 1894; re-transfcd. to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as district and sessions judge, Jan., 1895; mag. and collr., March, 1895; author of 'A Primer of Agriculture,' 'Field and Garden Crops of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,' 'The Central Prov. Settlt. Code,' 'Progress of the Central Prov., 1862-92,' &c.

FULLERTON, JOHN CAMPBELL, M.B., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Foreign Dept., Govt. of

India.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1874; med. officer, gen. hosp., Calcutta, 1874-75, and 1875-76; acting civil surgn., 1877 and 1878; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80; med. officer, Baluchistan agency, Aug., 1885; agency surgn., Quetta, and administrative med. officer for Baluchistan, April, 1891.

FULTON, EDMUND MCGILDOWNY HOPE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., branch inspr.-gen. of registrn. and educ. inspr. in Sind, and agent for sardars in the Deccan; registrar of the high court, Bombay, Feb., 1881; judge and sessions judge, Feb., 1886; served in Lower Burma as jud. commr., May, 1891 to Nov., 1892; acted as judge of the high court, Bombay, 1892-93, 1894-95, and in 1896.

FURLONG, MALCOLM, Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—Joined the Madras army as ensign, and arrived, 10th Feb., 1857; served at the capture of Copul, 1858, and with the Bellary movable column, 1859 (medal); personal asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Burma, June, 1865; served in the Burma commn. from Aug., 1867; dep. commr., May, 1884; prest., Maulmain municipal committee, Jan., 1885; reverted to mil. dept., 1891.

TURNIVALL, W. C., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., May, 1869, as supt. engr. on the Delhi and Rewari railway; under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Oct., 1873; offg. dir. of railways, central system, and joint sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov., May, 1877; joint sec. and offg. chief engr., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1879; dir. of construction for state railways, March, 1880; chief engr., and sec. to chief commr., British Burma, Oct., 1880; services lent to the Bengal Central and Bengal and N.W. railway cos., May, 1883; Nizam's guaranteed railway co., March, 1884; ret'd., 1886.

GABBETT, JOSEPH EDWARD, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1877, and posted as asst. engr. to state railways; exec. engr., July, 1892.

GABBETT, POOLE ROBERT, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col.—Joined the service in Oct., 1871, and served in Madras, in England (Woolwich), at Gibraltar, and in Egypt with expedit. force; surgn. to the govt. of Madras, Jan., 1891, to May, 1892; senr. medl. officer, Barbados.

GAFFNEY, JOHN BURKE, late Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Med. Dept., Central Prov.—Apptd. to the Indian med. service, 1st Oct., 1866, having previously served as asst. surgn., home service, 1864-65; was employed under the mil. dept., including service in the Nicobar expdn., 1867; house surgn., med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, March, 1868; from June, 1868, served in the Central Prov. as civil asst. surgn., civil surgn. and jail supt., &c.; ret'd., Oct., 1894.

GAHAN, HENRY HERBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 18th Sept., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Dholpur state, 1883-86; offg. dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, Lucknow, May, 1893; supt. of works, Wazirabad-Lyallpur state railway, Dec., 1894.

GAISFORD, GILBERT, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1868; from May, 1870, served with

the Punjab frontier force, and took part in the Jowaki expedn., 1877-78 (medal), and the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); asst. supt. of levies in Baluchistan, April, 1883; asst. pol. officer on expedn. to Zhob, Sept., 1884; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta and Pishin, Feb., 1891.

GAIT, EDWARD ALBERT, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1882, and served as asst. commr. in Assam; provl. supt. of census, 1890, and wrote the provl. report; acted as sec. to the chief commr., 1891-92 and 1895-96; dep. commr., Nov., 1892; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1893.

GAITSKELL, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer.

GALBRAITH, EDGAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt. and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, joint mag., asst. commr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., April, 1884.

GALTON, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Radley, Magdalen Gr. Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 10th Nov., 1863; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., asst. commr. (Nilgiri Hills), and sec. to the board of rev.; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1883; collr. and mag., Oct., 1884; acting commr. of salt and abkari rev., April, 1886; acting member, board of rev., April, 1887; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1888; acting chief sec., and member of legisl. council, Jan. to April, 1893; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

GAMBLE, JAMES SYKES, M.A., F.L.S., India Forest Dept.—Educ. at Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. conservator of forests, Oct., 1871; arrived, 27th Dec., 1871, and served in British Burma and Bengal; asst. to the inspr.-gen. of forests, 1877-79; dep. conservator, Bengal, July, 1880; conservator, Sept., 1882; transf. to Madras, Dec., 1882; transf. to N.W. Prov. as conservator of sch. circle, and dir. of the forest sch., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1890; author of a 'Manual of Indian Timbers.'

GAMBLE, REGINALD ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 30th Dec., 1884, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to finl. dept., govt. of India, as acting asst. compt. of India treasuries, Nov., 1889; acting dep. acctnt.-gen., Allahabad, June, 1890; under sec. in the fin. and commerce dept., Dec., 1894.

GANPATRAO DAMODHAR PANSE, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. probr. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr. and mag., 13th Dec., 1881; confd., 5th June, 1884; 2nd asst. Dec., 1890; 1st asst., June, 1894.

GARBETT, COLIN HUBERT, Col., I.S.C., Bengal Non-Regulation Commn.—First commn., 27th June, 1859; from 4th Dec., 1866, served in Bengal as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1879.

GARBETT, HUBERT, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., Nov., 1854, and was

employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1860; supt. of works, Derajat circle, Punjab, 1870; suptg. engr., July, 1873; ret'd., 1890.

GARDINER, JOHN WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Lycée Impérial Bonaparte, Paris; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 24th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., Dec., 1880; dep. commr., Nov., 1885; also settl. officer, 1887-88; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

GARDINER, RICHARD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., 8th Jan., 1868; 2nd asst. engr., Rajputana state railway, 10th Nov., 1870; exec. engr., W. Rajputana section, Feb., 1875; dep. manager, July, 1882; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Nov., 1886; manager and engr.-in-chief, Bhaunagar-Gondal railway, June, 1887; also charge of Morvi railway, June, 1887; ret'd., April, 1893.

GARRETT, ABRAHAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 19th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GARRETT, ALFRED HOWARD, Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., May, 1883.

GARRETT, ANNESLEY JOHN, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Haidarabad Administration.—Educ. at Marlborough; first commn., 29th May, 1866; mil. sec. to the rest., Haidarabad, March, 1890, to March, 1895.

GARRETT, CHARLES BAZETT, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived in India, 2nd March, 1858, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., district and sessions judge, and mag. and collr.; judge of the principal court of small causes in Nuddea and Jessor, Dec., 1882; district and sessions judge, 1st grade, Feb., 1883; ret'd., March, 1893.

GARRETT, HENRY WILLIAM, Pol. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at the King's Sch., Ely; clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 12th April, 1882; transf. to pol. dept., June, 1890.

GARRETT, JAMES HUGH ELIOT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Reading Gr. Sch., Clifton Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 25th Nov., 1887, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1894.

GARRETT, ROBERT VERNON, Major, I.S.C., Haidarabad Administration.—Entered the service, 11th Feb., 1875; regimental duty with 54th Foot till Jan., 1879; entered staff corps Jan., 1879; with 5th and 4th Infantry, Haidarabad contingent to June, 1887; asst. commr., June, 1887; special asst. commr., June, 1895; dep. commr., May, 1896.

GARSTIN, JOHN HENRY, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 1st Dec., 1857; served in rev. and jud. depts., Madras, and was apptd. private sec. to Lord Napier, March, 1866; collr. and mag., S. Arcot, and pol. agent, Pondicherry, May, 1871; served as acting sec. and addl. sec. to govt., rev. dept., 1876-80; C.S.I., May, 1878; collr. and mag. and agent, Vizagapatam, March, 1880, to April, 1881; board of rev., July, 1882; apptd. to revise the establs. of collrs. and divl. officers, Feb., 1883; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1886; second member of board of rev. and commr. of land rev., April, 1887; member of council of

govr. of Madras, Jan., 1889; acting govr. of Madras, 1st Dec., 1890, to 23rd Jan., 1891; retd., Jan., 1894; author of a 'Review of the Madras Famine of 1876-78'; and compiler of the 'District Manual of S. Arcot,' 1878.

GARSTIN, NORMAN ANTHONY, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1857, as dep. collr. and mag., Bengal; transf. to Oudh, Sept., 1859, as extra asst. commr.; asst. district supt. of police, Dec., 1863; district supt., Jan., 1867; retd., Dec., 1893.

GARTH, RIGHT HON. SIR RICHARD, Kt., M.A., Q.C.—Educ. at Eton, and Christ Church, Oxford; called to the bar in Lincoln's Inn in 1847, and went the home circuit; M.P. for Guildford, 1866-68; nominated chief justice, Calcutta, March, 1875; retd., March, 1886; sworn of the privy council, Feb., 1888.

GARTHWAITE, LISTON, B.A., late Educ. Dept., Madras.—Served from 8th April, 1857, as head master of various schs., and as dep. inspr. of schs.; from Sept., 1869, served as inspr. of schs.; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1884; retd., Dec., 1888; compiled Kanarese and Malayalam arx. for the sec. of state, and also various text-books.

GARWOOD, JOHN FREDERICK, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Indian Mil. Works Dept.—First commn., 17th April, 1866; arrived, 17th Feb., 1872, and served in the public works dept. in the N.W. Prov.; suptg. engr., Sept., 1890; on field service in the Zohb valley, 1890; acted as suptg. engr., Baluchistan civil works, and public works sec. to agent, in 1886, 1890, and 1892; transf. to Bombay command, March, 1895; and to headquarters as dep. dir., April, 1895.

GASKIN, LIONEL EDWARD PALMER, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.)—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

GATES, FRANK CAMPBELL, B.L., Indian C.S. (Burma)—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; from Aug., 1884, served in Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, March, 1887; junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1887; under sec., Oct., 1887; dep. commr., Aug., 1889; junr. sec., March, 1891; sec., March, 1893; rev. sec., Jan, 1895.

GATHERER, ARTHUR BRANDER, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 8th Nov., 1864, and served in the public works dept., Madras and Mysore; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; asst. sec. to rest., public works dept., Hyderabad, Dec., 1885; suptg. engr., March, 1889; transf. to Burma, April, 1889.

GAULD, WILLIAM, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 28th Dec., 1895.

GAUNTLETT, MAGER FREDERIC, Indian C.S. (Bengal)—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 27th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer.

GAY, EDWARD, M.A., late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. asst. in the fin. dept., Sept., 1865; asst. comptr.-gen. of accts., June, 1867; acting dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1868; dep. comptr.-gen., Calcutta, Sept., 1869; acting acctnt.-gen., Bengal, 1874-75; acctnt.-gen.,

Bombay, and commr. of paper currency, April, 1879; comptr. and auditor-gen. and head commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1888; retd., Nov., 1891.

GEAKE, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Bengal)—Educ. at Plymouth Coll. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 25th Nov., 1886, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1893.

GEE, HERBERT WALTER, Indian C.S. (Punjab)—Educ. at Sherborne Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Nov., 1887, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. officer, Tochi, April, 1896.

GEIDT, BERNARD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bengal)—Educ. at Shrewsbury; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 30th Nov., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr. and personal asst. to the chief commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1894.

GELL, RIGHT REV. FREDERICK, D.D., Ecclesl. Dept., Madras.—Fellow of Christ Coll., Cambridge; nominated lord bishop of Madras, 16th May, 1861; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1861; acting metropolitan bishop, Calcutta, March, 1876, to Feb., 1877.

GEOGHEGAN, HENRY THOMAS, B.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the dept., Nov., 1868, as exec. engr., Punjab N. state railway; suptg. engr., Indus valley railway, May, 1877; offg. consulting engr. to govt. of India for state railways, Feb., 1879; engr.-in-chief, Bhopal railway, June, 1879; and Madras state railway surveys, Feb., 1885; retd., 1890.

GEORGE, EDWARD CLAUDIUS SCOTNEY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Burma)—Educ. at Dulwich and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 7th March, 1887, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; C.I.E., Jan., 1894.

GERRARD, ARTHUR STAPLES, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 2nd Aug., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., July, 1881; offg. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, Lucknow, 1892-93.

GHOSE, MAHIMOHUN, Indian C.S. (Madras)—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Bombay, St. Peter's Coll., Exmouth, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 11th Feb., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

GIBB, MALCOLM COUPER, LL.B., Indian C.S. (Bombay)—Educ. at Harrow, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 11th Dec., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Nov., 1890; 1st asst., Feb., 1893.

GIBBONS, JAMES BARRY, Surgn.-Major, Bengal Med. Dept.—First commn., 1st Oct., 1881; from Aug., 1884, acted as civil surgn.; rest. physician, med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, Feb., 1885; supt., Campbell med. sch. and hosp., Sealdah, Jan., 1896.

GIBSON, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Madras)—Educ. at St. Alban's Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty in Karnul, 1876-78; acting under sec. to govt., Feb., 1880; private sec. to govr. of Madras, Dec., 1880; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1882; sec. to the board of rev., Jan., 1884; collr. and mag., Sept., 1888; member of board of rev., Nov., 1893; member of legisl. council, Jan., 1894; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1894; retd., Dec., 1896.

GIBSON, FREDERICK EDWARD, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Bath Gr. Sch., London Univ., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nellore, Oct., 1883; retd., Aug., 1887.

GIBSON, HENRY WILLIAM, B.L., late Oudh Commn.—Volunteer in the Meerut light horse, 1857–58; served subsequently in the mil. police, 1858–61; asst. district supt. of police, Oudh, June, 1861; district supt., April, 1863; from July, 1864, served in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; acted as supt. of encumbered estates, Sitapur, 1874; dep. commr., Nov., 1877; retd., April, 1894.

GIBSON, J. R., late Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1862; arrived, Sept., 1860, and served as asst. supt., asst. settl. officer, and dep. supt.; from 1881 to 1885 served in Egypt, being attached to the intelligence dept. during the campaign of 1882 (medal and Khedive's star); supt., rev. survey, Ratnagiri, May, 1885; from 1885 to 1890, was again employed under the Egyptian govt., and received the order of the Osmanieh; resumed his post in May, 1890, but returned immediately to Egypt; retd. from the dept., June, 1892.

GILCHRIST, WILLIAM GILCHRIST, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., Aug., 1870, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; services lent to S. Indian railway co., 1887–88, and to Nilgiri railway co., 1890–91; in charge of Kalka-Simla railway survey, March, 1892; engr.-in-chief, Arsikere-Mangalore railway survey, Nov., 1894.

GILES, ARTHUR HENRY, Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 14th Feb., 1863; district supt., Jan., 1866; dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1890; served temporarily under the govt. of Ceylon in 1888; has publd. report on police of Bombay, Madras and Calcutta, 1887; and report on administration of Police, Ceylon, 1889.

GILES, EDWARD, M.A., Bombay Educl. Dept.—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; arrived, 14th May, 1873, and served as prof. of history and pol. economy, Elphinstone coll., Bombay; educl. inspr., May, 1875.

GILES, FRANK, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov., as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag.; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1886; offg. sec. to board of rev., Sept., 1887; senr. asst. commr., May, 1888; offg. commr., Kumaun, 1889, 1891, and 1893; dep. commr., Oct., 1891; acted as legal remembrancer in 1893; member and sec. of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1893, to Jan., 1894; supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1896.

GILES, ROBERT, M.A., Sind Commn.—Arrived 31st Dec., 1868; asst. settl. officer, Indus survey, March, 1869; served from Jan., 1870, as dep. collr., supt. of police, and educl. inspr. in Sind; 2nd asst. collr. in Sind, July, 1884; dep. commr., Dec., 1885; coll. and mag., Nov., 1895.

GILL, JOHN EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 23rd Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1887; asst. commr., Nov., 1889; settl. officer, March, 1890; joint mag., July, 1890; district and sessions judge, March,

1893; author of 'Gazetteer of the Ghazipur District.'

GILLAN, Rev. G. G., M.A., late Ecclesi. Dept., Bengal.—Asst. chaplain of the Church of Scotland, 4th Jan., 1866; chaplain, Jan., 1876; senr. chaplain of the church of Scotland in Bengal, and of St. Andrew's church, March, 1880; retd., 1889.

GILLAN, ROBERT WOODBURN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Ayr Acad. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; offg. city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, March, 1894; asst. commr., May, 1896.

GILLESPIE, JAMES THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Newry Intermediate Sch., the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 21st Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

GILLILAND, JOHN HOWARD, B.A., Bengal Educl. Dept.—Apptd., 15th June, 1877, and served in the presidency and Howrah colls. as prof. of civil engr.; prof. in presidency coll., Nov., 1888; has acted as second asst. meteorological reporter to the govt. of India.

GILLMAN, HERBERT FRANCIS WEBB, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 1st Dec., 1888, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov. 1895.

GIRINDRA NARAIN DEB, KUMAR, Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr., Bardwan, Aug., 1878; apptd. under the native civil service rules proby. asst. mag. and collr., Dec., 1880; confd., 19th Feb., 1886; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892.

GLADSTONE, CECIL ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in the Punjab as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. commr.; on special duty at Delhi at the imperial assemblage, Dec., 1876; transf. to Burma as dep. commr., Sept., 1886; offd. as commr., April, 1889; retransf. to the Punjab, as dep. commr., Dec., 1889; retd., Sept., 1894.

GLASS, JAMES GEORGE HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—2nd asst. engr., Central Prov., 27th June, 1867; exec. engr., April, 1871; sec. to chief commr., public works dept., Central Prov., July, 1885; suptg. engr. and sec., Aug., 1888; C.I.E., May, 1890; chief engr. and sec., Jan.-March, 1893; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, buildings and roads branch, March, 1894; transf. to Bengal in similar capacity, Jan., 1896.

GLAZIER, EDWARD GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at N. London Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861; from April, 1862, served in Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Oct., 1879; retd., 1891; author of 'Report on the district of Rungpore,' 'Further Notes on the Rungpore Records,' 'Report on the Famine in Rungpore.'

GLENNY, WILLIAM HENRY, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Newry Sch., Royal Sch., Dunganon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.;

collr. and mag., Karnul, April, 1880; acting dir. of rev. settl. and agric., Oct., 1885; collr. and mag., N. Arcot, May, 1886; ret'd., 1888.

GLOSTER, JOHN CLERKE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Midleton Coll., Cork, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1838; arrived, 15th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

GODFREY, CHARLES WILLIS, Col., I.S.C. Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at foreign schs.; arrived, 6th Sept., 1858, and served in the Bombay artillery; from Aug., 1864, served in the dept. as asst. supt., and was promoted to supt. of survey, Jan., 1885.

GODFREY, FRANCIS EDWARD, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. comptr., and posted to Bombay, 1st April, 1871; dep. examr., Jan., 1880; examr., Jan., 1882; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Dec., 1892; examr. of telegraph accts., April, 1894.

GODFREY, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, May, 1874; served in Assam, 1874-80, and again from Oct., 1883; dep. commr., Jan., 1885; acting inspr.-gen. of police, &c., April, 1892; judge and commr., Assam valley districts, Oct., 1892.

GODLEY, SIR ARTHUR, K.C.B., Under Sec. of State, India Office.—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; Hertford scholar, 1868; Ireland scholar, 1870; Eldon law scholar, 1874; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1876; private sec. to the first lord of the treasury, 1872-74, and again, 1880-82; commr. of inland rev., 1882-83; C.B., 1882; permanent under sec. of state for India, 30th Sept., 1883; member of the Indian currency committee, 1892-93; K.C.B., Jan., 1893.

GODSELL, WILLIAM, Auditor, India Office.—Junr. asst., India audit office, 30th Sept., 1860; junr. inspr., Dec., 1868; asst. to the auditor, Oct., 1892; auditor, Jan., 1895.

GODWIN-AUSTEN, HAROLD, Berar Administration.—Educ. at the Royal Acad., Gosport; joined the service, 27th Feb., 1877, and held offg. appts. at Port Blair and in the Nicobars; on special duty with the joint commr. of Ladakh in 1880; extra asst. supt., Port Blair, Feb., 1883; transfld. to Berar as asst. commr., Nov., 1889; special asst. commr., June, 1893; dep. commr., June, 1895.

GOLDINGHAM, JOHN DALRYMPLE, late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1853; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-judge; judge of small causes, July, 1862; civil and sessions judge, Oct., 1868; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1872; ret'd., Sept., 1888.

GOLDSMITH, SEPTIMUS JESSE, Surgn.-Major.—Joined the Bombay med. service, April, 1873; arrived, 1st Nov., 1873; served as civil surgn. in N. Kanara and Gujarat; agency surgn., Bhaghelkhand, Sept., 1875; also tutor to Maharaja of Rewah from Oct., 1883; afterwards in med. charge of Bhaghelkhand agency and dispensaries; ret'd., March, 1891.

GOLDWYER-LEWIS, VEN. ALEXANDER, B.A., B.D., Ecclesl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service as chaplain, 19th May, 1875; senr. chaplain, May, 1885; archdeacon of Bombay and commissary, Oct., 1890.

GOMPERTZ, HENRY JAMES COLLEY, late Mad-

ras Rev. Survey Dept.—Apptd., 10th Aug., 1859; dep. supt., 1st class, July, 1887; employed in 1838 on special duty on boundary questions, Pudukottai, and in 1889, Karikal and Tanjore; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

GOMPERTZ, RICHARD LOUIS DALZIEL, India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 9th Nov., 1870, and employed on various lines in Bombay, Bengal, Madras, &c.; supt., Oct., 1887.

GOOD, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; suptg. engr., Feb., 1895.

GOODFELLOW, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, V.C., Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, July, 1853; 2nd lieut., June, 1855; arrived, March, 1857; served with the Central India field force, 1857-58 (medal and clasp, V.C.), and with the Okhamandal and Kathiawar field force, 1859; joined the dept. as special asst. engr., harbour defences, Bombay, April, 1860; exec. engr., Feb., 1862; served in the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-68 (medal); suptg. engr., May, 1884; chief engr. for irrign., Sept., 1887; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1891.

GOODFELLOW, WILLIAM WEST, C.B., Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—2nd lieut., Bombay engrs., Dec., 1851; arrived, Oct., 1853; joined the dept., July, 1854; served with the Persian expdn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp), and with the Abyssinian expdn., 1867 (C.B. and medal); exec. engr., Bombay defences, 1869-70; served subsequently at Aden and in the Poona districts; suptg. engr., June, 1876; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bombay, public works dept., April, 1885; returned to Europe, 1887.

GOODRICH, HARRY ST. AUBYN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., dep. dir. of rev. settl., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; collr. and mag., March, 1882; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1884-85, and 1885-86; ret'd., Feb., 1890.

GOODRICK, G. M., late Bengal Customs Dept.—Served from 30th Nov., 1859, as preventive officer and appraiser, Calcutta custom house, and was apptd. asst. collr. of customs, July, 1871, and dep. collr. of Calcutta, March, 1880; also supt. of excise rev., collr. of stamp rev., and a collr. under the licence tax act; ret'd., 1889.

GOODRIDGE, JOHN PERCH, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1869, and served as asst. commr., &c., in the Central Prov.; on famine duty in Madras, Sept., 1877; dep. commr., Sept., 1889; in addn. pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Dec., 1889; offd. as commr. of settlts. and agric. in 1889; and as commr., Chhattisgarh, in 1890; addl. sessions judge, Jan., 1892; ret'd., July, 1895.

GOPAL DAS, Rai Bahadur, late Punjab Commn.—Served in the settl. dept. and subord. jud. dept. of the Punjab from 6th July, 1851; extra jud. asst., Oct., 1888; ret'd., 1889; author of a 'Manual for preparation of Records of Rights' and of histories of the Gujranwala and Peshawur districts.

GOPENDRA KRISHNA DEB, KUMAR, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Served in the subord. exec. service, Bengal, from 27th

Jan., 1876, and offd. as registrar of Calcutta and inspr. of registrn. offices; apptd. under the native civil service rules, Nov., 1882, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1891; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1894; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1895.

GORDON, ALEXANDER EVANS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Bengal Non-Regulation Commn.—First commn., 18th April, 1865; from May, 1872, served in Bengal as asst. commr.; on special duty with the Maharaja of Johore, Dec., 1875; cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge, Dum-Dum, March, 1876; tutor to the Maharaja of Darbhanga, June, 1877; on depn. to Kuch Behar, Dec., 1883; dep. commr., March, 1887; acted as jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, in 1893.

GORDON, DONALD CLUNES, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. in 1868, as asst. comptr. of accts., N.W. Prov.; served subsequently as dep. comptr. in the Punjab; comptr., July, 1874; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., Bombay, 1877-79, and afterwards in Madras; examr., public works accts., Madras, May, 1881; in same capacity in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1887; and in the Punjab, March, 1891.

GORDON, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Radley Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1893; transfid. to Bengal as joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1894.

GORDON, HAMILTON WINKUP, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1879; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1881; has offd. at various periods as judge of high court, Calcutta.

GORDON, HUNTLY PRYSE, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at New Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., small cause court judge, and asst. commr.; collr. and mag., Dec., 1878; ret'd., Oct., 1887.

GORDON, JOHN JAMES HOOD, C.B., General, Indian Staff Corps.—First commn., 21st Aug., 1849; entered Bengal Staff Corps, 29th May, 1861; has received medals for the Indian mutiny, Jowaki, Afghan and Burma campaigns; asst. mil. sec. for Indian affairs at Horse Guards, Oct., 1890; member of the Council of India, Jan., 1897.

GORE, ST. GEORGE CORBET, Major, R.E., Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1870; arrived, 15th Nov., 1872; from March, 1873, served on the great trigonl. survey of India; on service in Afghanistan, 1879-80, with Sir D. Stewart's column on march from Kandahar to Kabul, as officer in charge of survey; attached to Afghan boundary commn., Aug., 1884; dep. supt., March, 1886; supt., trigonl. surveys, Dehra Dun, May, 1894.

GORST, RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN ELDON, Q.C., M.P.—Graduated at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, as 3rd wrangler, 1857; M.A., 1860; fellow of his coll.; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1865; Q.C., 1875; solicitor-gen. in Lord Salisbury's first administration, 1885-86; under sec. of state for India, in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886, to Nov., 1891; finl. sec. to the treasury, 1891-92; was British plenipotentiary at the labour conference at Berlin, 1890; lord rector of Glasgow

univ., 1893; vice-prest. of committee of council on educn. in Lord Salisbury's third administration July, 1895; author of 'The Maori King.'

GOSS, LOUIS ALLAN, Educ. Dept., Burma.—Held non-gazetted appts. from 5th Aug., 1874, to 7th April, 1878, when he was apptd. junr. inspr. of schs.; senr. inspr., March, 1893; in 1894, acted in addn. as dir. of public instrn.; author of 'The Story of Wethan-da-ya.'

GOUDGE, JOSEPH ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Taunton, London Univ., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

GOUGH, ARCHIBALD EDWARD, M.A., late Educ. Service, N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the dept., 2nd Nov., 1868, and served as profr., Benares coll., and Muir coll., Allahabad; served in Bengal, 1878-86, as profr. in the Hugli and presidency colls., and principal of the Calcutta Madrassa; principal, Muir coll., Allahabad, July, 1886; ret'd. July, 1895; author of 'Papers relating to the Collection and Preservation of the Records of Ancient Sanskrit Literature in India,' 1878, &c., &c.

GOULDSBURY, DUDLEY ELPHINSTONE, Lieut.-Col., Punjab Administration.—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1866; arrived, 1st March, 1867; served in mil. dept. to June, 1882, when he was transfid. to the Punjab as offg. cantonmt. mag.; re-transf. to mil. dept., Oct., 1882; resumed his appt., May, 1883; confd., March, 1887.

GOVAN, GEORGE MONCRIEFF, M.D., L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.E., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., Dec., 1851; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1852, and served under the mil. dept. in Bengal, Burma, and Assam, and throughout the Bhutan campaign, 1864-66 (medal and clasp for Burma); from Jan., 1883, served under the govt. of the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as civil surgn., &c.; ret'd., March, 1887.

GRACE, CHARLES HALLYBURTON, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., as ensign, Madras infantry, March, 1854; served in the Central Prov. from Sept., 1859, as asst. commr., dep. commr., and settl. officer; acted as commr. of Jabalpur, 1882; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

GRACEY, HUGH KIRKWOOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at City of London Sch., London Univ., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 17th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1894.

GRACEY, THOMAS, Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Educ. at Enniskillen Royal Sch.; 1st commn., 17th Dec., 1862; 1st asst. engr., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; engr.-in-chief, Pindi-Kohat section of Punjab N. railway, 1879; engr.-in-chief, provl. railways under construction, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1882; sec. to chief commr., public works dept., Burma, April, 1887; suptg. engr., Feb., 1888; offg. consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec. to govt., railway branch, Madras, July, 1890; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for railways, central divn., June, 1891; in similar capacity, Lucknow, June, 1892; chief engr., June, 1892; dir.-gen. of rail-

ways, and dep. sec., railway branch, Aug., 1895.

GRAHAM, ROBERT ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Winchester, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 11th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

GRAHAME, WILLIAM FITZWILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and offg. under sec.

GRAHAME, WILLIAM FRANCIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 4th Jan., 1864, and served in the Madras rev. and jud. depts.; on special duty in 1868 with Dr. Janssen in connection with the solar eclipse; collr. and mag., June, 1882; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1882.

GRANT, ALEXANDER, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., July, 1867, and employed in N.W. Prov. and Oudh on irrig. works; exec. engr., March, 1873; under sec. to govt., irrig. branch, in 1888; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Oct., 1895.

GRANT, ALEXANDER, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 15th Oct., 1873, and posted to mil. works; transfed. to Punjab, Nov., 1880; exec. engr., May, 1883; transfed. to Burma, Aug., 1889; and to Assam, May, 1894; to Haidarabad, Oct., 1895.

GRANT, ALFRED HAMILTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 9th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GRANT, ARTHUR JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Berkhamstead Gr. Sch., Dulwich Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 17th Nov., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and offg. under sec. to govt.; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., March-Nov., 1892; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., Oct., 1894; pol. officer, Wano, March, 1896.

GRANT, SIR CHARLES, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Harrow, Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Haileybury Coll., from which he was apptd. in 1858; arrived, 8th April, 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Central Prov.; sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., Nov., 1861; commr., Nov., 1870; acting jud. commr., Aug., 1877; acting chief commr., April to Nov., 1879; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1879; acting sec., govt. of India, home, rev., and agricl. depts., May, 1880; foreign sec., govt. of India, Sept., 1881; ret'd., Feb., 1885; compiled the 'Central Provinces Gazetteer.'

GRANT, CHARLES, Major-Gen., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 4th Jan., 1857; arrived, 6th Feb., 1857; special officer in charge, Kathiawar local battalion, and extra asst. pol. agent, Dec., 1867; in charge of state prison, Asirgarh, March, 1870; asst. rest., Persian Gulf, June, 1871, and acted as rest. in 1877; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, April, 1878; pol. agent, Muscat, Feb., 1880; supt., Rajputana railway police, Dec., 1880; from Feb., 1881, served as cantonmnt. mag., Nimach; ret'd., Jan., 1887.

GRANT, DONALD ST. JOHN DUNDAS, Surgn.-Major, M.A., M.B., Punjab Med. Dept.—Joined

the service, 1st Oct., 1881; served in the Punjab from Oct., 1886, as civil surgn.; chemical examr. to govt., and profrr., Lahore med. coll., Sept., 1891.

GRANT, GEORGE, Brigade-Surgn., M.B., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 10th Feb., 1859; arrived, 11th July, 1859; from June, 1860, served under the govt. of the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn.; offg. supt., govt. press, and curator of govt. books, N.W. Prov., and chemical examr., Aug., 1866; supt. of new central prison, Farakhabad, Dec., 1866; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons and civil hosp., N.W. Prov., in 1877, 1879, and 1882; supt., Bareilly jails, Dec., 1877; dep. sanitary commr., 1st circle, Dec., 1883; ret'd., Oct., 1885.

GRANT, GEORGE FRANCIS MYTTON, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Brighton; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Dec., 1864, and served as asst. collr. and mag., branch registrar-gen., and educt. inspr. in Sind; in charge of the Sachin state, May, 1870; junr. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Dec., 1882; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Thana, March, 1885; senr. collr., Sept., 1886; commr., N. divn., April, 1890; ret'd., Nov., 1890.

GRANT, GREGORY COLQUHOUN, Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th Nov., 1853; on mil. duty till Nov., 1860, including service with the Okhamandal field force in 1859; served subsequently as dep. collr. and mag. in Sind; judge and sessions judge, March, 1865; also acted as jud. commr. in Sind, and judge of the sadar court; reverted to mil. dept., 1890.

GRANT, J. G. GARROW, Dr., late Emigration Dept., Bengal.—Served in the dept. from 22nd May, 1863, as med. inspr. and protector of labourers, Kushtha; protector of emigrants and supt. of labour transports, Calcutta, Feb., 1871; supt. of emigration, Calcutta, Jan., 1874; ret'd., 1880.

GRANT, JOHN ANDREW, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; served under the foreign dept., govt. of India, at Kuram, 1880; settlt. officer, Dec., 1883; offg. registrar, chief court, June, 1885; settlt. officer, Feb., 1888; ret'd., 1896.

GRANT, JOHN HAY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 6th Feb., 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., and asst. rev. and police commr.; junr. collr., Sept., 1877; municipal commr., Bombay, Nov., 1877; collr. of Bombay, and supt. of stamps and stationery, Aug., 1883; special commr. for Bombay for international exhibn. at Calcutta, 1883; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

GRANT, JOHN PYNE, Col., I.S.C., late Rev. Survey of Mysore.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1856; joined the rev. survey, 1860; supt., Jan., 1868; ret'd., Jan., 1890.

GRANT, THOMAS, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 28th May, 1875.

GRANT, TREVOR JOHN CHICHELE, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 27th Jan., 1858; served in Bengal as asst. and joint mag.; mag. and collr., May, 1874; offg. sec., board of rev., April, 1874; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

GRANT-BROWN, GEORGE EUSTACE RIQU, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch.,

and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

GRANT-DUFF, RT. HON. SIR MOUNTSTUART ELPHINSTONE, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S.—Born in 1829; educ. at Edinburgh, and at Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A., 1853); called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1854; represented the Elgin burghs in parliament from 1857 to 1881; under sec. of state for India, Dec., 1868, to Feb., 1874; under sec. of state for the colonies, April, 1880, to Aug., 1881; govr. of Madras, Nov., 1881, to Dec., 1886; was lord rector of the univ. of Aberdeen, 1866-72; author of 'Studies in European Politics,' 'Elgin Speeches,' 'A Political Survey,' &c.

GRAVES, SOMERSET HENRY PAUL, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma Police Dept.—First commn., 2nd Sept., 1868; served in the 26th native infantry, and in June, 1885, was apptd. supt. of police, Port Blair; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; reverted to mil. duty, 1888.

GRAY, EVELYN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Prov.)—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 27th Dec., 1878, and served as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settl. officer; transf. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Dec., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892.

GRAY, MALCOLM ALEXANDER, B.A., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Assam Commn.—Ensign, 3rd (Buffs) regt., 7th Nov., 1868; arrived, 1st Feb., 1870; served in the Assam commn., from Aug., 1873, as offg. asst. commr. and asst. inspr. of immigrants; confid. as asst. commr., July, 1877; forest settl. officer, Oct., 1885; dep. commr., Sept. 1888.

GRAY, PERCY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 30th Dec., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; junr. sec., board of rev., April-July, 1885; joint mag., Sept., 1887, and asst. commr., Feb., 1890; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, May, 1891; registrar, high court, March, 1892; dep. commr., March, 1894; mag. and coll., Nov., 1895.

GRAY, ROBERT, M.B., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1859; arrived, 11th March, 1860, and served in the Punjab from Feb., 1863, as civil asst. surgn., jail supt. and surgn. at various stations; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Sept., 1884; ret'd., April, 1891.

GRAY, WELLINGTON, L.M., L. Ch., Brigade-Surgn.—Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 31st March, 1866; arrived, 9th Nov., 1866, and served as profir. and principal, Grant med. coll., surgn. at the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., curator of Victoria and Albert museum, and sec. to the surgn.-gen.; ret'd., April, 1893.

GREANY, JOHN PHILIP, M.D., Surgn.—Lieut.-Col., Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1875, and was employed on mil. duty, and in various acting civil posts; apptd. civil surgn., July, 1883; acted as dep. sanitary commr. in 1888 and 1893.

GREAVES, SIR GEORGE RICHARDS, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., Gen.—First commn., 30th Nov., 1849; served in the Mutiny, the Yusufzai expdn. of 1858 (medal with clasp), and the war

in New Zealand, 1862-66 (medal); chief of the staff in the Ashanti expdn. of 1873-74 (medal with clasp and C.B.), and in the Soudan campaign in 1885 (K.C.B., medal with clasp); commander-in-chief of Bombay army, and second member of council of govr. of Bombay, March, 1890; resigned, April, 1893; ret'd., from the army, June, 1896.

GREAVES, RICHARD HERBERT, late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 19th Jan., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; transf. to Assam as district and sessions judge, Sylhet, Oct., 1887; ret'd., July, 1896.

GREENE, WALTER JAMES, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Writer, 1st July, 1867; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878; clerk for exam. of army non-effective accts., March, 1885.

GREENSHIELDS, ROBERT SCOTT, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 9th Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam, March, 1882, as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to the chief commr., March, 1886; offd. as dir. of land rev. and agric. in 1889; dep. commr., Nov., 1889.

GREENSTREET, WILLIAM LEES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Oct., 1864; arrived, Jan., 1870; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1871, as asst. engr., N.W. Prov.; transf. to mil. works branch, 1872; exec. engr., Nov., 1874; suptg. engr., July, 1885; took part in the Hazara expdn., 1891; chiefengr., Sept., 1893; posted to Bombay command, April, 1895.

GREER, RICHARD TOWNSEND, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Erasmus Coll., Drogheda, and Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 10th Dec., 1877, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and asst. sec. to chief commr.; transf. to Bengal, Nov., 1888, as joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1893.

GREEVEN, RICHARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dulwich and at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 1st Dec., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1891; asst. supt. and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, 1892-93; acted as under sec. to govt. in 1894, and from July, 1895; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1895; author of 'Heroes Five.'

GREGG, WILLIAM HENRY, M.B., D.P.H., Brigade-Surgn.—Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1869; served in the Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp); civil surgn., Dinajpur, 1873, Jessor, 1875, Hughi, 1876-86; lecturer on botany, Hughi coll., 1877-86; offg. protector of emigrants, Calcutta, April, 1888; sanitary commr., Bengal, Jan., 1889; civil surgn., Burdwan, April, 1896.

GREIG, GEORGE, late Forest Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived, 20th Sept., 1857, as a lieut., 93rd Sutherland highlanders; ret'd. from the army, June, 1865; joined the forest dept., Dec., 1868, and was apptd. dep. conservator, March, 1871, and conservator, Nov., 1876; ret'd., 1880.

GREY, LEOPOLD JOHN HERBERT, C.S.I., Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 3rd Jan., 1857; asst. commr. in Assam, Feb., 1865; offg. civil officer with Bhutan Duar field force, Dec., 1865; from April, 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., but was employed under the govt.

of Bengal, 1868-69; pol. agent, Bahawalpur state, 1871-73, and 1877; jud. asst., July, 1876; on special duty in connection with imperial assemblage, 1876-77; dep. commr., Nov., 1876; sec. to board of examrs. (under govt. of India), March, 1882; commr. and supt., Delhi divn., March, 1886; offd. as first finl. commr. in 1891; and as second in 1893; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1894.

GREY, WILLIAM FRANCIS HUNGERFORD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., Aug., 1868; served in Burma from April, 1874, as asst. commr., and offg. asst. sec.; boundary officer, Paungde, Aug., 1881; cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, Sept., 1882; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; offg. commr., April, 1896.

GRIERSON, GEORGE ABRAHAM, C.I.E., B.A., Ph.D., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Bees, Shrewsbury, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 25th Oct., 1873; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and small cause court judge; on famine duty, 1874; on special duty in the rev. dept., Dec., 1882; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1884; mag. and collr., March, 1890; acting opium agent, Bihar, April, 1896; C.I.E., May, 1894; hon. Ph.D. (Halle), Aug., 1894; has publ. various works in connection with the Behari language and its dialects, translins., collections of folk songs, &c.; also 'Curiosities of Indian Literature,' 1885; 'Vidyapati and his Contemporaries,' 1885; 'Modern Vernacular Literature of Hindustan,' 1890; and 'Inscriptions of Piyadasi,' 1891.

GRIERSON, JOHN, Col., I.S.C., late India Public Works Dept.—2nd lieut., Bombay artillery, Dec., 1857; arrived, 24th Jan., 1858; joined the dept., Feb., 1864; exec. engr., Jan., 1871; from May, 1872, served in various parts of India as dep. controller of accts., dep. examr., and examr. of accts.; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1894.

GRIESBACH, CARL LUDOLF, C.I.E., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India.—Apptd. asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1878; arrived, 11th Nov., 1878; on special duty with troops in S. Afghanistan, 1880; employed on Afghan boundary commn., 1884-86; dep. supt., Nov., 1886; on depn. as geologist to the Amir of Kabul, 1888-89; supt., June, 1889; dir., July, 1894; author of numerous contributions to the survey publ.
