GRIFFIN, HENRY DALY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at French Coll., Blackrock, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1889; joint mag., July, 1890; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1896.

GRIFFIN, SIR LEPEL HENRY, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 17th Nov., 1860, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1870; supt., Kapurthala state, April, 1875; dep. commr., April, 1877; sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1878; on special duty at the Paris exhibn., July, 1878; during the occupation of Afghanistan, held the special appt. of chief pol. officer in north and west districts of that country (medal); rest. and agent to the govt.-gen., Central India, Feb., 1881; K.C.S.I., May, 1881; royal commr., Indian and colonial exhibn., 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1889; author of 'Punjab Chiefs,' 1865, 'The Law of

Inheritance to Chiefships,' 1859, 'The Great Republic,' 1884, and 'Famous Monuments of Central India,' 1886.

GRIFFITH, HUGH, Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Burma.—Joined the Indian med. dept. (Madras establ.), 1859; apptd. civil surgn. and health officer, Rangoon, April, 1871; also supt. of lunatic asylum, June, 1871, and supt. of vaccn., May, 1875; ret'd., May, 1890.

GRIFFITH, RALPH THOMAS HOTCHKIN, C.I.E., M.A., late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived 1st Dec., 1853, and served as prof. and head master, Benares coll.; principal, Benares coll., Jan., 1861; supt. of observatory, Benares, May, 1866; inspr.-gen. of schs., N.W. Prov., March, 1878 dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1879; ret'd., 1885.

GRIFFITHS, WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., Educn. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 1st July, 1869; arrived, 20th Jan., 1870, and served as asst. prof. and prof., presidency coll.; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1879; principal, Hugli coll., April, 1880; principal, presidency coll., Dec., 1892.

GRIGG, EDWARD EVANS, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Infantry, Kumaun Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, Dec., 1861; served under the mil. dept. till Jan., 1864, and subsequently as asst. commr. in Assam up to March, 1867, when he was transf. to the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1855; offg. commr., 1892, and from April, 1894; commr., Kumaun, Nov., 1895.

GRIMES, HENRY ROBERT, late Jail Dept., Madras.—Asst. dep. commr. of paper currency, Vizagapatam, 29th Nov., 1864; from April, 1866, served as supt. of the central jail, Coimbatore; inspr.-gen. of jails, Madras, July, 1884; ret'd., April, 1880.

GRIMLEY, REGINALD FEARNS, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 9th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

GRIMLEY, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; junr. sec. to the board of rev., 1877-79; mag. and collr., March, 1879; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1881; served under the finl. dept. from April, 1885; commr. of income tax in Bengal, 1886-87; commr., Chota Nagpur, Oct., 1889; member, board of rev., Calcutta, April, 1896; author of 'The Sea Customs Law,' 'Tariff Act, with notes,' an 'Income Tax Manual,' 'Manual of Rev. Sale Law, and Certificate Procedure.'

GRINDLAY, WALTER, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 18th Dec., 1881, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; ret'd., 1888.

GRISH CHANDER CHAUDHURI, B.L., Bengal Jud. Dept.—Served from 29th March, 1866, in non-gazetted appts.; subord. judge, Dec., 1877; small cause court judge, Hugli, Nov., 1891; Midnapore, Sept., 1895.

GROSE, JAMES, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 7th Oct., 1860; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., under sec. to govt., and receiver of the Carnatic property; fellow of Madras univ., May, 1868; sec. to board of rev., 1870; collr. and mag., Nellore,

March, 1877; acting dir. of rev. settlt., Nov., 1886; on special duty to Delhi, March, 1888; acting inam commr., May, 1882; member of legisl. council in 1888, and 1891-92; member of board of rev., and commr. of land rev., Jan., 1889; member of exec. council, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1896.

GROVES, HENRY SAMUEL, B.A., Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service as prob., finl. dept., July, 1873; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Nov., 1890; Madras, April, 1893; offg. comptr., Central Prov., June, 1893.

GROVES, HERBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 23rd July, 1868, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; transfed. to Central India, Oct., 1882, and employed on state railways; transfed. to Burma, May, 1887 (medal, with clasp); services lent to Mysore state, July, 1890; under sec. to govt. of Mysore, railway branch, July, 1891; joint sec., July, 1892; transfed. to Burma, April, 1895; suptg. engr., Nov., 1895.

GROWSE, EDWARD FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 18th Nov., 1881, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1890; mag. and collr., July, 1894; mag. of Cuttack, Dec., 1895.

GRUNING, JOHN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GUBBINS, FREDERICK BEBB, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1835; served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr. of Benares, 1852; civil and sessions judge, Benares, 1857; commr. of rev. and circuit, Benares, agent, and supt. of ex-rajah of Coorg, 1858; ret'd., 1863.

GUN, WILLIAM HENRY MAFFETT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Greenock Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 17th Nov., 1871, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1883; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1888.

GUNGARAM, Rai Bahadur, M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 2nd Oct., 1873, and posted to Punjab provl. establ.; exec. engr., May, 1883.

GUNTER, AUSTIN HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Dover Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GUPTA, INANENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 12th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GUPTA, KALI PADA, M.A., M.B., F.R.C.S., Surgn. - Lieut. - Col., Bengal Medical Dept.—Joined the service, 13th June, 1872, and served as supt. of vaccn., and on gen. and jail duty; in 1888 acted in addn. as sanitary commr. for Bengal; civil surgn., July, 1891.

GURDON, EVELYN PULTENEY, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 2nd April, 1852; from Aug., 1860, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., dep. commr., and small cause

court judge; addl. commr., Nov., 1881; commr. and supt., Aug., 1884; divl. judge, Oct., 1885; ret'd. from civil employment, Jan., 1890.

GURDON, PHILIP RICHARD THORNHAGH, Capt., I.S.C., Assam Commn.—Joined the army, 2nd Sept., 1882; apptd. asst. commr. in Assam, 2nd June, 1886; dep. commr., Feb., 1895.

GURDYAL SINGH, Sirdar, Punjab Commn.—Probr. under native civil service rules, 26th Jan., 1880; asst. commr., Jan., 1882; district judge, Nov., 1892.

GÜTERSLOH, FREDERIC NICHOLAS, late Railway Branch, Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., June, 1874, as asst. engr. on the Indus valley state railway; manager, Wardha valley railway, Dec., 1878; locomotive supt., Rajputana railways, March, 1880; ret'd., June, 1891.

GWYTHER, HENRY THOMAS, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll., Roorkee; apptd. as apprentice engr., 21st March, 1874, and posted to Bengal; asst. engr., March, 1875; transfed. to State Railways, Aug., 1875; exec. engr., Oct., 1889.

HABIB-ULLAH, SAIYID, Maulvi, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov., under the statutory rules, 14th Feb., 1887; joint mag., Sept., 1892; asst. commr., Nov., 1895.

HACKET, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, late Geological Survey Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst., 15th Nov., 1861; served in the Sone valley, on the Vindhya, in Central India, and in Jabalpur and S. Rewa; designation changed to dep. supt.; ret'd., July, 1888.

HACKETT, ARTHUR LUKE, Brigade-Surgn. - Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 1st April, 1867; arrived, 26th Oct., 1867; served under the mil. dept., and in various acting civil appts.; from Oct., 1878, served as civil surgn. at various stations, health officer and supt. of lock hosp., Madras, &c.; ret'd., May, 1895.

HADDON, HENRY EDMONDS, B.A., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to State Railways; exec. engr., May, 1888.

HAIG, CHARLES THOMAS, Major-Gen., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Obtained his first commn. from Addiscombe, June, 1854; lieut., Bombay engrs., May, 1856; served with the Persian expdn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp), and with the Rajputana field force, 1857 (medal and clasp); asst. exec. engr. at Nimach, May, 1858; apptd. as asst. in the great trigonl. survey of India, Sept., 1859; served with the Okhamandal field force in 1859; dep. supt., Oct., 1867; on depn. as commr., Venice geographical exhibn., 1881; dep. surveyor-gen. in charge of trigonl. survey branch, Feb., 1887; ret'd. from civil employment, 1890; and from the army, April, 1891.

HAILEY, HAMMET REGINALD CLODE, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.

HAILEY, WILLIAM MALCOLM, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HAINES, SIR FREDERICK PAUL, G.C.B.,

G.C.S.I., C.I.E., Field-Marshal.—Entered the army as ensign in 1839; mil. sec. to Sir Hugh Gough in the war on the Sutlej, 1845; was present at the battles of Mudki and Firozshah (medal and clasp); served through the Punjab campaign of 1848 and 1849, and was present at Ramnagar, the passage of the Chenab, and the battles of Chilianwala and Gujrat; served with the 21st fusiliers through the campaign of the Crimea, 1854-55 (medal with four clasps, 5th class of the Medjidie and Turkish medal); K.C.B., 1871; G.C.B., 1877; commander-in-chief of the Madras army, and member of the govr.'s council, May, 1871, to 1875; commander-in-chief of the forces in India, with local rank of gen. and extraordinary member of the govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1876, to 1881, and directed the mil. operations of the Afghan campaigns of 1878-79 and 1879-80 (G.C.S.I.; thanked by both houses of parliament, 1879 and 1881).

HAINES, HERMANN ANDERSON, Pol. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 15th Nov., 1881; pol. dept., 1886; précis writer, Dec., 1889, to Dec., 1894; senr. clerk, Feb., 1894.

HALL, CHARLES FREDERICK, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Budaun, Feb., 1883; retd., Dec., 1886.

HALL, GEOFFREY CRAYTHORNE, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Jail Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as asst. surgn., Bengal med. establt.; served under the mil. dept. until March, 1875, when he entered civil employ as offg. civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Jhansi; supt., central prison, Allahabad, Nov., 1877; inspr.-gen. of prisons, July, 1896; author of three med. works.

HALL, MONTAGU STANLEY, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Writer, India office, 21st Jan., 1875; clerk, 2nd class, Oct., 1878.

HALL, WILLIAM THOMAS, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Rossall, Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 5th Dec., 1878, and served in British Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dir. of land records and agric., Oct., 1887; on special duty to Perak tin mines in 1888-89; dep. commr., Nov., 1892; acting prest., Rangoon municipality, March, 1894; offg. commr. and sessions judge, April, 1896.

HALLEN, JAMES HERBERT BROKENCOTE, C.I.E., F.R.C.S., Veterinary Lieut.-Col., late Civil Veterinary Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 23rd May, 1850; acted as principal veterinary surgn., Abyssinian field force, Dec., 1867; prest. of the Indian cattle plague commn., Nov., 1869; inspecting veterinary surgn., Bengal army, July, 1872; ditto, horse-breeding operations, May, 1875; inspr.-gen., civil veterinary dept. in India, April, 1892; C.I.E., June, 1893; retd., May, 1894; author of 'Manual of the more deadly forms of Cattle Disease in India,' and the report of the cattle plague commn.

HALLETT, HENRY HONEYWOOD HUGHES, Col., I.S.C., Police Dept., Central Prov.—First commn., July, 1858; arrived, 11th Aug., 1858; served with the Bundelkhand field force, 1859; from 26th Dec., 1868, served in the Central Prov. as personal asst. to chief commr., asst.

commr., &c., and was apptd. supt. of the sch. of industry and central jail, Jabalpur, Aug., 1878; also supt. of reformatory sch., June, 1891; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Oct., 1894.

HALLETT, JOHN REVANS, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Univ. of Melbourne; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 17th Oct., 1864; asst. mag. and collr., Bardwan, May, 1865; inspr. of schs., Aug., 1870; second inspr. of registrn. offices, March, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1878; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1880; retd., Feb., 1890.

HALLIDAY, SIR FREDERICK JAMES, K.C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. in 1824; arrived, 8th June, 1825; served in Bengal as junr. asst. to the agent in the Saugor divn. and asst. registrar of the sadar court; joint mag. and dep. collr. in Bundelkhand, and afterwards in Noakhali and Balua, 1831-35; mag. and collr. at Dacca, Feb., 1835; mag., collr., and salt agent, Cuttack, Feb., 1836; sec. to the sadar board of rev., April, 1836; sec. to the govt. of Bengal, jud. and rev. depts., May, 1838; junr. sec. to the govt. of India, rev., jud. and legisl. depts., in addn. to his other duties, March, 1840, to 1843; sec. to the govt. of India, home dept., 1849; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1853; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, May, 1854; retd. in 1859; member of council of India, Sept., 1868, to Dec., 1886.

HALLIDAY, FREDERICK MYTTON, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 30th Oct., 1856, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; offd. as opium agent, Benares, in 1876 and 1877; commr., April, 1878; chairman, port commrs., Calcutta, April, 1887; offd. at various periods as addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council; member of board of rev., April, 1889; retd., Nov., 1891.

HALLIFAX, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 28th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Nov., 1892.

HALLIFAX, CHARLES JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and All Souls' Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 31st Dec., 1888, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; counsellor to the Raja of Suket, 1891-93.

HALLIFAX, HENRY FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

HAM, WILLIAM JAMES, Postal Dept. Govt. of India.—Joined the service, and was attached to the sea post office, Bombay, 6th May, 1868; supernumerary inspr., Oct., 1877; dep. postmr.-gen., Central India, May, 1880, and at Calcutta, attached to the railway mail service, April, 1882; asst. dir.-gen., June, 1887; inspr.-gen., railway mail service, Sept., 1890; has acted as postmr.-gen. at Lucknow, Calcutta, Madras, N.W. Prov. and Oudh.

HAMBLIN, RICHARD EBB, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and Christ Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 4th Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., June, 1887; joint

mag., March, 1890; dep. commr., April, 1893; mag. and collr., Sept., 1894.

HAMILTON, FRANK SUMNER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 10th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892.

HAMILTON, RIGHT HON. LORD GEORGE FRANCIS, M.P.—Born Dec., 1845; educ. at Harrow; ensign, rifle brigade, 1864; transfld. to the Coldstream guards, 1868; was elected member for Middlesex, Dec., 1868, and represented that county in parliament until the election which followed the reform bill of 1885, when he was returned for the Ealing divn.; under sec. of state for India, Feb., 1874; vice-prest. of the committee of council on educn., April, 1878; first lord of the admiralty in the Marquis of Salisbury's first and second administrations, 1885-86, and 1886-92; chairman, London school board, 1894-95; sec. of state for India in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895.

HAMILTON, RICHARD ERRIS, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., 1st Aug., 1868; served as asst. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., and British Burma, asst. compt.-gen., and asst. commr. of paper currency; offg. asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., March, 1882; compt. and dep. commr. of paper currency, Central Prov., Jan., 1883; compt., India treasuries, Nov., 1891; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, April, 1894; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1896.

HAMILTON, ROBERT CLIFTON, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

HAMILTON, THOMAS SHARP, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Old Trafford Sch., Manchester, Chester Coll., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 6th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, April, 1890.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM HENRY, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 1st Nov., 1893.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM ROBARTS, B.L., Police Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1869; asst. to the rev. and police commr., Oct., 1869; dep. collr., May, 1874; city mag., Sept., 1874; Huzur dep. commr., July, 1876; presidency mag., Jan., 1888; author of a police manual, and works on local self-govt. and village customs.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM STIRLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HAMMICK, MURRAY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 18th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Coorg as asst. commr. and mag.; from Jan., 1883, served in the Madras secretariat as asst. sec., jud. and legisl. depts., and acting under sec. in the rev. dept.; head asst., S. Kanara, April, 1885; acting postmr.-gen., Madras, 1886; sec. to the commrs. of land rev., Oct., 1887; collr. and mag., Malabar, Nov., 1893; inspr.-gen. of Madras police, Dec., 1894.

HAMMICK, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and tutor to the Maharaja of Kolhapur; on famine duty, Kaladgi, Sept., 1877; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Aug., 1883; judge and sessions judge, Shikarpur, Feb., 1885; member of Deccan agriculturists' relief act commn., 1891-92; retd., June, 1894.

HAMNETT, FREDERICK HARPER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, March, 1885; acting sub-collr., Godavari, April, 1886; head asst., Nov., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1891; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1895.

HAMNETT, GEORGE, C.I.E., late Registrn. Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 11th March, 1843; unconv. asst. to the consulting engr., Jan., 1859; marriage registrar for town of Madras, Jan., 1866; first unconv. asst., chief sec.'s office, Feb., 1867; receiver of the Carnatic property, Feb., 1875; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1880; C.I.E., June, 1885; was also registrar-gen. for Mysore, including Bangalore; retd., 1889; member of legisl. council, 1890-92.

HANCOCK, GEORGE EDWARD, Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1859, and served under the mil. dept.; from Nov., 1863, served as asst. supt. of rev. survey and assessment, Gujarat; acting supt. of the Rajkot and Limbdi states, and acting asst. rest. at Aden, and at Baroda; asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, Oct., 1871; asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1872; from Oct., 1873, served as supt. of surveys under rajasthanik court, Kathiawar; acting joint administrator of Gondal state, 1880-81, and was on special duty with the Thakur Saheb in Europe, 1883; on special duty to inquire into administration of Rajpipla state, Nov., 1883; third asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1885, continuing to be supt. of surveys; prest. of the rajasthanik court, Jan., 1886; pol. supt., Savantvadi, May-Oct., 1891; pol. agent, &c., Kolhapur; offd. in Kathiawar, Jan., 1894; and again from March, 1895; returned to Europe, May, 1896.

HANDLEY, ALFRED PERCIVAL, B.L., Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 14th March, 1890, as offg. chief presidency mag. and judge of the court for trial of pilots, Calcutta, and served until Oct., 1891; from Feb., 1892, acted as presidency mag., and was apptd. judge of Calcutta small cause court, May, 1892; chief judge, Oct., 1893.

HANDLEY, FRANCIS FREDERICK, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Sydenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 7th Oct., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; senr. marriage registrar, Calcutta, Dec., 1880; first inspr. of registrn. offices, Dec., 1881; acting inspr.-gen. of registrn., April, 1885; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1885; district and sessions judge, April, 1889.

HANDLEY, JAMES WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Madras.—Served as govt. pleader from 29th Oct., 1868; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1881; first judge of the court of small causes, Madras, July, 1881; offg. judge of

the high court, 1885, and from July, 1889; retd., April, 1893.

HANKEY, HERBERT, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Harrow, and Haileybury; apptd. in 1856; served in the Punjab till 1862, and subsequently in the Lower Prov., and was apptd. mag. and collr., Maldah, 1867, and subsequently at Chittagong; supt. of transport during famine of 1874; inspr.-gen. of police, Lower Prov., 1876; retd., 1878.

HANKIN, EDWARD LEWIS, Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Madras Police Dept.—Ensign, Dec., 1847; arrived, 16th June, 1848; served in the public works dept., Madras, from Aug., 1853, to July, 1859; served subsequently in the police dept. as supt. of police; acting mag. of police for town of Madras, March, 1870; joint mag., Utakamand, Nov., 1870; supt. of family payments and pensions, Oct., 1876; sec. to govt., mil. dept., Madras, Dec., 1882; returned to Europe, 1888.

HANKIN, ERNEST HANBURY, M.A., Chemical Examr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 26th Oct., 1892, as chemical examr., analyst, and bacteriologist.

HANNAGAN, JOHN SALISBURY, Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1859, and held various non-gazetted appts. till May, 1880, when he was apptd. sub-judge at Lucknow; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1892; served through the Mutiny (medal); retd., 1896.

HANNAN, JOHN, Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 31st July, 1866, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Dec., 1891; offg. joint sec. to govt., public works dept., April-Nov., 1893.

HANNAY, ALEXANDER LIDDERDALE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Uppingham, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 5th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HAPPELL, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Crosshill Sch., Maybole, Glasgow Univ., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; acting commr. of salt rev., Madras, 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1883; collr. and mag., Nov., 1883; retd., Jan., 1892.

HARCOURT, ALFRED FREDERICK POLLOCK, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 15th Oct., 1855; from Sept., 1862, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., March, 1877; dep. commr., April, 1877; retd., March, 1889; author of 'Down by the Drawl,' 'The Royal Umbrella,' and 'Kulu, Lahoul, and Spitti.'

HARDIE, ROBERT, Council of India.—Apptd. to the council of the sec. of state for India on 17th March, 1887; vice-prest., 1892-93; was previously engaged in the profession of banking, and held the office of sec. and treasurer of the Bank of Bengal for 15 years; in 1886 was a member of the finance committee apptd. by the govt. of India for the reduction of expenditure.

HARDING, FRANCIS HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1885; district and sessions judge, May, 1890.

HARDING, HERBERT OLIVE DENMAN, Indian

C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. to mag. and collr.; head asst. collr., Coimbatore, Feb., 1893; special asst., Malabar, Dec., 1894.

HARDING, HERBERT WILLIAM, India Audit Office.—Associate of King's Coll., London; writer, India office, 27th April, 1868; junr. examr., India audit office, Nov., 1873; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892; senr. clerk, Jan., 1895.

HARDY, RICHARD GILLIES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Witney Gr. Sch., and Victoria Coll., Jersey; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; mag. and collr., July, 1889; offd. as commr., 1889, 1895, and from April, 1896.

HARE, LANCELOT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Hurstpierpoint, and City of London Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 5th Nov., 1873; served in Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; on famine duty, July, 1874; asst. sec. to chief commr. of Assam, March, 1879, and offg. sec., March, 1880; offg. inspr.-gen. of police, Assam, July, 1880; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., June, 1890; offg. commr., April, 1896.

HARE, ROBERT DOUGLAS, Berar Commn.—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., Chota Nagpur, 14th June, 1869; served on famine duty, May, 1874, and in Berar from Oct., 1875; asst. commr., May, 1878; judge of small cause court, June, 1883; dep. commr., July, 1889; dir. of land records and agric., July, 1890, to Aug., 1891.

HARE, WILLIAM ERNEST KENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Haversham Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

HARI KISHEN KAUL, Pandit, M.A., Punjab Commn.—Probr. under native civil service rules, 17th April, 1890; extra asst. commr., April, 1892; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., Dec., 1895.

HARINGTON, ARTHUR HENRY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862; served in Oudh from Aug., 1863, as asst. commr. and junr. sec. to chief commr.; from Dec., 1866, to Jan., 1868, served under the govt. of India as offg. asst. sec., foreign dept., and offg. under sec., home dept.; offg. supt. of excise and stamps, Oudh, July, 1868; offg. settl. officer, Hardoi, March, 1870; manager of encumbered estates, Faizabad and Gonda, 1872-75; dep. commr., Aug., 1879; mag. and collr., Saharanpur, Oct., 1883; commr. in Oudh, June, 1889; commr., Allahabad, Nov., 1889; Meerut, May, 1891; retd., June, 1896; author of 'Economic Reform in Rural India.'

HARINGTON, HERBERT BARING, M.A., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, 2nd Feb., 1857, as asst. in telegraph dept.; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); from Feb., 1860, served in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; offg. sec. to chief commr., 1869-73; also offg. dir. of public instrn., 1870; dep. commr., July, 1875; district judge, Lucknow, 1879-81; retd., Nov., 1887.

HARINGTON, HERBERT SEPTIMUS, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; engr.-in-chief, Mari-Attock railway, April, 1893.

HARMAN, JOHN MACDONALD, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 29th May, 1872, and employed on mil. works in the N.W. Prov.; transfld. to state railways, Jan., 1880; exec. engr., May, 1883; offg. engr.-in-chief, N.W. railway (frontier section), April, 1895; Burma State railway, April, 1896.

HARRIDANCE, HENRY, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th March, 1878.

HARRIS, EDWARD BRANSON, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Commercial Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 14th Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1888; mag. and collr., June, 1892.

HARRIS (BARON), RIGHT HON. GEORGE ROBERT CANNING, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Educ. at Eton, and at Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated B.A. in 1874; is J. P. and D. L. for Kent, and dep. chairman of the East Kent quarter sessions; under sec. for India in Lord Salisbury's first administration, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; under sec. of state for war, Aug., 1886; govt. of Bombay, 12th April, 1890, to 18th Feb., 1895; G.C.I.E., May, 1890; G.C.S.I., May, 1895; apptd. a lord-in-waiting to Her Majesty, July, 1895.

HARRIS, GEORGE STEEL TRAVERS, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Nov., 1881; lent to Rewa state, Dec., 1883; and to Gwalior, 1886-94.

HARRIS, HENRY NEVILLE, Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 3rd April, 1866, as asst. supt. of police; employed in Assam in 1870; district supt., Feb., 1874.

HARRIS, LEONARD TATHAM, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Falmouth Gr. Sch., Bath Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 10th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HARRIS, ROBERT LISTON, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 1st Dec., 1877, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; depd. to settlt. dept., 1883-84; district judge, Nov., 1889; divl. judge, July, 1894.

HARRISON, CHARLES HOLMES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Nov., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HARRISON, CHARLES WILLIAM INGLEBY, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public works dept., irrign branch, N.W. Prov., from 10th April, 1863, as asst. engr., exec. engr., and asst. to chief engr.; suptg. engr., Dec., 1881; offd. as joint sec. to govt., Bengal, July 1886, and as sec., April-June, 1888; chief engr., Dec., 1889; joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1890; sec., July, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

HARRISON, FRANCIS CAPEL, Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 27th Nov.,

1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, May, 1889; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1890; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1892; acting acctnt.-gen., Madras, April, 1895.

HARRISON, HENRY ANTHONY, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1855; arrived, 3rd April, 1855; served from May, 1856, to Sept., 1858, in Bengal, as asst. mag. and collr., Cuttack, and subsequently in the N.W. Prov.; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, April, 1862; asst., settlt. dept., Feb., 1864; mag. and collr., March, 1872; offg. commr., Jhansi divn., Sept., 1875; civil and sessions judge, March, 1878; ret'd., June, 1887.

HARRISON, PERCY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., March, 1894.

HARRISON, WILLIAM PRINGLE, Lieut.-Col., Oudh Commn.—Arrived as ensign, 10th Feb., 1861; employed under the mil. dept. till Oct., 1867, from which date he served in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr., offg. district supt. of police and supt. of encumbered estates in Sultanpur; sub-judge, Partabgarh, Aug., 1879; cantonmt. mag., July, 1881; dep. commr., Feb., 1888.

HART, GEORGE HENRY REEVES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Probr. in finl. dept., 20th July, 1869; asst. to compt.-gen., Dec., 1870; asst. to private sec. to viceroy, 1874-76; asst. to acctnt.-gen., Bombay, 1877; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, March, 1880; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Lahore, Jan., 1889; Bombay, Feb., 1891; has acted as compt., India treasuries, Aug., 1893, and as asst. sec., fin. and commerce dept.

HART, JOHN HENRY EUSTACE, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 23rd Nov., 1854; joined the dept., Jan., 1855, and served as civil engr., acting exec. engr. for reclamations, Bombay, architectural exec. engr. and surveyor, Bombay, and acting supt., harbour works, Karachi; acting chief engr. for irrign., Sept., 1877; suptg. engr., March, 1880; joint sec. for irrign., March, 1880; chief engr., Dec., 1885; sec., public works and irrign. dept., Sept., 1887; ret'd., 1888.

HART, SIDNEY GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Sch., Rochester, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HART, WILLIAM, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. in 1834; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge and sessions judge; commr. for enquiring into claims, &c., S. Mahratta country, 1847; sec. to govt., gen. and ecclesi. depts., 1855, also in the public works dept., and railway branch, 1856; puisne judge of the court of sadar diwani, 1860; rev. and police commr., S. divn., 1862; member of Bombay legisl. council, 1864; ret'd. in 1865.

HART-DAVIES, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from May,

1876, served in Sind as educ. inspr. and manager of encumbered estates; 1st asst. collr., April, 1887; judge and sessions judge, June, 1891; acted in May, 1895, as jud. commr. in Sind.

HARTNOLL, HENRY SULIVAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Exeter Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1883, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1890.

HARTT, WILLIAM EDWARD, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 19th April, 1876; asst. traffic supt., state railways, 1878; district traffic supt., 1882.

HARTWELL, SYDNEY CHARLES ELPHINSTONE, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt., Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, 1st Jan., 1889; offg. manager, 1891-92, and from April, 1895.

HARVEY, EDWARD, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 22nd Dec., 1859; served in the dept. from April, 1864; exec. engr., mil. works branch, June, 1871; served in the Lushai expdn., 1871-72 (medal and clasp), in the Jowaki Afiridi campaign, 1878-79 (clasp), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); suptg. engr., May, 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1892.

HARVEY, JOHN, Educ. Dept., Punjab.—Served as asst. and head master of various schs. from 1st Nov., 1869; asst. inspr. of schs., April, 1877; tutor to the Raja of Chamba, Nov., 1879; inspr. of schs., Nov., 1883.

HARVEY, ROBERT, D.S.O., M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 31st March, 1865; from 1867 served as civil surgn., offg. sanitary commr., Bengal, med. inspr. of emigrants, and supt. of emigration; prof. of midwifery, med. coll., and obstetric physician to the coll. hosp., Sept., 1882; hon. surgn. on personal staff of the viceroy, May, 1883; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1890; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, May, 1894; principal med. officer, Punjab, April, 1895.

HARVEY, WILLIAM LEATHEM, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Acad. Instn., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 28th Dec., 1883, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; under sec. to govt., rev., finl. and gen. depts., Aug., 1891; 1st asst., Aug., 1894; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1895.

HARWARD, ALFRED EDGAR, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 12th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1892; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894.

HASHMAT-ULLA, Maulvi, M.A., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. a prob. under the native civil service rules, March, 1886; confd., 13th June, 1889, as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1894.

HASTED, JOHN ORD, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—2nd lieut., Madras engrs., Dec., 1853; served in the dept. from 30th Jan., 1856, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1865, and suptg. engr., Feb., 1877; chief engr. for irrign., and joint sec. to govt., April, 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1883; sec., gen. and buildings and road branches, 1886; ret'd., July, 1890.

HASTINGS, CHARLES GOTOLPHIN WILLIAM,

Punjab Police.—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt., Sept., 1873; dist. supt., Dec., 1882; on special duty in Kabul, Feb.-Oct., 1880; with Chitral relief force, June-Sept., 1895; special duty in Kurram from March, 1896.

HASTINGS, FRANCIS THEOPHILUS CLINTON, India Office.—Educ. at Haileybury Coll.; admiralty writer, 8th Oct., 1868; civil service copyist, Jan., 1874; clerk specially attached to the pol. and secret dept., India office, 7th March, 1877; rest. clerk, July, 1888; asst. private sec. to the sec. of state, June, 1892; précis writer, Nov., 1896.

HASTINGS, WARREN, Major, I.S.C., Berar Commn.—Educ. at Brighton Coll.; first commn., 28th Feb., 1874; served in Bengal as cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge from Jan., 1881; special asst. commr., Berar, Sept., 1887; dep. commr., July, 1891; in charge of provl. census operations, 1890-92.

HATCH, GEORGE WASHINGTON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

HATCH, HENRY FREDERICK, Bombay Survey Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1873, and served as asst. supt.; supt. of surveys and asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, April, 1896.

HATCH, WILLIAM KEITH, M.B., C.M., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Major, Bombay Med. Dept.—Arrived, 10th Nov., 1877; second surgn. of the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and prof. of anatomy, &c., Grant med. coll., May, 1884; senr. med. officer and senr. surgn. of the hosp., and prof. of surgery at the coll., April, 1893.

HATCHELL, CHARLES, Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 23rd July, 1858; served in N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn. from Dec., 1859, to Oct., 1864, and subsequently under the mil. dept.; took part in the Bhutan expdn.; served again under govt. of N.W. Prov. from Nov., 1869, as civil asst. surgn., jail supt., and civil surgn.; ret'd., Oct., 1888.

HATHERLY, JOHN RODNEY, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on various irrign. works; exec. engr., Feb., 1876.

HATHORNTWAITE, JAMES THOMAS, M.A., Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1872, and served from that date as prof. of mathematics at the Elphinstone coll., Bombay; also principal of the coll., April, 1894.

HATTEN, JOHN JAMES, B.A., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 12th Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883.

HAVELOCK, SIR ARTHUR ELIBANK, G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E.—Entered the army, Jan., 1862; private sec. and A.D.C. to the officer administering the govt. of Mauritius, 1873-74; acting chief civil commr., Seychelles Islands, 1874-75; member of council, Fiji, and for a time colonial sec. and receiver-gen., 1875; prest. of Nevis, 1877; administering the govt. of St. Lucia, 1878-79; chief civil commr., Seychelles, 1879; govr. of W. Africa settlts., 1881, and assisted in Anglo-French negotiations at Paris regarding that territory; consul for Liberia, 1881; K.C.M.G., 1884; govr. of Trinidad,

1884; govr. of Natal and special commr. for Zulu affairs, 1885; also govr. of Zululand, 1887; attended Brussels conference on the suppression of the slave trade, 1889; govr. of Ceylon, 1890; G.C.M.G., 1895; govr. of Madras, March, 1896; G.C.I.E., March, 1896.

HAWES, ARTHUR BRISCOE, Capt., India Office.—Educ. at Westminster; ensign, Bengal army, 12th Dec., 1849; chief exec. officer of telegraph from Bagdad to Constantinople, 1857–58; ret'd., Oct., 1858; asst. auditor, India office, March, 1859; member of committee for valuation of mil. equipments, April, 1863; author of a work on small arms ammunition.

HAWKINS, ROBERT WILLIAM LYONS, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Oct., 1873, and employed on irrign. works in N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1884.

HAY, GEORGE WILLIAM ROBERTSON, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1868; arrived, 24th Oct., 1868; served in the mil., civil, and jail branches of the service, and was apptd. port surgn. at Aden, June, 1876; sec. to the principal med. officer, Poona, Oct., 1890.

HAYDON, WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1862; arrived, 1st March, 1865; exec. engr., Jan., 1870; employed on Bombay reclamation works and defences, Tapti bridge, and with the Kandahar field force; employed under mil. works dept., 1890–91; ret., Dec., 1894.

HAYES, ALFRED, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 5th Feb., 1873, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; on duty in Madras on famine relief works, 1877–78; exec. engr., May, 1883.

HAYWARD, MAURICE HENRY WESTON, LL.B., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Aug., 1895; jud. asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1896.

HAZLETT, HENRY JAMES, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1867, and was employed on mil. and jail duty; analyst to salt commn., Madras, 1876; surgn., 4th district, Jan., 1887, to May, 1888; also profir. in med. coll., Aug., 1887; employed in mil. accts. dept. of govt. of India, 1888 and 1889; surgn., 3rd district, Feb., 1893.

HEAD, EDGAR ALFRED, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at King Edward's Gr. Sch., Bath; clerk, 2nd class, 30th May, 1895.

HEADLEY, ROBERT HOLLOWELL, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th March, 1896.

HEANEY, JOHN SAMUEL, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HEATON, JOSEPH JOHN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 24th Dec., 1881, and served in Bombay

as asst. collr. and mag., and acting under sec.; asst. judge and sessions judge, July, 1889; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, July, 1895.

HEAVISIDE, WILLIAM JAMES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1857; lieut., Bengal engrs., June, 1859; served in the public works dept. as asst. engr. from Sept., 1862; asst. field engr. with the Bhutan force, 1865–66 (medal and clasp); joined the great trigonol. survey, Jan., 1866; dep. supt., April, 1872; served in Afghanistan, 1878–79 (medal), and subsequently in Khadesh and Bombay native states; ret'd., July, 1888.

HEBBERT, FREDERICK BENBOW, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874; exec. engr., Sept., 1883; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, April, 1887; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., March, 1888; under sec. and dep. dir.-gen. of railways, May, 1892; offg. manager, Burma state railway, Jan.–May, 1896.

HEINIG, JOSEPH, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 26th July, 1867, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Oct., 1877; suptg. engr., Oct., 1895.

HEMING, DEMPSTER, Lieut.-Col., late Police Dept., Madras.—First commn., 16th Nov., 1861; proby. asst. supt. of police, Dec., 1865; dep. commr., Oct., 1874; asst. with the govt. of Madras, 1888, and 1890; supt. of police, Jan., 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1894.

HENDERSON, EDWARD PIERCY, B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 10th Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; govt. advocate, Feb., 1878.

HENDERSON, GEORGE, M.D., Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1859; civil asst. surgn., Shahpur, May, 1862; supt. of Lahore central jail, April, 1863; offg. supt., botanical gardens in Bengal, Dec., 1872; civil surgn., Gujanwala, Dec., 1875, and subsequently at other stations in the Punjab; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

HENDERSON, PHILIP DURHAM, C.S.I., Maj.-Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras cavalry, Feb., 1857; attaché, foreign dept., May, 1871; under sec. to the govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1872; on special duty in Kashmir, 1874; pol. officer on staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Sept., 1875; C.S.I., March, 1876; gen. supt. of operations for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, March, 1878; rest. in Mysore, and chief commr., Coorg, April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Feb., 1895.

HENNESSY, SEPTIMUS HOPE, late Jud. Dept., Central Prov.—Joined the service as acting extra asst. commr., 13th July, 1864, and was confd., 8th July, 1867; judge, small cause court, Sept., 1889; acted also as cantonmt. mag. from Nov., 1888; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

HENNIKER, FREDERICK CHANDOS, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1887, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

HENRY, EDWARD RICHARD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, June, 1882; on famine duty, Tirhut, 1873; private

sec. to lieut.-govr. in 1879 and 1880; mag. and collr., May, 1890; offg. sec. to board of rev., 1890-91; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1892.

HENSLOWE, CECIL WILLIAM EDWARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1866, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, and in the Central Prov.; transf. to railway establt., Jan., 1880; engr.-in-chief and manager, Cawnpore-Farukhabad and Muttra-Hathras railways, Dec., 1880; manager, Cawnpore-Achnera state railway, 1884; transf. to Burma state railway, Dec., 1886; ret'd., 1890.

HENVEY, FREDERICK, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., under sec. to govt., offg. sec. to board of rev., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on special duty in 1871 for purpose of compiling a narrative of the famine of 1869-70; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., July, 1874; offg. rest., Nepal, 1877-78; on special duty, Kashmir, April, 1878; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, Jan., 1883; rest., E. Rajputana states, Dec., 1885; agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

HERALD, JOHN LUMGAIR, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Arbroath High Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 24th Dec., 1883; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1894; mag. and collr., Aug., 1895; dep. commr., March, 1896.

HERBERT, CHARLES, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., June, 1874; served under the mil. dept. to 1882, including Afghan war of 1879-80; extra A.D.C. to viceroy, 1878 and 1882; offd. as asst. to the govr.-gen., Rajputana, June, 1882; mag. of Abu, Nov., 1882; on depn. to Mewar states to settle boundary disputes, 1883; offg. cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, 1884 and 1885-86; asst. commr., Ajmir, April, 1889; asst. to the agent, Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmir-Merwara, March, 1891; offg. pol. agent in E. states of Rajputana, 1891-92; 1st asst. to agent, Central India, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Kotah, March, 1894.

HERBERT, PATRICK HERBERT COGHLAN, Jud. and Public Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; junr. clerk, accts. branch, 11th March, 1872; jud. and public dept., 1877; rest. clerk, 1885-88; senr. clerk, May, 1891; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state (Mr. G. W. E. Russell, M.P.), 1892-94; editor of India Office List, 1887-91; compiled jointly with Mr. Newmarch the India office cypher code.

HERRON, HERBERT GEORGE WHITLEY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 27th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., Nov., 1888, and offg. under sec.; returned to Bengal, Nov., 1890.

HERSCHEL, JOHN, F.R.S., Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1855; first commn., Dec., 1856; arrived at Calcutta, Jan., 1859; served in the great trigonal survey from Nov., 1859, in the grades of asst. surveyor and dep. supt.; ret'd., March, 1886.

HERVEY, HAMILTON LAW, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Giggleswick Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived,

27th March, 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., May, 1894.

HEWETSON, JOHN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Repton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in Burma, from March, 1886, as asst. commr., judge, civil court, Mandalay, and district officer; returned to Madras, Oct., 1888; senr. asst., Ganjam, July, 1889; head asst., Aug., 1893; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1894.

HEWETT, JOHN PRESCOTT, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudhas asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; in charge of provl. gazetteer, Aug., 1883; acted as asst. acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1884; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1884; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1886; confd., Aug., 1887; offd. as private sec. to the viceroy in 1888 and 1892, and as sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1890; offg. census commr. for India, July, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1891; dep. commr., March, 1893; sec. of royal commn. on opium, Oct., 1893; mag. and collr., April, 1894; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1895; author of contributions to provl. gazetteer.

HEWETT, WILLIAM SELWOOD, Major, I.S.C., Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—First commn., 30th Dec., 1871; arrived, 13th March, 1872, and was employed on mil. duty, including service in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); cantonmt. mag., March, 1887.

HEWISH, JOHN, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Junr. clerk, 1st Feb., 1869; senr. clerk, Sept., 1883.

HEWITT, JAMES FRANCIS KATHERINUS, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1853; arrived, 12th Feb., 1859, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; dep. commr., wards' estate in Behar, Feb., 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., Sept., 1874; mag. and collr., Oct., 1878; commr., Chota Nagpur, March, 1879; ret'd., July, 1885; author of 'The Ruling Races of Pre-Historic Times in India,' &c.

HEYWOOD, JOHN MAGEE, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined Addiscombe, Aug., 1854; 1st commn., Bengal engrs., June, 1856; served in the Bengal public works dept. from April, 1858, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., May, 1881; offg. chief engr., and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., 1883-84; chief engr. and joint sec., irrign. branch, Punjab, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1889.

HIGGINS, ALFRED WILLIAM BUCKLE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 6th Dec., 1876, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; sec. to the draft forest bill committee, 1882; dep. conservator of forests, Cuddapah, Dec., 1882; also forest settl. officer, July, 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1890; principal asst. to collr., Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1892.

HIGGINS, ANDREW FRANK, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Oct., 1873, and posted to Haidarabad; exec. engr., May, 1881.

HIGGINS, JOHN JAMES, late Police Dept.,

Central Prov.—Apptd. acting adjutant, mil. police, Narsinghpur, for services rendered during the Mutiny under Sir Henry Havelock, Dec., 1858; from March, 1861, served in the police dept. of the Central Prov., and was promoted to district supt., 1st class, June, 1879; ret'd., March, 1894.

HIGHAM, THOMAS, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., July, 1867; arrived, Oct., 1867, and served in the irrig. dept., Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; asst. sec. to govt., irrig. branch, Nov., 1882; supt. of works, Derajat, Sept., 1883; suptg. engr., Aug., 1886; offd. as chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., Punjab, July-Oct., 1891; chief engr. and sec., irrig. branch, Oct., 1894; author of 'Hydraulic Tables'; C.I.E., May, 1896.

HILDEBRAND, ARTHUR HEDDING, C.I.E., Burma Commn.—From Feb., 1868, served in the Burma police dept. as asst. supt.; from March, 1872, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and was sent on special mission to Chiengmai, 1875, and in charge of expdn. to W. Karenni, 1876; dep. commr., Jan., 1885; supt., S. Shan states, May, 1888; C.I.E., May, 1888; supt. and pol. officer, S. Shan states, Aug., 1891.

HILDEBRAND, GODFREY, Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Oct., 1864; arrived, Jan., 1868; joined the public works dept., March, 1869, and served in Bengal and the N.W. Prov.; transfed. to mil. works, May, 1872; sec. to defence committee, 1881; suptg. engr., March, 1886; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, 1890-91; asst. insp.-gen. of fortifications, War Office, July, 1894.

HILL, CHARLES H., B.L., Calcutta High Court.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1889, as public prosecutor to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh; judge, Calcutta high court, April, 1892.

HILL, CLAUDE HAMILTON ARCHER, Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Mark's Sch., Windsor, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1837, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec., pol., &c., depts., and sec. to legisl. council, Nov., 1892; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Aug., 1895.

HILL, FREDERICK GEORGE, Funds Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th July, 1882.

HILL, HARRY CHARLES, India Forest Dept.—Arrived, 16th Dec., 1872, and was apptd. asst. conservator of forests; dep. conservator of forests, April, 1879; transfed. to the Punjab as offg. conservator, Jan., 1883; conservator of forests, and ex officio sec. to chief commr., Burma, in the forest dept. for Upper Burma, March, 1887; conservator, Naini Tal, March, 1893; acting inspr.-gen., 1889, 1893, and from Feb., 1895.

HILL, HENRY, Public Works Dept., India Office.—Educ. at the Edinburgh Acad. and Edinburgh Univ.; India office writer, old establ., 1859; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 1860; asst. private sec. and précis writer to the sec. of state, 1867-74; senr. clerk, Jan., 1872; rev., statistics and commerce dept., 1874; asst. sec. in that dept., Sept., 1877; asst. in the public works dept., and asst. govt. dir. of guaranteed Indian railway cos., June, 1892.

HILL, JOHN, Col., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Lieut., royal engrs., June, 1862; arrived,

10th Dec., 1865; apptd. to the trigonl. survey as asst. surveyor, Dec., 1866, and employed in Kumaon and Garhwal, Bombay, &c.; dep. supt., July, 1882; attached to Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; in charge of tidal and levelling operations, Poona, Nov., 1890; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1895; ret'd., March, 1896.

HILL, THOMAS COUTTS, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd., 1st Aug., 1857; supt., British Burma, Feb., 1879; employed in connection with occupation of Upper Burma, 1886; chief supt., Sept., 1892; ret'd., March, 1894.

HILL, WILLIAM, Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras infantry, Aug., 1853; served in the Mysore commn. from July, 1859; dep. supt., Nov., 1862; dep. commr., April, 1873; acted for various periods as commr. of divn.; acting commr. of Coorg, 1881; from March, 1885, served as civil and sessions judge, Bangalore; returned to Europe, 1891.

HILTON, JOHN EDWARD, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 8th Oct., 1868, and posted to the Punjab; transfed. to mil. works dept., May, 1872; exec. engr., Aug., 1877; transfed. to Punjab provl. establ., Nov., 1880; under sec. to govt., Sept., 1892; suptg. engr., May, 1893.

HIRTZEL, FREDERIC ARTHUR, B.A., Jud. and Public Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford (Craven scholar); fellow of Brasenose, 1895; apptd. junr. clerk, 11th June, 1894.

HITTU RAM, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1859, and held non-gazetted appts. till Feb., 1877, when he was apptd. native asst. to govt.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan; on special duty with Afghan boundary commn., 1884; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1890; on special duty under Kalat state, May, 1893; author of a Baluchi manual, and a history of Dera Ghazi Khan.

HLA OUNG, MAUNG, Burma Accts. Dept.—Entered the service, 1872; and served in the finance dept.; asst. compt., Nov., 1880; has published a revision of 'Gordon's Burma manual.'

HOARE, HERBERT JAMES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at private schs. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1895.

HOBART, ROBERT THOMPSON, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov., as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Dec., 1873; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Oct., 1882; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1889.

HOBDAY, JAMES RAMSAY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 13th Jan., 1869; proby. asst. supt., survey dept., Sept., 1874; confd., July, 1875; served in the Afghan war, 1879, and with Upper Burma field force, 1886; dep. supt., Jan., 1887; accompanied special expdn. in Upper Burma along the Chinese frontier, 1890-91.

HOBHOUSE (BARON), RIGHT HON. ARTHUR HOBHOUSE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Q.C.—Born 10th Nov., 1819; educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll.,

Oxford, where he gained a first-class in classics in 1840; called to the bar, 1845, and practised in the Rolls court; Q.C. in 1865; apptd. a charity commr. in 1866; endowed schools commr., 1869; legal member of council of the govr.-gen. of India, 1872-77; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1877; arbitrator under the Epping Forest Act, 1878; privy councillor, and member of the jud. committee, 1881; created a baron in 1885; one of the quorum of the House of Lords for jud. purposes; author of 'The Dead Hand,' a collection of addresses connected with endowments and the settlements of land, 1880; contested Westminster unsuccessfully in the general election of 1880.

HOBHOUSE, SIR CHARLES PARRY, Bart., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 22nd July, 1844, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in the finl. dept., govt. of India, as asst. to sub-treasurer, and asst. to acctnt.-gen.; collr. of Bardwan, 1857-59; mag. and collr., 1859; on special duty in 1860 to adjudicate on suits arising out of the indigo disturbances, Nuddea and Jessor; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, and govt. advocate, 1862; also member of Bengal legisl. council, 1863-64; civil and sessions judge, 1864-66; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1866; chairman of the Orissa famine fund, 1867; judge of the high court, 1868-71; ret'd., 1872.

HODGES, ROBERT NATHANIEL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th Oct., 1873, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; transf. to state railways, Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Sept., 1880; employed as engr.-in-chief on several railway surveys, including Saugor-Katni scheme.

HODGSON, GEORGE ROBERT, Lieut.-Col., Madras Police Dept.—Admitted to Bombay staff corps, 23rd June, 1863; after service under the mil. dept., was apptd. acting asst. supt. of police in Madras, March, 1869; confd., Dec., 1872; supt., Oct., 1882; ret'd. July, 1895.

HODGSON, GODFREY BECKETT, Capt., I.S.C., Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 1st May, 1878; served in Afghan war, 1880; apptd. proby. asst. supt., 13th Sept., 1883; confd., July, 1884; dep. supt., May, 1880.

HODSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., April, 1883; supt., way and works, N.W. railway, Nov., 1891; engr.-in-chief, Mushkaf-Bolan railway, July, 1894; services lent to Ceylon Govt., June, 1896.

HODSON, RICHARD GEORGE, late Educ. Dept., Burma.—Joined the dept. as dep. inspr. of schs., Mysore, April, 1861; inspr., April, 1867; acting dir. of public instrn., 1879-80; transf. to Burma as acting principal, high sch., Rangoon, April, 1880; from Oct., 1881, served as inspr. of schs. in Burma, and offd. as dir. of public instrn.; ret'd., Feb., 1893; has transld. Munshi Srinivasaya's 'Dialogues in Kanarese.'

HODSON, THOMAS CALLAN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 11th Dec., 1895; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HOEY, WILLIAM, M.A., D.LIT., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Wesleyan Connexional Sch., and Queen's

Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 18th Oct., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag., and joint mag.; employed on archaeological explorations in Gondia in 1884; dep. commr., Feb., 1890; mag. and collr., Aug., 1890; author of 'Trades and Manufactures in N. India,' 'A History of Asaf-ud-Daulah, Nawab Wazir of Oudh,' 'Buddha; His Life, His Doctrine, and His Order,' 'Memoirs of Delhi and Fyzabad,' &c., &c.

HOGAN, JOHN LEMON POICTEVIN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Nov., 1873, and posted to mil. works; transf. to Mysore, Sept., 1877; to Central Prov., Sept., 1878; and to state railways, Jan., 1880; services lent to Bombay, Baroda and Central India railway co., Jan., 1885; exec. engr., Feb., 1885.

HOGG, ADAM GEORGE FORBES, C.B., Lieut.-Gen., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1854; served with the Persian expedn., 1857 (medal and clasp), in the Mutiny, the China war, 1860, the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal), and the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal and clasp); apptd. pol. rest. and collr. of stamp rev., with charge of the vice-admiralty court, Aden, May, 1885; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

HOGG, SIR FREDERIC RUSSELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 12th Feb., 1857, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., Punjab and Sind, July, 1863; postmr.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1868; representative of the govt. of India at the postal congress, Paris, 1878; dir.-gen. of post office of India, March, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; K.C.I.E., May, 1888; ret'd., April, 1889.

HOGG, STAPLETON COTTON, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Temporary clerk, 25th July, 1861; junr. clerk, accts. dept., 1862; transf. to finl. dept., 1863; senr. clerk, Dec., 1875; asst. sec., Sept., 1895.

HOGG, SIR STUART S., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1853; arrived, Jan., 1853; served in the Punjab as asst. commr. till 1859, and afterwards in Oudh as dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Bardwan, 1861; chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, 1866; commr. of police, Calcutta, and member of Bengal legisl. council, 1874; ret'd., 1878.

HOGG, THEODORE WILLIAM, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., 4th Dec., 1857; arrived, 28th Jan., 1858; and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. from Aug., 1867; small cause court judge, March, 1883; dep. commr., Aug., 1889.

HOJEL, ABRAHAM NICKSON, Brigade-Surgn., M.K.Q.C.P., L.R.C.S. (Ir.), late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 27th July, 1859, and served under the mil. dept.; prof., Grant med. coll., and asst. surgn., Jamsetji Jijibhoy hosp., Feb., 1865; from July, 1866, to Nov., 1871, served in the Persian Gulf as civil surgn., Bushire, and asst. to the rest.; served subsequently under the mil. dept., and as acting ophthalmic surgn., presidency surgn., and marine surgn., Bombay; prof. of physiology, Grant med. coll., and second physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoy hosp., Sept., 1876; physician of the European hosp., April, 1887; ret'd., April, 1891.

HOLBROW, STANLEY CHARLES, Police

Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service, 16th May, 1864, as asst.; district supt., April, 1873.

HOLDERNESS, THOMAS WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 17th Feb., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. sec. to govt., and junr. sec., board of rev.; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Sept., 1881; joint mag., N.W. Prov., April, 1885; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., Nov., 1886; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1888; sec. to govt., finl. dept., March, 1892, at the same time acting as sec. in the jud. dept.; member of legisl. council, Aug., 1892; mag. and collr., Aug., 1893; sec. to govt., judl. dept., July, 1894.

HOLDICH, THOMAS HUNGERFORD, C.B., C.I.E., Col., R.E., Survey of India.—Lieut., royal engrs., Dec., 1862; arrived, 3rd March, 1865, and served in the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (medal and clasp); joined the topogr. survey, July, 1866; on duty with the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (medal); joined the Afghanistan field force in 1879, and was in charge of survey operations in N. Afghanistan during second period of the campaign, and asst. field engr. during defence of Sherpur (medal and clasp); in charge of Kohat topogr. survey, Feb., 1881; dep. supt., April, 1883; in charge of Baluchistan topogr. survey, Oct., 1883; attached to the Afghan boundary commn., Sept., 1884; survey officer with the Zhor field force in 1890; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; C.B. and C.I.E., May, 1894; on special duty with Khaibar-Asmar delimitation commn., 1894-95; in charge, Pamir detachment, June, 1895; temporary appt. to Ceylon, Sept., 1896; author of articles on the geography of Afghanistan and the Indian frontier, and on mil. instrn., topography, &c.

HOLIDAY, FREDERICK CHARLES, India Audit Office.—Temporary clerk, 17th June, 1861; junr. asst., June, 1863; senr. examr., Dec., 1868; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892; asst. auditor, Jan., 1895.

HOLME, ALAN THOMAS, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 15th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.

HOLME, CHARLES HENRY, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll., apptd. 1st Oct., 1876, and posted as asst. engr. to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1891.

HOLME, HENRY EDWARD, B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 27th Sept., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1894.

HOLMES, ROBERT ANDREW KING, M.A., M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Jail Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ.; jail supt., Meerut, April, 1876; transfed. in similar capacity to Lucknow, Feb., 1885; in 1892 acted as inspr.-gen. of prisons.

HOLMES, WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Windermere Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov.,

1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; in charge of Amir Yakub Khan, March, 1880; joint mag., April, 1881; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., March, 1883; asst. commr., Oudh, Dec., 1883; joint mag., June, 1884; dep. commr., Aug., 1887; mag. and collr., Dec., 1888; acted as inspr.-gen. of police in 1893; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

HOLMES, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and in Bombay as offg. asst. acctnt.-gen.; head asst., Coimbatore, Dec., 1884; sub. collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, S. Kanara, Feb., 1893.

HOLMS, JOHN MITCHELL, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 26th Nov., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1889; junr. sec. to board of rev., May, 1889, and Feb., 1891; asst. commr., Oudh, March, 1892; joint mag., Sept., 1892; acted as registrar, high court, in 1894; offg. joint sec. to rev. board, June, 1895; dep. commr., Nov., 1895.

HOLMWOOD, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 16th Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1890; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1894.

HOLROYD, WILLIAM RICE MORLAND, Col., I.S.C., late Educ. Dept., Punjab.—First commn. as ensign, Dec., 1853; served from 11th Aug., 1858, as inspr. of schools, and was apptd. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, Feb., 1868; under sec. to govt., educ. dept., Oct., 1884; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1890.

HOME, ALEXANDER LEFREVRE, Indian Forest Dept.—From 1st March, 1864, served in the dept. in the Punjab, Central Prov. and Bengal, and was apptd. dep. conservator, Oct., 1871; asst. comptr.-gen. of forests, April, 1877; conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1883; Upper Burma, Oct., 1892; Assam, Dec., 1894.

HOME, FREDERICK JERVIS, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public works dept., N.W. Prov., irrign. branch, from Feb., 1861, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1883; offg. chief engr., irrign. branch, and joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1885; on special duty in the Bheartpur state, Oct., 1886; chief engr. and joint sec., Punjab, Dec., 1889; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Nov., 1890; C.S.I., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1894; ret'd., May, 1895.

HOME, WALTER, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1877, and posted as asst. engr. to State Railways; exec. engr., Dec., 1891.

HOOKER, WILLIAM HENSLOW, Store Dept., India Office.—Supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depôt, Aug., 1877; 1st grade, July, 1888.

HOOPER, JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 7th Dec., 1871, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; settlt. officer, Basti, Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., April, 1890; sec., board of rev., Aug., 1890; author of Basti settlt. report,

HOOPER, WILLIAM ROE, Surgn.-Major-Gen., Med. Board, India Office.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., Feb., 1859; arrived, Aug., 1859, and served in Bengal, holding various mil. med. charges; from July, 1861, served in the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn., jail supt., Benares, and civil surgn. at Benares and Lucknow; prest. of the India office med. board, Jan., 1895.

HOPE, CHARLES WILLIAM WEBLEY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Mannamead Sch., Plymouth, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1860; asst. commr., Oudh, June, 1891; settlt. officer, April, 1893; joint mag., April, 1896.

HOPE, HUGH RICHARD, C.B., Major-Gen.—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1860; A.D.C. to Sir W. Denison, govr. of Madras, 1863-64; sec. to govt. of Madras, mil. dept., Oct., 1891, to 1st April, 1895, when appt. was abolished.

HOPE, JAMES, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1861, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in Bombay, 1864-67; principal asst. collr., Ganjam, Sept., 1870; from Jan., 1877, served in the jud. dept. as district and sessions judge; ret'd., 1888.

HOPE, SIR THEODORE CRACRAFT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.L. late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 5th June, 1853, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and educ. inspr.; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, May, 1860; prest. of committee on finances of educ. dept., March, 1861; joint commr. to receive N. Kanara from the Madras govt., Feb., 1862; dep. commr. of customs, salt, and opium, Oct., 1862; collr. and mag., Jan., 1867; from July, 1875, served as addl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, also acting as sec. to govt. of India in dept. of rev., agric. and commerce, and was apptd. addl. sec. for famine, Dec., 1876; reapptd. as addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council in 1877 and 1878; commr., N. divn., and agent to govr., Surat, Jan., 1878; on special pol. duty in England, 1878; provisional member of council, Bombay, Oct., 1880; sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Aug., 1881; ordinary member of govr.-gen.'s council in charge of public works dept., July, 1882; C.I.E., July, 1882; K.C.S.I., May, 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

HOPKINS, EGBERT SILAS, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, registry dept., 14th Nov., 1883; transf. to finl. dept., Sept., 1891.

HOPKINS, HENRY MAYNE REID, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 24th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1892; asst. supt. and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, Feb.-Dec., 1893; asst. commr., Feb., 1896.

HOPKINS, JOHN ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Oct., 1861, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., inspr. of schs., and offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn.; mag. and collr., March, 1878; offg. commr., June, 1888; opium agent, Bihar, April, 1893; ret'd., Dec., 1896.

HOPKINSON, WILLIAM, Col., Bengal Infantry.—First commn., 1859; from 28th Nov.,

1867, served in Bengal as asst. commr. and sub-registrar of assurances; cantonmt. mag., March, 1874; small cause court judge, Nov., 1875; dep. commr., April, 1886; again cantonmt. mag., April, 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

HORDERN, PETER, B.A., late Educ. Dept., Burma.—Apptd. to the dept., 19th Nov., 1861; arrived, March, 1862; asst. prof., presidency coll., Calcutta, March, 1862; dir. of public instrn., British Burma, Nov., 1867; fellow of Calcutta univ., April, 1876; ret'd., 1889; author of a 'Handbook of Rules and Practice of the Educ. Dept., Burma.'

HORMASJI DADABHAI, Khan Bahadur, Bombay Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 26th April, 1889; judge, Bombay small cause court, July, 1891.

HORN, DAVID BAYNE, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; asst. sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Feb., 1878; exec. engr., March, 1882; under sec., public works dept., 1885-86; suptg. engr., Feb., 1896.

HORNE, WILLIAM OGILVIE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting dep. registrar, high court; head asst., March, 1890; special asst., Ganjam, Feb., 1893; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., Nov., 1893; head asst., S. Kanara, March, 1894; sub.-collr. and joint mag., April, 1890.

HORSFALL, JEREMIAH GARNETT, C.I.E., B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras, and was apptd. district and sessions judge, April, 1878; collr. and mag., Jan., 1879; ret'd., April, 1890; C.I.E., May, 1890.

HORSFALL, THOMAS MENDELSSOHN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Wilhelms-Gymnasium, Berlin; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Madura, Dec., 1884; also forest settlt. officer, Nov., 1885; registrar of high court (appellate side), Sept., 1888; principal asst., Ganjam, Dec., 1890; collr. and mag., Dec., 1891; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1892.

HORSFORD, NORMAN MACLEOD THOMAS, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived, as ensign, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the Mutiny campaign (medal and clasp); from Aug., 1861, served in the police dept., N.W. Prov., as asst. inspr.-gen., and district supt.; and from Dec., 1867, in Oudh as asst. commr., junr. sec., and offg. dir. of public instrn.; supt. of encumbered estates, Rae Bareli, 1874-76; offg. supt. of excise and stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., April, 1876; cantonmt. mag., Lucknow, March, 1878; city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, April, 1883; district judge, April, 1886; dep. commr., March, 1887; chief commr., Andaman and Nicobars, April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Sept., 1895.

HORSLEY, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and was in charge of the forests in Khandesh, 1872-74; on famine duty, 1874 and 1877; forest
