(Bengal).—Educ. at Oratory Sch., Edgbaston, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 1st Nov., 1879; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag., and in Assam as asst. commr., and district and sessions judge; district and sessions judge, Jessor, March, 1895.

POPE, THOMAS HENRY, M.D., C.M., Surgn.-Major, Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1878, and served in Afghanistan, 1879-81 (medal); acting residency surgn., Travancore, April, 1885; civil surgn., Negapatam, Oct., 1885; asst. physician, gen. hosp., Jan., 1888; also profir. at the med. coll., to Dec., 1888; district surgn. and supt. of jail, S. Arcot, March, 1892; supt., ophthalmic hospital, Madras, April, 1894.

POPERT, EDWIN PHILIP, Forest Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 16th Dec., 1872, and was employed in Burma as asst. conservator of forests; dep. conservator, July, 1880; conservator, March, 1887; transfed. to Madras, Jan., 1892.

PORTEOUS, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 16th Jan., 1879, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1890; offg. pol. agent and supt., Manipur, Dec., 1892; offg. pol. officer, N. Lushai, July, 1894.

PORTEOUS, CHARLES ARKCOLL, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 26th Nov., 1856, and was employed in the suppression of the Mutiny, 1857-59 (medal and two clasps); served in the dept. from May, 1860, as asst. supt., and supt.; in charge of the Madras police at the imperial assemblage, Delhi, Dec., 1876; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1883; inspr.-gen. of jails, Jan., 1889; inspr.-gen. of police, March, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1895.

PORTEOUS, WILLIAM, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and pol. agent; from Jan., 1874, was employed in Mysore as asst. commr., and in various offg. appts.; from April, 1878, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and acting collr. of salt rev.; junr. collr. and mag. Nov., 1886; senr. collr., April, 1890; acted as commr. in 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

PORTER, ALEXANDER, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., M.R.I.A., Surgn.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 31st March, 1865; held the civil surgery of Akola, 1866-74, and acted as sanitary commr., Berar, 1870-71; from April, 1874, served in Madras, as chemical examr. to govt., surgn., 4th district, and profir. in the med. coll.; fellow of Madras univ., 1883; principal and profir. of medicine, med. coll., Dec., 1886; reverted to mil. duty, July, 1890; ret'd. July, 1895; author of 'Notes on the Pathology of Famine Diseases.'

PORTER, FORTESCUE WILLOUGHBY, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Felsted

Gr. Sch., and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, and joint mag.; dep. commr., Aug., 1885; mag. and collr., Nov., 1886; inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1891; ret'd., May, 1894.

PORTER, GEORGE EDWARD, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Felsted Gr. Sch., and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Dec., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., inspr. of schs., and dep. commr., wards' estate, Patna; district and sessions judge, April, 1879; jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, Oct., 1885; offg. judge, high court, Calcutta, Feb., 1886; ret'd., Sept., 1887.

PORTER, JOHN SINCLAIR, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settl. officer, joint mag., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Shahjahanpur, April, 1879; acted as opium agent, Benares, 1886, and as commr. of Allahabad, 1886; ret'd., July, 1889.

PORTER, LESLIE ALEXANDER SELIM, M.A., LL.D., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Royal Acad. Instn., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and settl. officer; offd. as dep. sec. and sec. to govt. of India, legis. dept., 1891; sec. to Deccan agriculturists relief commn., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Sept., 1892; mag. and collr., Feb., 1894.

PORTER, LUDOVIC CHARLES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Eton, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 8th Jan., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; city mag., Lucknow, Nov., 1895.

PORTER, WILLIAM NINNIS, Burma Commn.—Joined the service as inspr. of police, 11th Oct., 1870; was apptd. asst. district supt., April, 1875, and district supt., Jan., 1880; asst. commr., April, 1886; dep. commr., Nov., 1890.

PORTMAN, AUGUSTUS BERKELEY, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 11th April, 1860, and was employed on mil. duties till May, 1866; subsequently served in the dept. as asst. supt., district supt., and supt. of railway police; acting inspr.-gen. of registrn. and stamps, 1879, and 1880-83; 1st grade supt. of police, Jan., 1885; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

POSFORD, JOSEPH, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 12th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1882; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1888; ret'd., April, 1896.

POSSMANN, JULIUS, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 11th Aug., 1863; asst. supt., Sept., 1866; supt., Aug., 1869; engr. and electrician, Karachi, Dec., 1886; offg. dir., Persian Gulf section, May-Oct., 1891.

POTTINGER, BRABAZON HENRY, Lieut.-Gen., R.A.—Second lieut., 12th Dec., 1857; sec. to govt. of Bombay in the mil., marine and eccles. depts., June, 1887; ret'd., Dec., 1892.

POTTINGER, JOHN CURVEN, M.I.C.E.,

Bombay Sanitary Dept.—Joined the Bombay public works dept., 23rd July, 1868; exec. engr., May, 1875; sanitary engr. and member of sanitary board, Oct., 1893.

POULTON, HENRY DUNBAR, India Audit Office.—Jnr. examr., 26th Dec., 1873; designation changed to jnr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

POWELL, WALTER JOHN, Commander, R.I.M., Marine Dept., Madras.—First commn., 19th Dec., 1864; presidency port officer, Madras, 18th Feb., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

POWER, AMBROSE WILLIAM BUSHE, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. in Germany, and at Kingstown Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and dep. commr. and pol. agent, Hill Tipperah; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; commr., Bardwan, Dec. 1891; ret'd., April, 1894.

POWER, GUY FRANCIS THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., head asst., and addl. sessions judge; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1891; collr. and mag., Jan., 1894; again district and sessions judge, March, 1895.

POWLETT, PERCY WILLIAM, C.S.I., Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 58th native infantry, Bengal, April, 1855; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); asst. commr., Punjab, 1862; and Berar, 1865; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, 1868; settl. officer, Alwar, Jan., 1871; pol. agent, Kota, Jan., 1877; pol. agent for the W. Rajputana states, 1880; rest., W. Rajputana, Dec., 1881; C.S.I., Jan., 1891; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892; compiled the gazetteers of Bikanir, Alwar and Karoali.

PRAMODA CHARAN BANERJI, B.A., B.L., High Court, N.W. Prov.—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1872, as munsif; sub-judge, Aug., 1880; after serving as small cause court judge at Agra and Allahabad, was apptd. addl. civil judge, Lucknow, Aug., 1893; and puisne judge, high court, N.W. Prov., Dec., 1893.

PRASANNA KUMAR RAY, D. Sc., Bengal Educl. Dept.—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1877, and served as asst. prof., Patna and Dacca colls.; prof., presidency coll., Feb., 1888.

PRATT, EDWARD MILLARD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 21st Dec., 1886, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., March, 1894.

PRATT, FREDERICK GREVILLE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 24th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

PRATT, JAMES, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen and London Univs.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 12th Oct., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1880; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1888.

PRATT, WILLIAM DERING, Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, 6th Sept., 1863; district supt., Aug., 1868; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1894.

PREECE, HENRY CHARLES, Store Dept.,

India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 5th Dec., 1885.

PRENDERGAST, SIR HARRY NORTH DALRYMPLE, K.C.B., V.C., Gen.—Second lieut., 8th June, 1854; served in the Persian war in 1857 (medal with clasp), with the Malwa field force in 1857, with the Central India field force in 1858 (medal with clasp and V.C.), and in the Abyssinian war 1867-68 (medal); in command of sappers in the Indian expdn. to the Mediterranean, 1878; commanded the expdn. that resulted in the annexation of Upper Burma in 1885-86 (clasp); has received the distinguished service reward; held appts. in the dept. of public works and in the railway dept.; commanded the Madras sappers and miners for 10 years; acted as quartermaster-gen. of the Madras army and as sec. to govt. in the mil. dept.; offd. as British rest. in Travancore and Cochin in 1887, and as British rest. in Mysore and chief commr. in Coorg in 1887 and 1888; agent to the govr.-gen., Baroda, Jan., 1889; offg. rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, March to Oct., 1889; offg. rest., Mysore, and chief and jud. commr., Coorg, June, 1891; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1892.

PRESTON, SIDNEY, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 10th Aug., 1870, and employed in the Punjab on irrign. works; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; offg. under sec. to the Punjab govt., 1885-86; suptg. engr., April, 1893.

PRICE, GORDON, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—Served from 29th July, 1875, as civil surgn. and on jail duty; principal asst. to opium agent, Behar, Nov., 1887, to April, 1889.

PRICE, JAMES CATTRALL, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; settlt. officer, Midnapur, Dec., 1871; also supt. of survey, March, 1876; mag. and collr., Aug., 1876; author of 'Notes on the History of Midnapur.'

PRICE, JOHN ARTHUR, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Madras civil engrg. coll.; apptd., 14th May, 1869, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., April, 1882; transf. to Burma, July, 1887.

PRICE, JOHN FREDERICK, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Melbourne Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 25th Oct., 1862; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr.; collr. and mag., S. Arcot, and pol. agent for Pondicherry, April, 1878; collr. and mag., Chengalpet, Sept., 1878; board of examrs., Jan., 1882; acting dir. of rev. settlt. and agric., Madras, April, 1885; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1887; member of legisl. council, Oct., 1888; chief sec. to govt. of Madras, Nov., 1888; C.S.I., Jan., 1893.

PRICE, WILLIAM, M.D., M.Ch., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 7th Nov., 1869; civil surgn., Vellore, April, 1877; med. officer, Utakamand, March, 1878; zillah surgn., Feb., 1881; senr. physician, gen. hosp., and principal, med. coll., Oct., 1891; ret'd., April, 1896.

PRIDEAUX, WILLIAM FRANCIS, C.S.I., Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1860; asst. pol. rest., Aden, April, 1864; attached to mission to Abyssinia, March, 1865, and was in captivity at Magdala, 1866-68; offd. as pol.

agent, Zanzibar, 1873-75; fellow of Bombay univ., 1874; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., June, 1875; offg. pol. rest., Persian Gulf, 1876-77; pol. agent, Bhopal, March, 1879; agent to the govr.-gen. with the ex-King of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions, Calcutta, Nov., 1880; offg. rest., E. Rajputana states, 1882-83; apptd. in 1887 agent to the govr.-gen. for the affairs of the late King of Oudh, and supt. of pol. pensions; rest. at Jaipur, Nov., 1888; acting rest., Kashmir, Dec., 1890; rest. in Mewar, Nov., 1893; acting agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1893-94; rest., Jaipur, April, 1894; C.S.I., Jan., 1895; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1895.

PRIEST, HENRY HALSTED, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at the Real-Schule, Marburg; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and in various acting appts.; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, Jan., 1891; dep. commr., Aug., 1891; in addn., pol. agent, Chattisgarh feudatories, June, 1892; rev. sec., May, 1893; chief sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1895.

PRINGLE, ROBERT KEITH, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1820; arrived, 1821, and served in the Bombay rev. dept.; supt. of rev. survey and assessment of the Deccan, 1826; sub-collr. and mag., Sholapur, 1831; acting dep. civil auditor and deputy mint-master, 1838; collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Khandesh, Dec., 1840; sec. to govt. of Bombay, gen. dept., June, 1843; rev. commr., July, 1843; chief sec. to govt., rev. and fin. depts., govt. dir. of the Bank of Bombay, and master of the mint, April, 1846; acting member of council, Bombay, April, 1846; chief commr. of Sind, July, 1847; resigned, Feb., 1851.

PRINSEP, SIR HENRY THOBV, Kt., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 7th Dec., 1855, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; offg. junr. sec., board of rev., Sept., 1861; registrar of the sadar diwani and nizamat adalat, Feb., 1862, and subsequently of the high court, July, 1862; mag. and collr., 1st grade, May, 1866; served in the jud. dept., from Feb., 1867, as district and sessions judge, and offd. as jud. commr. of Mysore, 1875-76; judge, high court, Calcutta, Aug., 1878.

PRITAMDAS PARSUMAL CHANDANANI, L.C.E., Bo. Public Works Dept.—Joined the service 20th April, 1875, and served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1886.

PRITCHARD, ALFRED BASSETT, Capt., Indian Staff Corps, Burma Commn.—First commn., 11th Nov., 1878; apptd. to Burma as asst. commr., June, 1887.

PRITCHARD, SIR CHARLES BRADLEY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 24th Jan., 1858, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Feb., 1873; on special duty in Madras as prest. of the salt commn., 1876; on special duty to submit a scheme for the working of the abkari system, 1877-78; commr. of customs, &c., April, 1881; commr. of salt rev., Oct., 1882; C.S.I., March, 1886; acting commr. in Sind, April, 1887; confd., Aug., 1888; temporary member of council of govr. of Bombay, May, 1889; confd., Nov., 1890; K.C.I.E., May, 1891; member of viceroy's council, Nov., 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

PRITCHARD, CHARLES HAMERTON, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at

Clifton Coll.; first commn., 10th May, 1882; from March, 1888, acted as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; engaged in boundary settl. work in Mewar, 1889-90; asst. to rest., Haidarabad, and to the gen. supt. thagi and dakaiti, April, 1890; asst. commr., Merwara, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, E. states of Rajputana, April, 1893; settlt. officer, Tonk, Feb., 1894.

PROPERT, WILLIAM HENRY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 6th Nov., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and pol. agent; from Feb., 1876, served as collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Khandesh; senr. collr., Sept., 1877; commr., April, 1886; ret'd., April, 1890.

BROTHERO, MICHAEL ERNLEE DU SAUTOY, M.A.—Bengal Educ. Service.—Joined the service as prof'r., presy. coll., Calcutta, 17th April, 1881; actg. inspr. of schools, April, 1896.

PROTUL CHANDAR CHATTERJI, Rai Bahadur, M.A., B.L., Punjab Chief Court.—Joined the service as offg. judge, 14th April, 1804; addl. judge, July, 1894.

PUCKLE, RICHARD KAYE, C.I.E., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch., and Haileybury Coll.; entered the Madras civil service in 1851, and was apptd. dep. dir. of rev. settlt. in 1859; collr. and mag., Tinnevelly, 1866; dir. of rev. settlt., 1874, and member of board of rev., 1879; was engaged for 22 years in preparing data for the re-assessment of the lands, and for the revision of govt. establs. in the ryotwari villages of Madras; employed in famine districts in 1877; C.I.E., 1878; ret'd. in 1881; publ'd. a hand-book on rev. matters and the duties of village officers.

PULFER, JOHN WILLIAM, Store Dept., India Office.—Writer, 3rd Aug., 1865; clerk, 2nd class, India store dept., Aug., 1878.

PULFORD, RUSSELL RICHARD, Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., 23th Feb., 1870, as asst. principal, Thomson coll.; asst. engr., June, 1873; exec. engr., March, 1874; served through the Afghan war, 1879-80; suptg. engr., March, 1892; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July-Oct., 1894; transf'd. to Rajputana and Central India, May, 1896.

PURSER, WILLIAM EDWARD, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Enniskillen Royal Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 21st Nov., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; senr. settlt. officer and dep. commr. of settlt., Jalandhar, Sept., 1880; dep. commr., Dec., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1890; author of reports on the settlt. of the Montgomery district, and of the Muktsar and Mamdot parganas.

PURVES, HENRY BLACK, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—First commn., 1865; from 12th March, 1867, served as civil asst. surgn., and civil surgn., in various stations in Bengal; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

PUTNAM, JAMES, Pol. Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 26th Feb., 1892; transf'd. to pol. dept., Dec., 1895.

PUTNAM, PHILIP, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 28th July, 1881.

QUERIPEL, ALFRED ERNEST, Veterinary Lieut.-Col., India Civil Veterinary Dept.—Joined the (home) service, 27th Aug., 1870; arrived in India, 3rd Feb., 1877; served in Afghan war, 1879-80, and with Nile expedny. force, 1884-85; asst. supt., horse breeding operations, Babugarh,

1880-83; reverted to home establt., April, 1884; apptd. to charge of chief station veterinary hosp., Ambala, and instructor, army veterinary sch., Simla, March, 1891; inspecting veterinary officer, E. circle, Dec., 1893; inspr.-gen., civil veterinary dept., May, 1894; author of an essay on stable management and dietetic disease in India, and of a manual of materia medica for veterinary students.

QUICKE, WILLIAM HENRY, Surgn.-Major, Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1883; acted as Bombay port health officer in 1888-89, and as supt. of vaccn. in the latter year; confd. as civil surgn., Kaira, Sept., 1890; 2nd surgn., Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and prof'r. of anatomy and curator of museum, Grant med. coll., April, 1893.

QUIN, HENRY OSBORNE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 13th Nov., 1885, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., March, 1892.

QUIN, THOMAS JAMES, Col., Oudh Commn.—First commn., ensign, 13th April, 1859; from May, 1869, served under the govt. of the N.W. Prov. as offg. asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag.; dep. commr., Nov., 1889; served during the Bhutan campaign of 1865 (medal).

QUINLAN, FRANCIS PETER, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the accts. branch, Oct., 1870, and was employed on the accts. of various railways in the grades of acctnt., asst. examr., asst. controller, and dep. examr.; examr., Sept., 1881; ret'd., March, 1895.

QUINN, CHARLES CAMPBELL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 8th Jan., 1864; served in the rev. and jud. depts., Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., June, 1882; commr., Bhagalpur divn., Oct., 1891; ret'd., March, 1895.

QUINN, JOHN, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., Oudh, April, 1877, and mag. and collr., Ballia, Dec., 1881; commr., Lucknow divn., March, 1884; offd. as jud. commr., Oudh, in 1884; ret'd., March, 1888.

RADICE, CHARLES ALBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. officer.

RADICE, EVASIO HAMPDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt., Tarai, asst. commr. and joint mag.; city mag. and wasika officer Lucknow, Aug., 1893; offg. postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1895; joint mag., Nov 1895.

RAGHUNATH SHIVRAM TIPNIS, B.A., LL.B., Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Served as subord. judge from July, 1878, and was apptd. an asst. collr. in Bombay, under the native civil service rules, 1st April, 1887; on depn. to the Junagad state, 1893-94; 2nd asst., June, 1894.

RAIKES, FREDERICK DUNCAN, C.I.E., Lieut.-

Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, 66th regt., 1st April, 1870; acting asst. supt. of police, Panch Mahals, March, 1873; from May, 1873, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr., and acted as asst. town mag., Rangoon, 1874-75; transf. to Upper Burma, Feb., 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; served in the Burmese campaign, 1885-87 (medal and clasp); on special duty with the Chin field force, Dec., 1888; offg. commr., 1892-93 and 1894-95.

RAJAGOPALA CHARI, PERUNGAVUR, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Madras Univ.; served in the jud. dept. from 13th May, 1836; dep. collr., Dec., 1887; apptd. prob. for the civil service, 10th Jan., 1888; served as asst. collr. and mag., and was confid., 2nd May, 1890.

RAJANINATH RAY, M.A., Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., Nov., 1872, and served in the Central Prov., and afterwards in Bombay; asst. comptr.-gen., Nov., 1882; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug.-Nov., 1885; dep. comptr.-gen., April-June, 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1893.

RAJARATHNA MUDALIYAR, PAKAM, Dewan Bahadur, Madras Rev. Dept.—Served in the salt dept. from 26th Oct., 1860; head sarishtadar, board of rev., Aug., 1882; sec. to the commr. of rev. settl. and dir. of the dept. of land records and agric., Nov., 1887; offd. as inspr.-gen. registrn. from July, 1896.

RAM AUTAR PANDE, Pandit, B.A., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Joined the service as offg. dep. collr., 13th March, 1884; apptd. asst. mag. and collr. under the statutory service rules, 12th Feb., 1887; joint mag., Nov., 1891; asst. commr., Nov., 1895.

RAMACHANDRA AIYAR, CUDDALORE, late Madras Jud. Dept.—Joined the dept., 19th Jan., 1860; 4th presidency mag., Madras, March, 1885; apptd. asst. collr. and mag., Feb., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1889; district and sessions judge, June, 1891; ret'd., March, 1894.

RAMACHANDRA RAO, RAGHUNATHA RAO, B.A., Madras Rev. Dept.—Apptd. prob. under the native civil service rules, 13th June, 1890, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. collr., July, 1892.

RAMA SHANKAR MISRA, Pandit, M.A., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Offg. profir. of mathematics, Benares coll., Feb., 1877; resigned, Jan., 1878; rejoined as profir., Agr. coll., 23rd Aug., 1879; apptd. under the native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 29th Dec., 1887; joint mag., May, 1892; asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1893; asst. commr., April, 1896; author of a 'Treatise on Mensuration' in Hindi, and a 'Manual of Rent and Revenue Law.'

RAMPINI, ROBERT FULTON, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the Acad., Instn., and Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; joint sessions judge, Sept., 1875; district and sessions judge, July, 1880; judge of high court, Calcutta, April, 1893; edited Alexander's 'Indian Case Law on Torts,' Field's 'Law of Evidence,' and (with Mr. Finucane) 'The Bengal Tenancy Act.'

RAMSAY, HENRY LUSHINGTON, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Feb., 1874; acted as asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, 1877-78; junr. attaché,

foreign dept., March, 1878; and seni., June, 1880; pol. officer on staff of Sir F. S. Roberts with the Kuram field force, Sept., 1879 (medal and clasp); asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Oct., 1880; mag. at Abu, and asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., Nov., 1880; asst. agent, Baluchistan, June, 1882; consul at Bassora, May, 1883; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Nov., 1883; British joint commr. of Ladakh, Oct., 1885; pol. agent, Bhopawar, March, 1891; pol. agent, S. Baluchistan, Nov., 1893; pol. officer with Sardar Ayub Khan, Oct., 1894; author of 'Western Tibet.'

RAMSAY, HERBERT MAYNARD, Col., Police Dept., Bengal.—First commn., 1860; from 2nd July, 1863, served in the dept. as asst. supt., district supt., and personal asst. to the inspr.-gen.; on depn. to report on chaukidari system, 1890-91.

RAMSAY, JAMES, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service 8th Oct., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1871; engr.-in-chief, Indus valley state railway, Nov., 1880, and subsequently of the Sind-Sagar and Muskhaf-Bolan lines; suptg. engr., Feb., 1890; chief engr., Feb., 1893; ret'd., July, 1894.

RAMSAY, JOHN, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; from July, 1886, served at Ajmer and Indore as acting asst. agent; confid. in latter post, Jan., 1889; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Dec., 1891; 1st asst., Indore, May, 1892; in addn., asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti, Nov., 1892; pol. agent in Bundelkhand, April, 1893.

RAND, WALTER CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1883, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. collr., Bombay, and inspr. of factories, May-Dec., 1891; 1st asst., Satara, March, 1894.

RANGA CHARI, SIVARAM, B.A., Madras Rev. Dept.—Joined the service, 6th April, 1865; asst. commr. of rev. settl., April, 1887; dep. commr. of rev. settl. Jan., 1889.

RANKIN, JAMES THOMSON, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow and Edinburgh Univs.; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

RANKING, GEORGE SPIERS ALEXANDER, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1875; apptd. sec. to board of examers., June, 1894; author of a guide to Hindustani for mil. med. officers, a work on Arabic and Persian prosody, &c.

RANSOM, HENRY EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 12th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1894.

RASTAMJI MERWANJI PATEL, Khan Bahadur, M.A., LL.B., Bombay Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1894, as 2nd judge, Bombay small cause court.

RATTON, JAMES JOSEPH LOUIS, M.D., M.R.C.S., Surgn.-Major, late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 30th Sept., 1867; served in the Abyssinian expdn. (medal); civil surgn., Central Prov., 1869-70; on special duty in connection with salt manufacture, 1872-76; on famine relief works, 1876; zillah surgn. and supt. of jail, Chittur, April, 1877; surgn., gen. hosp., Madras, and profir. in the med. coll., Feb., 1885.

fellow of Madras univ., 1886; retd., Jan., 1888; has publ. a 'Handbook on Common Salt,' a 'Treatise on Tetanus,' and a 'Syllabus of Surgery.'

RAVENS CROFT, EDWARD W., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at King Henry VIII.'s Gr. Sch., Ottery St. Mary, and Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 25th May, 1851, and served as asst. collr. and mag.; inspr.-in-chief under the cotton frauds act, Bombay, Feb., 1864; prest. of the municipal commn. Bombay, June, 1864; chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, in the rev., &c., depts., 1877; collr. of Bombay, and supt. of stamps and stationery, March, 1868; member of legisl. council, Bombay, 1871 and 1873; collr. of salt rev., Oct., 1871; senr. collr. and mag., Dec., 1871; member of the Baroda commn. to investigate the charges brought against the Gaekwar; member of govr.'s council, Bombay, April, 1879; retd., March, 1884.

RAVENS HAW, CHARLES WITHERS, Major, I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 8th May, 1872; from Dec., 1875, offd. as asst. cantonmt. mag. at various places; from Feb., 1877, served under the foreign dept. as asst. pol. agent; on special boundary duty in 1879-80; served in Afghan war (medal); supt. of Rajputana railway police, Sept., 1880; asst. commr., Merwara, Dec., 1885; 1st asst. pol. rest., Persian Gulf, July, 1887; cantonmt. mag. and judge, small cause court, Secunderabad, April, 1890; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, April, 1891; offg. rest., Gwalior, April, 1895.

RAWLINS, THOMAS WILLIAM, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 11th Jan., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; small cause court judge, Allahabad, Nov., 1872; served under the govt. of India, fin. dept., from March, 1877; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, and dep. commr. of paper currency, Aug., 1880; acctnt.-gen. and commr. of paper currency, Madras, Oct., 1888; retd., Feb., 1896.

RAWSON, COURTNEY CLARKE, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., 27th June, 1871; exec. engr., March, 1878; served in Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); acted as dep. chief engr. and under sec. to govt. in 1881 and 1882; suptg. engr., Jan., 1896.

RAWSON, EDWARD CRESWELL, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Jan., 1889; sub-collr. and joint mag., March, 1893.

RAYE, DANIEL O'CONNELL, M.D., Surgn.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 31st March, 1866; from 1st Sept., 1875, served as rest. surgn., presidency gen. hosp.; profr., med. coll., and surgn. to the coll. hosp., May, 1881; principal med. officer, Calcutta, Jan., 1895; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Punjab, March, 1895.

RAYMENT, GEORGE JOSEPH, Veterinary Major, late Indian Civil Veterinary Dept.—First commn., 24th July, 1872; apptd. supt., civil veterinary dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1st April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894.

REAY (BARON), RIGHT HON. DONALD JAMES MACKAY, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., LL.D.—Took his seat as govr. of Bombay, 30th March, 1885; G.C.I.E., June, 1887; G.C.S.I., March, 1890; returned to Europe, April, 1890; was lord rector

of the univ. of St. Andrew's, 1884; parly. under sec. of state for India, March, 1894, to July, 1895.

REAY, ROBERT WILLIAM, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 23rd Oct., 1894.

REBSCH, SAMUEL, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884.

REDFERN, THOMAS ROBERT, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at New Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 2nd Oct., 1870, and served in Oudh as asst. commr. and offg. settl. officer; joint mag., May, 1885; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1887; retd., Oct., 1895.

REED, JOHN HENRY FREDERICK, B.A., Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; B.A., London univ., 1881; junr. clerk, June, 1887.

REES, FREDERICK WILLIAM JOSEPH, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 11th Jan., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., April, 1878; from Dec., 1880, served in the jud. dept. as district and sessions judge; retd., Dec., 1887.

REES, JOHN CHARLES, B.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 8th Aug., 1873, and posted to Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1882; employed on Burma state railway, 1884-85; received Burma medal and clasp; in charge, Pegu divn., May, 1894.

REES, JOHN DAVID, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London, and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; from April, 1882, served in the secretariat as asst. sec. and under sec., and in Mysore (1884-85) as acting civil and sessions judge; private sec. to the govr. of Madras, Dec., 1886; fellow of Madras univ., 1887; on special duty in connection with visit of Prince Albert Victor, 1839-90; C.I.E., May, 1890; apptd. collr. and mag. and sessions judge, Nilgiris, Aug., 1890, continuing to act as private sec. until Dec., 1891; acting rest., Travancore, April, 1895; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1895; author of 'Notes of a Journey from Kasween to Hamadan in N.W. Persia,' 'Tours in India by Lord Connemara,' 'The Duke of Clarence in S. India,' and 'Epochs of Indian History: The Muhammadans,' and compiler of 'The Administration of Lord Connemara,' and 'Speeches of Lord Connemara.'

REID, ARTHUR HAY STEWART, M.A., Barrister-at-Law.—Profr. of Law, Muir Coll., Allahabad, May, 1883; offd. as public prosecutor, high court, N.W.P., March, 1895-Jan., 1896; and as judge, chief court, Punjab, April, 1896.

REID, GEORGE BOILEAU, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 1st asst. collr., July, 1878; settl. officer at Baroda, in 1879 and 1882; junr. collr. and mag., Oct., 1886; collr. and mag., Nov., 1886; senr. collr., May, 1888; commr., N. divn., Nov., 1892.

REID, JAMES ROBERT, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh

Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 13th Dec., 1860, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and joint mag., and offg. dep. commr. in Oudh; from Aug., 1868, served as asst. settl. officer, and settl. officer, Azamgarh; mag. and collr., Jan., 1878; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. Dec., 1883; chief sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1884; commr., Kumaon divn., Nov., 1888; member of board of rev., Feb., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1890; member of legisl. council, March, 1891; ret'd., April, 1894.

REID, JOHN WILLIAM, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1855; served from July, 1856, in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-judge, and sub-collr. and joint mag.; from June, 1869, served in Malabar as civil and sessions judge; district and sessions judge, Coimbatore, Oct., 1884; ret'd., 1886.

REID, WILLIAM JAMES, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

REILY, CHARLES HORACE, B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1876, as reporter for the Indian law reports, Calcutta; in Feb., 1882, was apptd. clerk of the crown and law lecturer, presidency coll.; asst. sec. to the govt. of Bengal, legis. dept., Nov., 1883; offd. as chief presidency mag. in 1885, and as judge of the court for trial of pilots in 1886-87; resigned, Oct., 1893.

REILY, HENRY MAXWELL, late Police Dept., Bengal.—From May, 1858, served as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; from July, 1863, served in the police dept., and was apptd. district supt., 1st grade, Dec., 1882; on special duty, Garo Hills expedn., Nov., 1872; ret'd., 1890.

REINOLD, EDWARD KONRAD, late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; ret'd., Jan., 1895.

RENDEL, SIR ALEXANDER MEADOWS, K.C.I.E.—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge (scholar and wrangler); studied as a civil engr., and in 1856 became engr. to the London dock co., the Leith harbour and dock commrs., the East Indian railway, and other companies; visited India in 1857-58, and on various occasions; built the Shadwell new basin, the royal Albert dock, and other works on the Thames, the Albert and Edinburgh docks at Leith, the Workington dock and harbour, and other works; member of the commn. apptd. by the sec. of state for India in 1870, to determine the narrow gauge for Indian railways; designed the Sukkur bridge over the Indus (opened 1889); is consulting engr. to the sec. of state for India, and to various Indian railway cos.

RENDELL, ALAN WOOD, late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Arrived, March, 1874; joined the dept. as locomotive supt., N. Bengal state railway, 8th Nov., 1877; locomotive supt. of N.E. system, June, 1881; and of E. Bengal state railway, July, 1884; ret'd., 1890.

RENNICK, ALBERT DE CLANEY, B.L., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Punjab Commn.—First commn., 6th July, 1864; arrived, 28th Nov., 1866; from Oct., 1870, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Dec., 1880; dep. commr., May, 1889.

RENNIE, FRANCIS PEPYS, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and Clare Coll.,

Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 23rd December, 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

RENNIE, JOHN GEORGE MACKWORTH, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 19th Dec., 1881, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, Dec., 1892.

RENNY, ROBERT HOME, Bengal Non-Regulation Commn.—Served in the exec. dept. from 12th Nov., 1864, as dep. mag. and dep. collr. and extra asst. commr.; joined the commn., March, 1877; dep. commr., April, 1890.

RENOUF, WINTER CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 14th Dec., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

REYNOLDS, CHARLES HENRY, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 7th Jan., 1868; personal asst. to dir.-gen., 1870, and 1872; in charge of famine divn., 1874; supt., April, 1886; delegate for India at the Berlin telegraph conference, 1885; dir., construction branch, July, 1889; dir., traffic branch, Dec., 1892; dep. dir.-gen., June, 1894; dir.-gen., April, 1895.

REYNOLDS, GEORGE BERNARD, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., M.I.S.I., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to state railways; employed in superior rev. establ., locomotive dept., 1879-91; exec. engr. and manager, Warora colliery, April, 1891.

REYNOLDS, HERBERT JOHN, C.S.I., B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 24th Sept., 1856; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., supt. of survey, and mag. and collr.; between 1874 and 1880 served as sec. to the govt. of Bengal, gen. and rev. depts., and as sec. to the board of rev.; member, govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1880, and again in 1882 and 1884; member, Bengal legis. council, 1881, 1883, 1885, and 1887; board of rev., July, 1884; chairman of port commrs., Calcutta, Oct., 1887; ret'd., April, 1889.

REYNOLDS, HERBERT WILLIAM WARD, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. sec. to govt., and junr. sec., board of rev.; joint mag., Aug., 1890; dep. commr., Dec., 1893; mag. and collr., Jan., 1895.

REYNOLDS, PLAYFORD, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, and was employed in Khandesh and Gujarat on irrig. works; exec. engr., May, 1879; supt. engr., June, 1896; services lent to Baroda state in 1883-85.

REYNOLDS, WILLIAM HENRY, Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 14th March, 1861, and was engaged on survey duty on the N.W. frontier until 1871, including service with the Ambela expedn., 1863; from Jan., 1871, served in the forest dept. as asst. conservator, dep. conservator, and dep. supt. of forest surveys; rejoined the survey dept. as dep. supt., Oct., 1890; supt. of forest surveys, March, 1891.

RHIND, ROBERT HUNTER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. prob. by the sec. of state, June, 1862; arrived, 7th Dec., 1862, and served in Madras as asst. engr., and exec.

engr.; transf. to Bengal, Aug., 1872; supt. engr., Nov., 1886; ret. d., May, 1893.

RHODES, FRANCIS WILLIAM, D.S.O., Col., late 1st Royal Dragoons.—First commn., lieut., 23rd April, 1875; served in the Soudan expedn. in 1884 (medal with clasp and Khedive's star); also in Nile expedn. of 1884-85, and in the operations near Suakin, 1888 (3rd class Medjidie); mil. sec. to Lord Harris, govr. of Bombay, 1890-93; ret. d., Sept., 1896.

RIBBENTROP, BERTHOLD, C.I.E., Forest Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. by the sec. of state an asst. conservator of forests, Dec., 1866; conservator, Burma, Oct., 1876; transf. to the Punjab, 1883; inspr.-gen. of forests, Jan., 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; author of 'Hints on Arboriculture.'

RICE, PERCIVAL STANLEY PITCAIRN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 8th Dec., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

RICE, WALTER FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 16th Dec., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

RICE, WILLIAM GEORGE LEWIS, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Oscott Coll., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 10th Nov., 1882, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; transf. to Burma, Feb., 1886, as asst. commr.; resigned, Oct., 1890.

RICE, WILLIAM ROCHE, M.D., C.S.I., Surgn.-Major-Gen.—Joined the Bengal med. service Nov., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal); civil asst. surgn., Central Prov., June, 1859; civil surgn., Jabalpur, May, 1871; acting asst. to sanitary commr., Central Prov., July, 1872; supt., central jail, Jabalpur, April, 1873; civil surgn., July, 1879; inspr.-gen. of civil hosps., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1887; sanitary commr. and surgn.-gen. to govt. of India, March, 1890; ret. d., March, 1895.

RICHARD, HENRY JOSEPH, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, July, 1868; asst. engr., Rangoon, 28th Oct., 1868; exec. engr., April, 1875; served with the Burma expedn. force, 1835-86 (medal with clasps); supt. of works, Upper Burma, Jan., 1886; supt. engr., Sept., 1889; chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Burma, public works dept., Aug., 1895.

RICHARDS, JAMES DAY, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893; 2nd class clerk, Nov., 1896.

RICHARDSON, EDWARD, Jud. Dept., Burma.—After service in various subord. posts, was apptd. extra asst. commr., 2nd Feb., 1865; first judge, civil court, Mandalay, Sept., 1887.

RICHARDSON, JOHN, M.B., M.A., Surgn.-Col., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., 27th July, 1859; served with the Bhutan field force (medal and clasp); from April, 1866, was employed under the govt. of the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn., supt. of vaccn., and sanitary commr.; dep. surgn.-gen., Central Prov., May, 1889; inspr.-general of civil hosps., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1890; in 1892 acted as surgn.-gen. and sanitary commr. with the govt. of India; ret. d., May, 1894.

RICHARDSON, THOMAS WILLIAM, Indian

C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Brighton Coll., Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., gen., rev., and statistical depts., March, 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1895; registrar, high court, appellate side, Dec., 1895.

RICHARDSON, WILLIAM PELHAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 8th Oct., 1868, and employed on irrigation works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; ret. d.

RICHEY, SIR JAMES BELLETT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 7th Dec., 1856, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., educt. inspr., asst. rev. commr., offg. under sec. to govt., talukdari settlt. officer, and asst. to the agent and special commr. at Baroda; junr. collr. and mag., Jan., 1879; senr. collr., May, 1880; on special duty at Kharaghoda, 1883 and 1884, and at Baroda, 1884; acting commr. of customs, March, 1884; acting member of council, Bombay, March, 1885, and again, Oct., 1886; chief sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., May, 1885; confd. as member of council, Bombay, Aug., 1887; ret. d., March, 1890; K.C.I.E., May, 1890.

RICKETTS, GEORGE H. M., C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and Haileybury; apptd., 1847; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and as asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, S.W. frontier; from 1853, served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dept. commr., and from 1858 in the N.W. Prov. as mag. and collr., and civil and sessions judge; C.B. for service in the Punjab in 1857, and medal for service with the troops; commr., Allahabad divn., 1873; junr. member, board of rev., 1878; ret. d., 1879.

RICKETTS, MONTAGUE POYNTZ, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—Arrived 22nd March, 1855; served in the Central Prov. from Sept., 1859, as asst. commr.; small cause court judge, Jabalpur, Aug., 1862; dep. commr., May, 1867; addl. commr., Nov., 1883; ret. d., April, 1890.

RIDDELL, ROBERT VANITTART, Col., R.E.—Joined Addiscombe, Feb., 1858; lieut., royal engrs., 10th June, 1859; arrived, March, 1861; from Aug., 1862, served in the topogr. survey of India as mil. asst.; dep. supt., Oct., 1867; asst. surveyor-gen. Oct., 1876; apptd. mint master, Calcutta, Jan., 1884; mint master and mint engr., Bombay, Aug., 1880; ret. d., March, 1895.

RIDGEWAY, THE RIGHT HON. SIR JOSEPH WEST, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Col.—First commn., Bengal infantry, Dec., 1860; in civil employ from May, 1868, in the Central India and Rajputana agencies; attaché, foreign dept., 1873; asst. agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1875; pol. agent, E. states, Rajputana, Aug., 1876; on special pol. duty with the field force in Afghanistan, under Gen. Sir F. Roberts, 1880 (medal with clasp, bronze star); from Sept., 1880, served in the secretariat of the foreign dept., and was apptd. asst. sec., Nov., 1880, and junr. under sec., Dec., 1881; from Aug., 1884, served as asst. with the commn. for the delimitation of the Afghan frontier, and was apptd. H.M.'s commr. for this purpose, Sept., 1885; subsequently was H.M.'s commr. for same purpose at St. Petersburg; returned to Europe, 1887, and was apptd. under sec. for Ireland; ret. d. from the army, Aug., 1889; K.C.B., Jan., 1891; envoy to Morocco,

Jan., 1893; lieut.-govr. of the Isle of Man, Nov., 1893; apptd. govr. of Ceylon, Sept., 1895.

RIEU, JEAN LOUIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

RIGG, ARTHUR EDMUND, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 15th Dec., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

RIGG, HENRY, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th June, 1862, and arrived, 7th Dec., 1862; asst. engr., Feb., 1864; served in various divns. in Madras, and was apptd. exec. engr., March, 1868; on special duty, Upper Burma, 1885-86 (medal and clasps); manager and engr. in-chief, Burma state railway, June, 1889; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for railways, central divn., May, 1892; chief engr., Aug., 1892; consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, Jan., 1896.

RIGHY, VANS, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., Feb., 1856; exec. engr., March, 1871; from Jan., 1875, was employed on Tirhut state railway, and was apptd. manager and supt. of way and works, Tirhut and Patna Gya state railways, March, 1880; transf. to superior rev. establ., Oct., 1882; supt. of way and works, N.W. railways, 1886; ret. d., Dec., 1891.

RILEY, HENRY JAMES, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd April, 1882.

RIPON (MARQUESS OF), THE RIGHT HON. GEORGE FREDERICK SAMUEL ROBINSON, K.G., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., P.C., D.C.L.—Born 24th Oct., 1827; attaché to a special mission to Brussels, 1849; sat in the house of commons (as Viscount Goderich) as member for Hull, 1852-53, for Huddersfield, 1853-57, and for the W. Riding of Yorkshire, 1857-59, in which year he succeeded to the upper house; under sec. for war, June, 1859; prest. of the royal geographical society, 1860; under sec. for India, Jan.-July, 1861; sworn a privy councillor, April, 1863; lord high steward of Hull, Oct., 1863; sec. for war, April, 1863, to Feb., 1866; sec. of state for India, Feb.-July, 1866; lord prest. of the council, Dec., 1868, to Aug., 1873; K.G., 1869; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1870; chairman of the joint high commn. which arranged the treaty of Washington, 1871; created Marquess of Ripon, 1871; viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, June, 1880, to Dec., 1884; was a royal commr. for the London exhibn. of 1886; first lord of the admiralty, Feb.-Aug., 1886; sec. of state for the colonies, Aug., 1892, to July, 1895.

RISLEY, HERBERT HOPE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 24th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1877; under sec., rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1878; offg. under sec., govt. of India, some dept., Feb., 1879; asst. commr., Jan., 1882; supt. of survey of Ghatwali and other service tenures in Manbhum, Feb., 1884; employed on the special duty of compiling statistics relating to the castes and occupations of the people of Bengal, Jan., 1885; dep. commr., April, 1886; sec., police commn., 1890; mag. and collr., May,

1891; sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. and municipal depts., July, 1892; member of provl. legislative council, 1892-93, and from March, 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; author of 'Sikkim and Tibet,' 'Tribes and Castes of Bengal,' an 'Ethnographic Glossary,' and other works on ethnology.

RITCHIE, JOHN GERALD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 28th Oct., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1887; dep. commr., May, 1891; mag. and collr., Dec., 1891; chairman of Calcutta commrs., April, 1895.

RITCHIE, RICHMOND THACKERAY WILLOUGHBY, India Office.—Educ. at Eton, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 18th May, 1877; rev., statistics and commerce dept., 1881; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, 1883-92; senr. clerk, Oct., 1892; private sec. to permanent under sec., Oct., 1892, to Feb., 1894; sec. to royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895, but resigned on appt. as private sec. to sec. of state (Lord George Hamilton), July, 1895.

RIVAZ, CHARLES MONTGOMERY, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Blackheath Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and offg. under sec. and sec. to govt.; supt., Kapurthala state, April, 1877; dep. commr., April, 1885; sec. to Punjab govt., May, 1887; commr. and supt., Nov., 1888; 2nd finl. commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., May, 1895; addl. member of legislative council of gov. gen., May, 1896-Oct., 1896.

RIVAZ, GEORGE WILMOT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Blackheath Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 29th Oct., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; registrar, chief court, Punjab, 1877-84; district judge, Aug., 1886; divl. judge, March, 1891; edited the 2nd edn. of the circular orders of the chief court.

RIVETT-CARNAC, CHARLES JAMES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., 21st May, 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. and the Punjab; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Oct., 1882; asst. compt.-gen. (forest branch), Oct., 1884; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1885; compt., Central Prov., Nov., 1891; transf. to Burma, Nov., 1895.

RIVETT-CARNAC, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 12th Nov., 1858, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Sept., 1861; asst. sec. to the chief commr., Central Prov., June, 1862; settl. officer, Chanda and Wardha, Aug., 1864; cotton commr., Central Prov., Sept., 1866, and Berars, April, 1867; commr. of cotton and commerce with the govt. of India, Dec., 1871; member and hon. sec. of central committee of international exhibns.; on duty in England, 1872-73; special commr. of transport, Bengal famine; opium agent, Benares, March, 1875; ret. d., Feb., 1894; author of reports on Indian cotton cultivation and trade, and on the interchange of traffic on Indian railways, &c.

RIX, HARRY SIDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S., (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and

served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

ROBARTS, HARRY OCTAVIUS WARWICK, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 15th Dec., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1896.

ROBB, JOHN, M.D., C.M., Surgn.-Major, late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 1st Oct., 1868; from Nov., 1873, served as civil surgn., Zanzibar, and acted as asst. pol. agent in 1874-75 and 1881; from July, 1882, served under the govt. of Bombay, as civil surgn., jail supt., &c., at Aden, Surat, and Ahmadabad; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

ROBERTS, ALLAN SCOTT, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Infantry, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 2nd April, 1862; from June, 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1894.

ROBERTS, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Col., Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—Cornet, Bengal cavalry, Feb., 1859; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, 1864; cantonmt. mag., Morar, Feb., 1875; asst. commr., Merwara, April, 1881; pol. asst., Sujangarh, Aug., 1881; in 1881-82 was employed in operations against the rebellious Bikanir thakurs, and received the surrender of the fort of Nahargarh; asst. pol. agent, Banswara and Partabgarh, Aug., 1883; pol. agent and supt., Charkhari, Sept., 1884; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Dec., 1885; pol. agent, Jacobabad, Dec., 1887; Khelat, March, 1888; Alwar, May, 1888; asst. agent, Mount Abu, Nov., 1888; pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1889; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, Sept., 1889.

ROBERTS, CHARLES HAMILTON, Kumaun Commn.—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1874, and served as extra asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag.; dep. collr., Jan., 1887; on special duty in Garhwal, April, 1890; supt., Tarai, Sept., 1890; dep. commr., Naini Tal, Oct., 1891.

ROBERTS, DAVID THOMAS, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Liverpool, Liverpool Inst. and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1870; dep. commr., Dec., 1886; mag. and collr., June, 1887; commr., April, 1894; author of 'Ballia Gazetteer' (in part), and of the Ballia settlt. report.

ROBERTS (BARON), RIGHT HON. FREDERICK SLEIGH, V.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., Field-Marshal, R.A.—First commn., Bengal artillery, 1851; during the Mutiny served at the siege and capture of Delhi, &c. (medal and three clasps, V.C.); dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, April, 1858; served in the N.W. frontier expedn., 1863 (medal and clasp); asst. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, Feb., 1863; served with the Abyssinian expedn., 1868 (medal), and with the Lushai expedn., 1871-72; dep. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1872; quartermaster-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1875; during the operations in Afghanistan in 1878-9-80, commanded the Kuram valley field force, and subsequently the Kabul field force at the occupation of Kabul and battle of Kharasiab, and in the march to the relief of Kandahar (medal with

four clasps, bronze star, thanks of both houses of parliament, created a bart., K.C.B., G.C.B.); commander-in-chief, Madras, and member of govr.'s council, Nov., 1881; commander-in-chief, in India, and member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Nov., 1885, to April, 1893; created a peer, Jan., 1892; G.C.S.I., June, 1893; field-marshal, May, 1895; commander-in-chief in Ireland, Oct., 1895.

ROBERTS, SYDNEY GORDON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 27th Nov., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

ROBERTS, THOMAS, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., jud. asst., and dep. commr.; addl. commr., Jalandhar, Aug., 1884; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; ret'd., April, 1891.

ROBERTS, WILLIAM HOWLAND, M.D., Surgn.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 23rd July, 1858; arrived, 24th Sept., 1858, and served in the Central Prov. till Sept., 1864, and subsequently in Madras as civil surgn., supt. of jail, &c.; was also in charge of meteorological observatories at Coimbatore and Salem; reverted to mil. duty, Sept., 1885; ret'd., July, 1893.

ROBERTSON, BENJAMIN, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at the Inst., Elgin, the Gr. Sch. and Univ. of Aberdeen, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 28th Nov., 1885, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; transf. to Central Prov. as provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890; asst. commr., Oct., 1892; commr. of excise, Oct., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1894.

ROBERTSON, CHARLES, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., 4th Aug., 1856; arrived, 17th Dec., 1856; served under the mil. dept. and also as zillah surgn., Masulipatam (1862-65); from May, 1874, served as zillah surgn., and supt. of jail, Cuddalore; ret'd., Dec., 1885.

ROBERTSON, DONALD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, royal Scots fusiliers, Aug., 1865; arrived, Oct., 1865; served from Nov., 1869, as asstt. commr. in the Central Prov.; transf. to Rajputana, June, 1872; cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, May, 1877; offg. asst. commr., Merwara and Ajmir, 1877-78; offg. supt., Jhalawar, 1879; 1st asst. agent, Indore, 1881; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, May, 1885; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1886; pol. agent in Baghelkhand, and supt. of the Rewa state, Dec., 1888; rest., Gwalior, Feb., 1894; offg. govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, April, 1895.

ROBERTSON, ELPHINSTONE POURTALES, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 27th March, 1851, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., dep. commr. of customs, salt, and opium, and sub-collr. and joint mag.; senr. collr. and mag., Dharwar, Jan., 1873; rev. and police commr., Nov., 1876; commr. of the central divn., Jan., 1878; ret'd., 1886.

ROBERTSON, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; special duty,

Maler Kotla settl. operations, Nov., 1883; dir. of land records and agric., Dec., 1889; author of 'Customary Law of Rawalpindi District,' and of reports on the revised settl. and forest settl. of that district.

ROBERTSON, SIR GEORGE SCOTT, K.C.S.I., Surgn.-Major, Indian Med. Service.—Educ. at Westminster Hosp.; joined the service, 30th March, 1878; served through the Afghan war, 1879-80; civil surgn., N.W. Prov., 1880-88; on depn. to Gilgit, June, 1888; agency surgn., Gilgit, July, 1889; on special duty in Kafiristan, 1890-91; chief pol. officer, Hunza-Nagar expedn., 1891; C.S.I., May, 1892; offg. agent, Gilgit, May-Oct., 1892; mission to Gor and capture of Chilas, 1892; in charge, Chitral mission, 1893; agent, Gilgit, Jan., 1894; K.C.S.I., July, 1895, for his defence of Chitral fort.

ROBERTSON, GRAHAM, Bengal Marine Dept.—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1891; 2nd marine surveyor, Calcutta port, Nov., 1892; 1st engr. and shipwright surveyor, June, 1895.

ROBERTSON, JAMES CASAMAJOR, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1854; arrived, 31st Jan., 1855, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt., Dehra Dun, small cause court judge, and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Allahabad, Oct., 1871; offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn., and commr. of excise and stamps, July, 1876; civil and sessions judge, April, 1879; commr., Jan., 1882; offd. as member of the board of rev. on various occasions; ret'd., Jan., 1890.

ROBERTSON, JOHN HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Sir Roger Cholmeley's Sch., Highgate, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 29th Nov., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

ROBERTSON, LAURENCE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM, Funds Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India audit office, 27th Feb., 1886; transf. to funds dept., India office, 3rd Dec., 1894.

ROBERTSON, WILLIAM ROWNTREE, M.R.A.C., late Madras Educn. Dept.—Arrived, 21st Nov., 1868; supt., govt. farms, Nov., 1868; supt. of the sch. of agric., Saidapet, Oct., 1876; fellow of Madras univ., May, 1882; supt., govt. farms and sch. of agric., and agricl. reporter to govt. of Madras, Nov., 1882; principal, agricl. coll., Saidapet, April, 1884; ret'd., April, 1889.

ROBINSON, ALEXANDER, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., April, 1887; ret'd., Sept., 1892; author of 'Memoirs of the Ballia and Jaunpur Districts.'

ROBINSON, WILLIAM, B.A., Mil. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Carmichael Sch., Queen's Coll., Cork, and the Royal Univ. of Ireland; junr. clerk, 11th June, 1894; rest. clerk, Dec., 1894.

ROBSON, SAMUEL, M.A., Bengal Educ. Dept.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the dept., 20th Dec., 1878, as profir., presidency coll.; transf. to Patna coll., June, 1880; and to Hugli coll., Jan., 1883; again profir., Patnacoll., Aug., 1888.

ROE, SIR CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., Nov., 1878; addl. commr., Lahore and Rawal Pindi divns., May, 1883; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; addl. judge, chief court, Nov., 1888; judge, April, 1889; chief judge, Dec., 1895; Kt., Jan., 1897; author of 'Customary Law of the Multan District,' and of three settl. reports.

ROE, FRANCIS REGINALD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 8th Nov., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ROE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER CRAWFORD, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; in mil. employ till 22nd March, 1878, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgn., Jalandhar; in 1879 served with the Kuram valley field force; confid. as civil surgn., Sept., 1880; med. adviser to Maharaja of Kapurthala, April to Oct., 1885; sanitary commr., Punjab, May, 1894.

ROGERS, ALEXANDER, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1845; arrived, 9th Nov., 1845; served in the Bombay rev. and jud. dept., and was apptd. 1st asst. collr. and mag., Broach, Oct., 1851, and afterwards at Ahmadabad, Oct., 1852; settl. officer, Gujarat, Nov., 1852; collr. and mag., Ahmadabad, March, 1860, and afterwards at Surat; rev. and police commr., N. divn., April, 1865; member of council, Bombay, June, 1872; ret'd. in 1879; author of a transl. of three modern Persian plays, a 'History of the Land Rev. Settlt. of Bombay,' and a transl. of 'Yusuf and Zuleika'; has also edited the 'Bostan' of Sa'adi.

ROGERS, ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 25th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.

ROGERS, HENRY THOMAS, Major-Gen., R.E., late Madras Rev. Survey Dept.—Second lieut., Dec., 1849; arrived, 28th Aug., 1852; served in the public works dept., Bengal, 1853-59, and subsequently in Madras as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; fellow of the univ. of Madras, 1872; principal, civil engrg. coll., April, 1871, to Jan., 1880; from Nov., 1879, served as supt. of rev. survey, Madras; ret'd., July, 1886.

ROGERS, MALCOLM WILLIAM, Col., R.E., Survey of India Dept.—Lieut., royal engrs., Dec., 1861; arrived, Oct., 1865; asst. surveyor, great trigonl. survey, July, 1866; served with the Quetta field force as survey officer, 1878-79 (medal); dep. supt. of survey, July, 1882; asst. surveyor-gen., May, 1884, to Sept., 1887; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; again asst. surveyor-gen. in charge of surveyor-gen.'s office and mathematical instrument office, from May, 1889.

ROGERS-HARRISON, AUGUSTUS NAPOLEON, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1879, and was employed under the mil. dept. until Dec., 1880, when he was apptd. civil surgn. and supt. of jail and lunatic asylum, Vizagapatam; civil surgn., Salem, June, 1887; district med. and sanitary officer, Oct., 1895.

ROLLAND, ALEXANDER TULLOCH, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras in-

fantry, April, 1856; served in the dept. from Dec., 1862, as asst. supt., and supt.; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Feb., 1885; acted as commr. of police, Madras city, in 1889; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1893.

ROLLO, JAMES THEODORE, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 7th Oct., 1870, and posted to Rajputana; transfld. to Assam, June, 1880; exec. engr., May, 1881; transfld. to Burma, June, 1892.

ROMESH CHUNDER DUTT, C.I.E., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Colootola Branch Sch., Calcutta, Presidency Coll., Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 28th Sept., 1871; served in rev. and jud. depts., Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., March, 1888; acted as commr., Bardwan, 1894-95; member of provl. legisl. council, Jan.-Oct., 1895; has publ. 'Three Years in Europe,' 1872; 'Peasantry of Bengal,' 1876; 'Literature of Bengal,' 1877; a 'History of Civilisation in Ancient India,' 1888-89; a 'Brief History of Ancient and Modern India,' 1891; 'Laws of Ancient India,' 1894; &c., &c.

ROMESH CHUNDER MITTER, SIR, B.L.—Puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 30th March, 1874; acted as chief justice in 1882; retd., 1890; member of the govt.-gen.'s legisl. council to Aug., 1891.

RONSON, WILLIAM TOMLINSON, Rev. and Statistics Dept., India Office.—Writer, 1st Sept., 1873; clerk, 2nd class, Feb., 1879; junr. clerk, Feb., 1894.

ROPER, CAMPBELL ROWLEY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ROSE, BENJAMIN JAMES, Rev. and Statistics Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Williams' Gr. Sch., Clapham, and the Birkbeck Instn.; entered the India office, 23rd Feb., 1871; technical asst. in the rev. and statistics dept., June, 1882; also clerk to the special asst., 1891; awarded gold medal at Amsterdam exhibn. for statistical diagrams shewing the condition and progress of British India; prepared abstract catalogue of the Indian section at the same exhibn.; also reports on wheat and Madras tobacco.

ROSE, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Folkingham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 7th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; offg. commr., Gorakhpur, April-Oct., 1894; and at Agra, Jan., 1895.

ROSE, FRANCIS ERNEST, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Sept., 1865, and posted to Central Prov.; transfld. to the Punjab, Nov., 1866; exec. engr., Jan., 1877.

ROSE, GEORGE PRINGLE, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1877; posted to state railways, Nov., 1878; exec. engr., Feb., 1891; C.I.E., 1892; lent to Nizam's govt., April, 1895.

ROSE, HORACE ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 4th Oct., 1888, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ROSS, ALEXANDER, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Acad. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. from Haileybury, 1836; served in the jud. and rev. depts. of the N.W. Prov., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1853; special commr., Farakhabad, Dec., 1857; civil and sessions judge, 1858; commr. of rev. and police, Agra divn., May, 1859; judge of the sadar court, N.W. Prov., 1862; judge, high court, N.W. Prov., 1866; retd., Oct., 1871.

ROSS, ALLAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Jan., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ROSS, DAVID (Burma Commn.).—Joined the service, March, 1886, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ROSS, SIR EDWARD CHARLES, Kt., C.S.I., Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 3rd European regt., Bombay, May, 1855; served in Central India during the Mutiny (medal and clasp); asst. to pol. agent, Muscat, 1863; asst. pol. agent, Gwadar, 1869; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, April, 1871; pol. rest., Persian Gulf, Oct., 1872; also consul-gen. for Fars and the Persian Gulf, Nov., 1878; offg. rest., Haidarabad, April-Oct., 1886; reverted to mil. dept., 1891; Kt., Jan., 1892; author of several reports and memoranda on subjects connected with the Persian Gulf.

ROSS, GEORGE CUMBERLAND, Surgn.-Col., Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866; served from Nov., 1867, as civil surgn. in various stations in the Punjab; administrative med. officer and sanitary commr., Central Prov., Jan., 1894; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Bengal, March, 1895.

ROSS, HENRY THOMAS, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Perth Acad., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; special asst., Malabar, Sept., 1884; acting sub-sec. board of rev., Aug., 1885; registrar, high court, Feb., 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1888; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1889.

ROSS, HERCULES GREY, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 16th Nov., 1856; from April, 1858, served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and city mag. at Lucknow; supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1869; mag. and collr., Benares, Oct., 1882; settlt. officer, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1883; commr., Kumaon, April, 1885; retd., Nov., 1888.

ROSS, WILLIAM.—Apptd. supt. of govt. printing, India, 1st May, 1891.

ROSSITER, CHARLES ARTHUR, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

ROUSE, ROBERT, Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1854; from May, 1869, served as civil surgn. and offg. supt. of jail in the Punjab; retd., Sept., 1885.

ROUTH, REGINALD SAMUEL JOHN, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; appt. 1st October, 1876, and posted to State Railways as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1889.

ROWE, FREDERICK JAMES, M.A., Educn. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 15th June, 1870, and served as profir. at Krishnagar, Dacca,

and Hugli colls., also as inspr. of schs.; profir., presidency coll., July, 1880; joint author of various eduel. works, &c.

ROWE, JOSIAH EBENEZER, late Punjab Commn.—Joined the service, 20th March, 1858, and served in the Punjab in various capacities; extra asst. commr., Oct., 1865; small cause court judge in 1874, 1875, and 1881-84; extra jud. asst., Nov., 1884; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

ROWLANDS, PERCY JONES, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—East India house writer, old establ., 1st Sept., 1853; asst. to registrar, June, 1877.

ROXBURGH, JAMES, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 30th Dec., 1895.

ROY, MADHUB CHUNDER, B.A., B.C.E., M.I.C.E., Rai Bahadur, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 16th Sept., 1862, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1872; ret'd., 1894.

ROYLE, JOSEPH R. E. J., C.I.E.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; entered Indian army from Addiscombe, 7th June, 1861; ret'd., Jan., 1867; on the extra staff of the India museum, 1st Jan., 1867; sec., Indian commn. for Vienna exhibn., 1873; supt., India museum, 1874; clerk to the special asst. in the statistics and commerce dept., India office, 1879-91; exec. commr. for govt. of India, Amsterdam exhibn., 1883; asst. sec. for India to royal commn. for the Colonial and Indian exhibn., 1886, and official agent for the govt. of India; C.I.E., 1886; curator of Indian section, Imperial Inst., 1891; author of the official reports on the Indian sections of the Amsterdam and Colonial and Indian exhibns.; also of a lecture before the Society of Arts, June, 1882, on the 'Production of Tea, Cinchona, Wild Silks, and Rhea in India.'

RUNDALL, FRANCIS HORNBLOW, C.S.I., Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Madras engrs., 1841; asst. civil engr., 1844; civil engr., public works dept., Ganjam, 1854; district engr., Rajamahendri, May, 1855; consulting engr., Madras irrign. and canal co., Oct., 1859; chief engr. of irrign. and joint sec. to govt. of Bengal, July, 1867; chief engr., 1st class, Aug., 1869; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1872, to April, 1874; C.S.I., Dec., 1875.

RUNDALL, JAMES WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Joined the dept. as supernumerary engr., 2nd Aug., 1855; exec. engr., July, 1863; supt. of works, Kistna, Aug., 1880; suptg. engr., Oct., 1881; ret'd., 1889.

RUSSELL, ALEXANDER SHERWOOD, Madras Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1875; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt. irrign. branch, in 1882; exec. engr., April, 1886.

RUSSELL, CHARLES LENNOX SOMERVILLE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

RUSSELL, EDGAR GEER, M.B., B.Sc., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and served on gen. and mil. duty, and in various offg. appts.; civil surgn., Patna, March, 1890; Darjiling, April, 1892; Dacca, March, 1894; profir., Calcutta med. coll., Sept., 1896; author of a work on 'Malaria and Injuries of Spleen.'

RUSSELL, SIR EDWARD LECHMERE, K.C.S.I.,

Gen.—Ensign, 12th native infantry, Bombay, Jan., 1838; served in the Afghan war of 1842, and the Sind campaign of 1842-43; fort adjutant, Bombay, Jan., 1855; remount agent, May, 1857; commanding brigade, Disa, March, 1863, and subsequently at Bombay; pol. rest. and commanding brigade, at Aden, Dec., 1867; commanding northern divn., Bombay, 1870-76; placed on unemployed list, July, 1881.

RUSSELL, GEORGE WILLIAM ERSKINE.—Educ. at Harrow, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; M.P., Aylesbury, 1880-85; parly. sec. to local govt. board, 1883-85; alderman of the county of London, Feb., 1889; M.P. for N. Bedfordshire, 1892-95; under sec. of state for India, Aug., 1892; under sec. of state, home dept., March, 1894, to July, 1895; author of a 'Life of Mr. Gladstone,' and editor of 'The Letters of Matthew Arnold.'

RUSSELL, ROBERT VANE, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 5th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

RUSSELL, SAMUEL, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Santry Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; special asst., Ganjam, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1890; district and sessions judge, Chengalpat, Feb., 1893.

RUSTAMJI DHANJIBHAI SETHNA, B.A., LL.B., B.L., Bombay High Court.—Joined the service, 27th March, 1886, as acting profir., Bombay law sch.; confd., Feb., 1889; registrar of the high court, appellate side, July, 1895.

RUTHERFORD, DAVID, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, July, 1867, as proxy. asst. engr.; exec. engr., Feb., 1873.

RUTHERFÜRD, JOHN MURIC, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained in Europe for the telegraph dept., and apptd. asst. supt., Oct., 1869; arrived, 8th Jan., 1870, and served in the Punjab, and in Rajputana; transfed. to state railways, March, 1880, and served as district traffic supt. on the Rajputana state railway; Indus valley state railway, March, 1882; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, July, 1886; traffic supt., Oct., 1888; ret'd., 1891.

RUTHERFÜRD, JAMES, Col., late Mysore Rev. Survey.—First commn., ensign, 4th Aug., 1859; joined the Mysore rev. survey dept., Jan., 1865; dep. supt., April, 1881; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1892.

RYLAND, WILLIAM HENRY, late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—From 10th Dec., 1852, served in the subord. exec. service, Bengal; offg. collr. of stamps, and supt. of abkari rev., March, 1868; offg. asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1869; from Dec., 1874, served in the jud. dept. and was apptd. judge of the small cause court at Sealdah, April, 1875; offg. supt. of stamps and stationery, April, 1880; ret'd., April, 1887.

RYVES, ROBERT HUGH, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and acting under sec.

RYVES, THOMAS JAMES, Local Capt., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Served as a volunteer in the Mutiny (medal); adjutant of mil. police, Bijnaur, 1859; asst. inspr.-gen., Rohilkhand,

April, 1861; from Aug., 1861, served as supt. of police in various districts; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, June, 1888; dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1895.

SABONADIERE, ALFRED, Indian C.S., N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Jan., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1893.

ST. CLAIR, THE HON. LOCKHART MATHEW, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Central Prov.; exec. engr., Jan., 1884; services lent to Nepal state, 1889-92; transf. to Assam, April, 1894.

ST. QUINTIN, WILLIAM ASTELL, India Audit Office.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; temporary clerk, 31st Dec., 1866; junr. examr., Dec., 1868; senr. examr., Nov., 1878; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892.
