Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; asst. commr., Nov., 1884; joint mag., March, 1888; settlt. officer in addn., March, 1890; dep. commr., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891; mag. and collr., May, 1894.

WYLIE, HENRY, C.S.I., Col., Foreign Dept

Govt. of India.—Ensign, Oct., 1861; arrived, 15th Nov., 1861; and took part in the N.W. frontier campaign, 1863, the 2nd Yusufzai expdn. (medal and clasp), the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (clasp), the Abyssinian expdn., 1868 (medal), and the Hazara campaign, 1868 (clasp); on special duty in Baluchistan, with Major R. Sandeman, 1875-77; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, 1877; served in the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); C.S.I., Feb., 1881; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, 1882; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; Jhalawar, April, 1885; Bhopal, March, 1887; rest., Nepal, Oct., 1893.

WYLLIE, WILLIAM HUTT CURZON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 106th Foot, Oct., 1866; arrived, Feb., 1867; served in Oudh, 1870-79, as asst. commr.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1875, and as junr. sec. to govt., 1876-77; from Jan., 1879, served under the foreign dept., as cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, asst. commr. in Merwara and Ajmir, and as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; served in Madras, 1880-81, as mil. sec. to the govr., and acted as private sec.; C.I.E., May, 1881; pol. agent, in charge of ex-Gaekwar of Baroda, Mulhar Rao, 1881; asst. rest., Haidarabad, 1882; asst. commr., Ajmir, Nov., 1883; 1st asst. agent, Rajputana, 1884; addl. pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1885; boundary settl. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Kotah, Jan., 1889; offg. commr., Ajmir, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, Jhalawar, in 1891 and 1892; offg. rest., states of Rajputana, May, 1893; rest., Mewar, March, 1894.

WYNCH, LIONEL MALING, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to land rev. commrs., Feb., 1894.

WYNDHAM, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 13th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1894.

WYNNE, SYDENHAM HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 21st Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. to the collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Oct., 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; collr. and mag., Jan., 1890.

YATE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.M.G., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settl. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in

connection with the Afghan boundary commn. pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhob, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' &c.

YELD, HORACE PARR, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1874; was employed in the mil. dept. up to Aug., 1884, when he was apptd. prob. in the assay dept., Bombay mint; transf. to Calcutta mint, Sept., 1884; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1886, and Calcutta mint, Jan., 1888; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1892; retd., Nov., 1894.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 22nd May, 1888.

YOUNG, GEORGE GORDON, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 23rd Oct., 1856; from June, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; dep. commr., Jan., 1873; commr. and supt., Jan., 1881; offg. 2nd finl. commr., 1888 and 1890; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

YOUNG, HARRY GAVIN, Madras Police Dept.—Entered the service, April, 1874, and served as inspr., asst. supt., and supt. of police; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1895.

YOUNG, JAMES, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., July, 1879.

YOUNG, MATTHEW, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, C.S.I., B.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Heidelberg Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 16th Dec., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; civil and sessions judge, Oct., 1880; jud. commr., Oudh, Aug., 1886; acting judge of high court, April-Dec., 1890; C.S.I., Jan., 1891; retd., Dec., 1891.

YOUNG, WILLIAM MACKWORTH, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settl. officer, and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., Oct., 1873; settl. sec. to finl. commr., March, 1875; dep. commr., Jan., 1878; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Feb., 1878; sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1880; commr. and supt., May, 1887; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1888; commr., Jalandhar divn., April, 1888; 2nd finl. commr. of the Punjab, April, 1889; 1st commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June-Nov., 1893; prest. of the Indian hemp drugs commn., Oct., 1893; rest., Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Sept., 1895; nominated lieut.-govr. of the Punjab.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ARTHUR DELAVAL, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clifton Coll.; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1875; arrived, 9th Dec., 1877, and served as asst. collr. and mag.,

talukdari settlt. officer, and asst. pol. agent; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1890; and as administrator, Rajpipla state, in 1888 and 1890-91; transfld. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Nov., 1893.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ROMER EDWARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 14th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., March, 1886; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888, to April,

1890; offg. junr. sec., May, 1893; dep. commr., Sept., 1894.

YUSUF, MOHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the Patna Coll. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. mag. and collr., Jan., 1896.

ZORAB, JOHN MANOOK, M.B., C.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the mil. dept., and as civil surgn., Balasor, March, 1887, Cuttack, March, 1891, and Bhagalpur, April, 1896.

LIST OF CASUALTIES.

In addition to those mentioned in the casualty lists given in the earlier part of the book, the following officers have died since the last issue.

BEARD, H. G., Store Dept., India Office.—
Died, 5th June, 1896.		 HUMFREY, Lieut.-Col. J., Bombay Police
Dept.—Died, 13th May, 1896.
BOILEAU, Lieut.-Col. H., I.S.C., Bengal Non-
regulation commn.—Died, 19th Feb., 1896.		 McCONNELL, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col. J. F. P.,
Bengal Med. Service.—Died, 24th Aug., 1896.
BOULTON, Major-Gen. C. F., I.S.C., late Sind
commn.—Died, Nov., 1896.		 MASTERS, Major E. S., I.S.C., Foreign Dept.,
Govt. of India.—Died, 20th July, 1896.
BROWNE, Major-Gen. Sir J., K.C.S.I., C.B.,
Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Died, 13th
June, 1896.		 MOORE, Surgn.-Gen. Sir W. J., K.C.I.E., late
Bombay Med. Dept.—Died, 8th Sept., 1896.
CAMERON, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col. A.,
M.D., late Indian Med. Dept.—Died, 1st Aug.,
1896.		 NUTT, Col. H. L., I.S.C., Bombay Pol. Dept.—
Died, 1st Dec., 1896.
EDWARDS, Commr. W. M., R.I.M., late Port
Officer, Karachi.—Died, 14th Aug., 1895.		 OMMANNEY, Major-Gen. E.L., C.S.I., I.S.C.,
late Punjab Commn.—Died, 3rd Nov., 1896.
FORBES, Col. W. E., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.
—Died, 10th May, 1896.		 REYNOLDS, Col. E. S., I.S.C., late Foreign
Dept., Govt. of India.—Died, 12th Nov., 1896.
FOSTER, Gen. Sir C. J., K.C.B., late Member
of Council of India—Died, 11th Feb., 1896.		 SMITH, Lieut.-Col. S., R.E., India Public Works
Dept.—Died, 13th March, 1896.
HOME, Col. R., C.I.E., R.E., late India Public
Works Dept.—Died, 8th July, 1896.		 STUART, Sir R., Kt., Q.C., late High Court,
N.W. Prov.—Died, 26th Aug., 1896.
HUME, Col. W. W., I.S.C., late Bengal Police.
—Died, 8th June, 1895.		 TREVOR, Major-Gen. J. S., C.S.I., late R.E.—
Died, 9th June, 1896.
WALKER, Gen. J. T., C.B., F.R.S., R.E., late
Survey of India Dept.—Died, 16th Feb., 1896.

353

Kishengarh, 139

Nawab Sidi, of Janjira, 140

Aitchison, Sir C. U., 22, 116, 117, 128,
129
Aitchison, J. E. T., 141

Albany, H.R.H. Duchess of, 145

Aldworth, St. L. B., 40

Algie, W., 28, 354

Ali Akbar, 93
Ali Kadir Sayid Husan Ali, Nawab,
of Murshidabad, 139
Alison, Sir A., 1, 112, 354
Allen, B., 16, 49, 354
Allen, B. C., 15, 42, 43, 354
Allen, C. E., 30
Allen, C. G. H., 12, 49, 354
Allen, D. B., 10, 48, 354

Allen, G. W., 140
Allen, H. J., 81
Allen, H. V., 56
Allen, J., 120
Allen, J. J., 30, 355

Allen, P. U., 13, 59, 355

Allison, H., 83, 355
Allsop, J. G. W., 68

Amar Singh, Raja, of Kashmir, 136
Ambica Charan Sen, 17, 49, 355
Ameer Hossein, Syed, 142, 355
Amherst, Lord, 112

Bahadur, 7, 142

An, 39

Bahadur, 7

65, 356

Anderson, J. C., 19
Anderson, J. D., 11, 48, 49, 355
Anderson, J. S., 19
Anderson, J. W. M., 356
Anderson, J. W. T., 94, 93
Anderson, K. M., 36
Anderson, R. H., 11, 47, 356
Anderson, T., 102, 103
Anderson, W. C., 133
André, J. E., 89
Andrew, J., 72, 77, 80, 356
Andrews, F. H., 68
Andrews, J., 103
Andrews, J. L., 80
Andrews, O., 3
Andrews, W., 2, 356
